title: Actor-Critic Framework-Based on Optimal Tracking Strategy for Snake Robots with Reinforcement Learning Method
authors: Dongfang Li, Rong Hu, Yilong Zhang, Rob Law, Zhezhuang Xu, Suet To, Edmond Q. Wu, Limin Zhu
abstract: Series Snake robots possess strong adaptability for unstructured environments, but their trajectory tracking control is hindered by nonlinear dynamics and model uncertainties. This article proposes an optimal tracking control strategy based on an actor–critic reinforcement learning framework. The method integrates line-of-sight guidance with serpentine gait generation, while a neural network identification system approximates the solution of the Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman equation for unknown dynamics. Actor and critic networks are employed to update control policies and cost functions online, reducing dependence on precise models. Rigorous theoretical analysis proves that position and velocity errors achieve semi-global uniform ultimate boundedness. Both simulations and prototype experiments were conducted based on a servo-driven yaw-pitch linkage alternating series snake robot. The results verify that the proposed method can achieve accurate trajectory tracking, rapid convergence, and stable joint control, demonstrating its effectiveness and superiority compared to existing methods.

title: Leveraging Generative AI for Security Defense of Mobile Edge-Enabled Digital Twins: A Survey
authors: Yaoqi Yang, Geng Sun, Hongyang Du, Changyuan Zhao, Zehui Xiong, Dusit Niyato, Abbas Jamalipour, Zhu Han, Khaled B. Letaief
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Addressing rising security threats that hinder the deployment of mobile edge-enabled digital twins (MDTs), this paper investigates the transformative potential of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) for enhancing MDT security frameworks. We propose a comprehensive five-layer MDT architecture and analyze various malicious security threats across physical and digital twin spaces. Focusing on GAI’s unique capabilities in data quality and diversity, we explore the application of variational autoencoders, generative adversarial networks, generated diffusion models, and transformer-based models within different layers of the MDT architecture. We also provide a summary of performance metrics, evaluation methodologies, and implementation considerations, concluding with key future research directions aimed at realizing real-time and complex attack defense strategies. Our findings show that GAI can significantly improve security measures for MDT systems, effectively addressing critical challenges in this rapidly evolving field.

title: Attribute–Topology Cross-Frequency Aligned Graph Neural Networks for Homophilic and Heterophilic Graphs in Node Classification
authors: Yachao Yang, Yanfeng Sun, Jipeng Guo, Jinlu Wang, Shaofan Wang, Junbin Gao, Fujiao Ju, Baocai Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have excelled in handling graph-structured data, attracting significant research interest. However, two primary challenges have emerged: interference between topology and attributes distorting node representations, and the low-pass filtering nature of most GNNs leading to the oversight of valuable high-frequency information in graph signals. These issues are particularly pronounced in heterophilic graphs. To address these challenges, we propose attribute–topology cross-frequency aligned (ATCFA) GNNs. ATCFA combines low- and high-pass filters to capture both smooth and detailed representations from topological and attribute perspectives. It also enforces frequency-specific constraints to reduce noise and redundancy in each frequency band. The model can dynamically adjust the filtering ratios for both homophilic and heterophilic graphs. Crucially, ATCFA establishes dynamic associations between corresponding frequency components of topology and attribute, achieving systematic alignment and interactive fusion that explicitly mitigates interference and promotes complementary information utilization across domains. Extensive experiments on standard datasets show that ATCFA delivers higher classification accuracy than state-of-the-art methods, proving its capability to handle both homophilic and heterophilic graphs in node classification.

title: DMS: Throughput-Aware Distributed Multimodal Model Surgery on Embedded Devices
authors: Qixuan Cai, Xiulong Liu, Kaixuan Zhang, Xin Xie, Xinyu Tong, Keqiu Li
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) generates diverse sensory data, and aggregating this information can significantly improve the inference accuracy and robustness of deep learning models. As multimodal data often originates from distributed devices, efficiently deploying multimodal models on real devices with limited bandwidth has become a key topic. Though existing methods reduce the transmission delay by compressing the original data or segmenting the model, they ignore the heterogeneity of transmission throughput of distributed devices and the limited computing power of multiple embedded devices. This paper proposes <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</u>istributed <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</u>ultimodal model <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>urgery (DMS), the first model partitioning method for multimodal models on multiple distributed embedded devices. First, we propose a lightweight autoencoder with a tunable structure and a joint training method to compress the transmitted data; Second, we formulate the multimodal model partition as an optimization problem and propose an efficient model partitioning algorithm; Third, we build and test a real-world prototype system using three different datasets and models. The evaluation results for activity recognition indicate that DMS can reduce the inference delay by up to 58.33% while achieving a 0.22% higher accuracy than the best baseline method.

title: The Safety Frontier in LLMs: Risks, Defenses, and Open Challenges
authors: Ishika Goyal, Siva Sai, Aahana Gupta, Vinay Chamola
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, Claude, and LLaMA are rapidly transforming domains including healthcare, education, law, and finance. However, their increasing adoption in high-stakes applications raises serious safety concerns. Without sufficient safeguards, LLM-powered AI systems are vulnerable to threats such as prompt injection, training data poisoning, model theft, denial-of-service through prompt abuse, misinformation generation, adversarial examples, model inversion, data leakage, and insider misuse. These vulnerabilities can produce harmful, biased, or misleading outputs, compromise privacy, and undermine trust. To address these risks, researchers have proposed safety approaches that aim to constrain and guide model behaviour, proactively detect and resist attacks, and incorporate ethical alignment into model design. Together, these methods form a multi-layered defence that combines preventive, detective, and corrective measures to improve LLM robustness and trustworthiness. This paper presents a comprehensive review of key safety mechanisms for LLMs, categorizing them into guardrails, red teaming, adversarial training, alignment techniques, jailbreak detection and prevention, watermarking, and fuzzing. For each category, recent advances are summarized, strengths and limitations are assessed, and open challenges are identified to support safer and more reliable real-world deployment of LLMs.

title: Robust Beamforming for RSMA-Aided Bistatic Integrated Sensing and Communication in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Zhijie Lyu, Xuantao Lyu, Ye Wang, Rui Wang, Yi Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) technology enhances the next generation of wireless networks by introducing a new approach to secure communication. In this work, a novel rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA)-aided bistatic ISAC system is proposed to serve multiple users and to estimate the parameters of a moving target. The design of the beamforming matrix aims to both maximize weighted sum-rate (WSR) and minimize the Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for the target under a power budget constraint. In particular, we consider a scenario where a dual-functional radar communication (DFRC) waveform is emitted with a trade-off between WSR for multiple users and CRB for a target. Inspired by the robust downlink communication capabilities of RSMA, users exhibit improved performance compared to the space division multiple access based ISAC, as it effectively addresses multi-user interference by decoding it partially and treating the remaining interference as noise. In order to strike a balance between communication and sensing requirements, the system combines two metrics of DFRC through a coefficient. Additionally, methods such as weighted minimum mean square error, semidefinite relaxation, and successive convex approximation are employed to tackle the issue of non-convexity. The simulation results demonstrate the performance gain and effectiveness of the proposed architecture over the baseline scheme.

title: Continuous Measurement Methods for Transient Physiological Discomfort in VR Locomotion
authors: Tianren Luo, Pengxiang Wang, Shuting Chang, Ke Zhou, Nianlong Li, Yulong Bian, Xiaohui Tan, Qi Wang, Teng Han, Feng Tian

title: Label Hierarchy Transition: Delving into Class Hierarchies to Enhance Deep Classifiers
authors: Renzhen Wang, De Cai, Kaiwen Xiao, Xixi Jia, Xiao Han, Deyu Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Hierarchical classification aims to sort the object into a hierarchical structure of categories. For example, a bird can be categorized according to a three-level hierarchy of order, family, and species. Existing methods commonly address hierarchical classification by decoupling it into a series of multi-class classification tasks. However, such a multi-task learning strategy fails to fully exploit the correlation among various categories across different levels of the hierarchy. In this paper, we propose Label Hierarchy Transition (LHT), a unified probabilistic framework based on deep learning, to address the challenges of hierarchical classification. The LHT framework consists of a transition network and a confusion loss. The transition network focuses on explicitly learning the label hierarchy transition matrices, which has the potential to effectively encode the underlying correlations embedded within class hierarchies. The confusion loss encourages the classification network to learn correlations across different label hierarchies during training. The proposed framework can be readily adapted to any existing deep network with only minor modifications. We experiment with a series of public benchmark datasets for hierarchical classification problems, and the results demonstrate the superiority of our approach beyond current state-of-the-art methods. Furthermore, we extend our proposed LHT framework to the skin lesion diagnosis task and validate its great potential in computer-aided diagnosis. The code of our method is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/renzhenwang/label-hierarchy-transition</uri>.

title: Fault Detection of Coil Misalignment in Multi-Receiver Wireless Power Transfer Systems With the Estimated Mutual Inductance
authors: Mingi Kim, Minseok Kang, Chang-Yeob Chu, Youngseok Lee, Sunyeol Gwon, In Gwun Jang
abstract: In a multi-receiver wireless power transfer (WPT) system, mutual inductance is a key determinant of electrical performance. In small mobile-device applications, receivers (RXs) can be placed or moved arbitrarily on a single transmitter (TX) pad, making coil misalignment unavoidable; therefore, reliable fault detection is essential for stable operation. This study proposes a two-stage approach to estimate the full set of mutual inductances. In Stage 1, each receiver is sequentially activated with a fixed pulse width modulation duty cycle to estimate the TX–RX mutual inductance MTi and equivalent load resistance Req,i. In Stage 2, receiver pairs are activated under the same condition to estimate the RX–RX mutual inductance Mik. Then, misalignment faults can be detected by comparing the estimated mutual inductance values with predefined thresholds. A three-receiver prototype (10 mm airgap) validates accurate mutual inductance estimation and reliable fault detection.

title: Active RIS-Enabled Joint 6D UE Localization and Environment Mapping in mmWave MIMO Systems
authors: Hua Chen, Zelong Yi, Han Yan, Wei Liu, Songjie Yang, Gang Wang, Yuanwei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: The problem of six-dimensional (6D) user equipment (UE) localization (i.e., 3D position and 3D orientation) and environment mapping is studied in active RIS-aided millimeter-wave MIMO systems. By designing specific UE pilot sequences and RIS phase configurations, decoupling of the angles of departure (AODs) at the UE from the cascaded channel parameters is achieved, enabling separate estimation of the angles of arrival (AOAs) at the base station (BS), the AODs at the UE, and the cascaded channel parameters. To address the challenge of estimating the cascaded channel parameters, a low-complexity algorithm that decouples the 2D cascaded channel parameters is developed. In particular, the estimation process is first transformed into multiple 1D searches using greedy algorithm principles. Then, the spatial alternating generalized expectation maximization algorithm is applied to further refine the cascaded channel parameters. By formulating a weighted least squares problem, the estimation of cascaded channel parameters is effectively converted into the estimation of AOAs and AODs at the RIS. Finally, by pairing the AOAs and AODs from different paths, 6D UE position estimates and environmental mapping result are obtained. Simulation results are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed estimation algorithm.

title: Manufacturing, Sensing, and Applications of Data Gloves: A Review
authors: Songwen Shao, Mengde Li, Yueming Hu, Miao Li
abstract: Data gloves have emerged as essential wearable interfaces for capturing hand motion and enabling natural human-computer interaction. This review traces their evolution from early mechanical and optical systems to contemporary multimodal platforms that integrate advanced flexible materials and embedded intelligence. We analyze three functional dimensions: gesture and posture sensing through inertial, strain-based, and vision-assisted approaches; tactile perception combining mechanical, thermal, and optical modalities; and haptic feedback spanning vibrotactile, electrotactile, and pneumatic mechanisms. Beyond sensing principles, we examine hardware implementation challenges—including sensor integration, modular architecture, and power efficiency—as well as signal processing strategies for real-time recognition on embedded platforms. Applications in medical rehabilitation, virtual reality, and robotic teleoperation demonstrate the technology’s growing practical impact. Looking forward, we identify key challenges in standardization and computational efficiency, and highlight emerging directions such as self-powered systems, neuromorphic processing, and brain-computer interface integration that promise to advance data gloves toward more intuitive human-machine interaction.

title: Translating Music to Touch: Exploring Tactile Perception of Pitch, Roughness, and Pleasantness
authors: Jeremy Marozeau, Alice Taylor, Sasha Novozhilova, Mario Prsa, Daniel Huber
abstract: Music is a rich multisensory experience, yet individuals with hearing impairments often lack access to this important aspect of culture. As tactile technologies advance, there is growing interest in whether musical information can be conveyed through vibration. This study investigates how core dimensions of auditory music perception, pitch, roughness, and pleasantness, can be translated into the tactile domain. Participants were asked to rate these perceptual dimensions for sinusoidal stimuli with frequencies ranging from 40 to 750 Hz, and for complex waveforms with fundamental frequencies of 52, 73, and 98 Hz, including amplitude modulated, sawtooth, and missing fundamental stimuli. Perceived pitch showed a systematic relationship with stimulus frequency for most participants, suggesting that tactile devices could at least partially convey some form of simple melodic patterns. The sawtooth waveform emerged as particularly effective for representing pitch changes, underscoring the role of rapid temporal transitions in tactile pitch encoding. Roughness ratings were negatively correlated with pleasantness, mirroring well-established findings in auditory perception. Waveforms with sudden temporal changes or rapid amplitude modulations were consistently judged as less pleasant. Taken together, these findings in normal hearing participants may inform the design of vibrotactile displays that could support access to selected music relevant perceptual dimensions, although generalization to people with hearing loss remains to be tested. Importantly, our results highlight the crucial role of the fast temporal envelope rather than the temporal fine structure, in shaping vibrotactile perception. Consistent with this interpretation, our data provide evidence for a tactile analog of the auditory missing fundamental phenomenon, reinforcing the idea that tactile pitch perception primarily relies on envelope periodicity rather than the presence of specific frequency components.

title: IQCM-Net: Cross-Modal Fusion with Multi-Window STFT via SNR Partitioning for Robust Modulation Recognition
authors: Junwei Li, Ming Huang, Jingjing Yang, Zhe Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Automatic Modulation Recognition (AMR) is crucial for cognitive radio and spectrum management. However, achieving robust performance, especially under low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) conditions, remains challenging. This paper introduces IQCM-Net, a novel deep learning architecture specifically designed for enhanced AMR. IQCM-Net leverages two key innovations: a Multi-Window STFT via SNR Partitioning preprocessing strategy, and a Bidirectional Cross-Attention Deformable Convolution (BCADC) fusion module. The Multi-Window STFT approach tailors Time-Frequency (T-F) analysis by assigning pre-optimized window sizes to distinct SNR partitions, improving feature quality across varying noise levels. The BCADC module then effectively fuses temporal and frequency-domain features through dynamic cross-modal attention and receptive fields provided by deformable convolutions. Complemented by a TCN for long-range dependency modeling and a hierarchical feature extraction backbone, IQCM-Net generates highly discriminative representations. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets (RML2016.10a/b, RML22 and RML2018) demonstrate that IQCM-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly outperforming existing methods, particularly in low SNR conditions. The results validate the efficacy of the proposed dynamic preprocessing and cross-modal fusion strategies for robust AMR.

title: The Integration of Interactive Psychoeducation and Virtual Agent Embodiment in Virtual Reality Training: An Evaluation on Breathing Techniques
authors: Christian Corrò, Luca Chittaro
abstract: Immersive Virtual Reality (VR) is increasingly used in education for its ability to create engaging learning experiences. However, VR-based systems for skill acquisition, especially in the health domain, often lack adequate educational foundations, potentially limiting their effectiveness. To address this limitation, this paper investigates an interactive psychoeducation that incorporates users' real-time physiological signals. Moreover, given the growing use of virtual agents in education and their potential to enhance psychoeducation delivery through natural, human-like interactions, the paper also studies the role of virtual agent embodiment as a second variable. We conducted a 2×2 between-subjects study (n = 56) using a VRbased breathing training system, varying the presence of psychoeducation and virtual agent embodiment to assess their individual and combined impacts on users' self-efficacy, motivation, attention, social presence, and performance of the taught skill. Our results revealed that psychoeducation significantly enhanced self-efficacy and motivation, particularly by increasing participants' interest in, and perceived value of, the experience, while agent embodiment improved social presence, relatedness, and skill performance. These findings show that interactive psychoeducation and embodied virtual agents can enhance complementary aspects of VR-based training effectiveness.

title: Template Method for Microstrip Antenna Fabrication_supp2-3681778.xlsx
authors: Daniel Yeboah
abstract: Fifth generation (5G) network considerably improves communication capabilities and has therefore caught the attention of researchers, industry and government. Owing to its desirable bandwidth, front end miniaturized radio frequency components, low interference and high resolution, it is considered a potential candidate for high-speed communication services. Additive manufacturing on the other hand, has changed how products are developed owing to its low cost and part customization capability. Diverse printing techniques and materials further allow for new substrate properties when designing patch antennas. Thus, it can be used as an option to fabricate sub-6 GHz antenna devices. The present work studies a method where substrates made of a commercial resin are imprinted with nickel films for planar, compact, low-profile patch antennas with the aid of separately printed template patterns and electrodeposition. The electrical and material integrity of antennas are characterized to determine which process parameter(s) offer optimum fabrication. Simulations were carried out using ANSYS High-Frequency Structure Simulator (HFSS®) to optimize both slot and non-slot patch antennas prior to fabrication. The antennas are found to be operable at 6.9 GHz and 5.8 GHz respectively with bandwidths of 0.77 and 1.77 GHz. The antennas via our novel method have a potential for mobile applications, particularly WLAN and 5G networks.

title: Unleashing the Potential of Skyrmion Racetrack Memory as Energy-Efficient On-Chip Memory
authors: Yong-Cheng Liaw, Ya-Hui Yang, Shuo-Han Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: Skyrmion racetrack memory (SK-RM) is a promising replacement for static random-access memory (SRAM) as an on-chip memory device owing to its high memory density and near-zero static power consumption. Unlike other nonvolatile random-access memories (NVRAMs), SK-RM requires shift operations to move data bits across access ports, where data reads and writes are performed, along the racetrack. To facilitate data management on SK-RM, word-based mapping and bit-interleaved mapping architectures have been proposed. While word-based mapping can achieve higher energy efficiency by re-permutating existing skyrmions to compose new data patterns, bit-interleaved mapping achieves shorter latency by parallel data reads and writes. However, when SK-RM is used as on-chip memory or as a cache device, current mapping architectures often lead to issues such as unpredictable access performance or excessive energy consumption during cache operations. This paper addresses the aforementioned concerns by proposing a dual-write-mode (SK-DWM) strategy that leverages the advantages of existing mapping architectures and allows seamless switching of the data update policy on SK-RM. In addition, to adaptively fit the write latency requirement of each on-chip memory access, this study further proposes the SK-DWM<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{+}$</tex-math></inline-formula> strategy to adjust the data update policy dynamically for either low-latency or energy-efficient data updates. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is demonstrated through benchmark-driven experiments.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Green Industry: Pathways to Sustainable Economic Growth
authors: Priti Sharma, Rupa Khanna Malhotra, Himani Upreti, Amar Johri, Anu Sayal, Palak Saxena
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Since its inception in the middle of the 20th century, artificial intelligence (AI) has changed from focussing on mimicking human intelligence to solving a wide range of problems. “Applied Intelligence,” which applies intelligent systems to real-world issues, has emerged as a result of this change. AI also has a big impact on green development and ecological civilisation, identifying several issues and encouraging integration with different businesses. A qualitative and quantitative analysis of AI's potential to support green development finds opportunities and suggests solutions. Traditional methods associated with excessive energy use, waste, and environmental damage present serious problems for the worldwide industrial sector. To meet sustainable development big data, social media and data sciences are necessary to meet the desired results. Demand for sustainable manufacturing, which seeks to balance minimising adverse environmental effects with satisfying global economic demands, has increased as a result of growing awareness of these issues. This entails applying the concepts of sustainability—economic performance, environmental stewardship, and social responsibility-to every stage of the manufacturing process, from design to disposal. Increased consumer awareness and strict government regulations which necessities industries to achieve sustainable development goals are the factors driving the need for sustainable practices. Artificial intelligence is supporting industries in each possible way to achieve the objective of green industry and reduces carbon footprints. This paper explores the impact of Artificial Intelligence on carbon footprints leading to green Industry.

title: Research on Series-Parallel Hybrid Compensation Equipment for High Voltage Problem Management and Fault Ride-Through Capability Enhancement in Photovoltaic Access to Distribution Network
authors: Shaojie Liu, Yingying Zhao, Tangyun Xu, Luyao Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: After the access of distributed photovoltaic, the problem of high voltage appears in many stations. In addition, the fault ride-through capability of distributed photovoltaic inverter is low. In view of the voltage problem and fault ride-through risk faced by distributed photovoltaic grid connection, this paper first analyzes the causes of overvoltage caused by distributed photovoltaic grid-connection, and then puts forward a series-parallel hybrid compensation device for solving the high voltage problem of distributed photovoltaic grid connection and improving the fault ride-through capacity. Through the combined use and coordinated operation of series reactor and static var generator (SVG), the voltage level of the device access point is dynamically adjusted, the overvoltage problem is solved, and the high-low voltage ride-through capability of photovoltaic inverter is improved. The device is simple in structure and control strategy, and high in reliability, so it is very suitable for over-voltage control and fault ride-through capacity improvement demand scenarios when distributed photovoltaic is connected to the distribution network in large quantities.

title: Resilient Multi-Modal IoT Crowd Monitoring System with Self-Healing Sensor Architecture and Intelligent Alert Prioritization
authors: S. Ramesh, B. Rohith, K. Chetananand, R. Manikandan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Mass gatherings in public venues and transport hubs pose safety risks due to overcrowding and unexpected emergencies. Nevertheless, most of the existing crowdmonitoring systems are solely dependent on single-modality sensing, centralized cloud processing, or laborious manual oversight. This paper presents the design of a resilient and privacy-preserving IoT for crowd safety management that effectively integrates multiple sensing modalities: RGB/thermal vision, WiFi-based crowd estimation, and acoustic/event sensing alongside lightweight inference at the edge on resource-constrained devices. A self-healing network can independently detect faults, dynamically reconfigure, and maintain continuity, while an intelligent multi-channel alert engine prioritizes notifications across push, SMS, sirens, and responder consoles. Multi-modal sensor fusion leverages WiFi-based probabilistic estimation and fuses it with visionbased density modeling to improve robustness in low-light or occluded environments. The notion of self-healing further reduces downtime and blind-spots, while edge-first alerting ensures rapid, low-latency dissemination of critical warnings. With a comprehensive prototype evaluation, the system demonstrates high accuracy, fast response times, and is resilient to sensor failures and varying crowd conditions.

title: Fault Diagnosis of Secondary System of Intelligent Substation Based on Knowledge Graph
authors: Kai Song, Ze Xu, Xuhong Li, Weijie Xiao, Yu Xiao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low accuracy and efficiency of fault diagnosis of secondary system of intelligent substation, this paper proposes a knowledge graph-based fault diagnosis program for secondary system of intelligent substation. The BERT-BiLSTM-CRF entity extraction algorithm and CASREL relationship extraction algorithm are utilized to extract common fault information and its corresponding solutions from the secondary equipment technical documents and historical defect library, and establish the secondary equipment fault knowledge graph, and efficient and reliable diagnosis of the secondary system faults of the intelligent substation can be realized by querying the knowledge graph of the secondary equipment faults. The test results show that the precision rate, recall rate and F1 value of the BERT-BiLSTM-CRF algorithm adopted in this paper are 90.56%, 91.22% and 90.89%, respectively, which are better than the other comparative models, and it can more accurately construct the knowledge map of secondary equipment faults, effectively improve the accuracy and efficiency of fault diagnosis, and provide a new technological path for the diagnosis of power system faults. It provides a new technical path for power system fault diagnosis.

title: Multi-Objective Energy Management Strategy for Fuel Cell Trucks Using DP-Pretrained SAC
authors: Zhiqiang Zhou, Hongwen He, Jingda Wu, Kunang Li, Ruchen Huang, Jiaqi Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: In the development of fuel cell hybrid electric trucks, the energy management strategy (EMS) plays a key role in enhancing system efficiency and operational economy. The objectives of the EMS include not only minimizing hydrogen consumption but also extending the powertrain system's lifespan to support long-term sustainable commercial operation. This paper proposes a multi-objective energy management framework that integrates Dynamic Programming (DP) and the Soft ActorCritic (SAC) algorithm. Specifically, DP is utilized offline to generate optimal energy allocation trajectories, which are then used to initialize the SAC replay buffer and thereby warm start the training process. Furthermore, a multi-objective reward function is designed to jointly optimize hydrogen consumption and the degradation of both the fuel cell and lithium-ion battery systems. Simulation results show that the proposed strategy incurs only 2.86% higher total cost compared to the DP baseline, while achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 1 1 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3. 7 4 \%}$</tex> lower costs than the standard SAC and rule-based strategies, respectively. In addition, the training time is reduced by 50.64% compared to the standard SAC method, confirming the effectiveness of the pretrained SAC approach in accelerating convergence and delivering highperformance energy management.

title: An Active-Clamped Phase-Shifted Full-Bridge Converter
authors: Zhiping Lei, Chunguang Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: To solve the drawbacks of conventional Phaseshifted Full-bridge (PSFB) DC-DC converter with current doubler rectifier, a switched-capacitor scheme is proposed. Thanks to the easy PWM control of the switched capacitors, ZVS for the primary side switches is achieved at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0} \boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{1 0 0 {\%}}$</tex> load range; no rectifier diode voltage swinging during power transfer from the transformer primary side to the secondary side; nearly zero circulating current during the idle interval. What's more, the turning-off current of primary side switches is nearly zero, with the finely-designed switched capacitors and resonant inductor. The efficiency of the converter at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0 \sim 100 {\%}$</tex> load range could be extremely high if only the switches for switched capacitors is finely chosen. The experiment result is presented.

title: Design and Comparison of New Bio-Inspired Antenna-Sensors for Dielectric Characterization
authors: Elvis Ferreira Soares, José Garibaldi Duarte Júnior, Valdemir Praxedes da Silva Neto
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: A comparative analysis of three new bio-inspired antenna models applied to sensing and dielectric characterization of materials is presented. The Interdigital Capacitor (IDC) and Complementary Square Split-ring Resonator (CSSRR) geometries are used within the reference model for comparative sensitivity study and analysis. The frequency response of the proposed models was investigated. Antenna/sensor models with dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70 \times 70 \times 1.57 \text{mm}$</tex> were constructed and subjected to experimental measurements, which validated their design, with the CSSRR model being the most sensitive. In general, the proposed sensor models demonstrate a good level of accuracy in the dielectric characterization process of materials.

title: Adaptive Cryptographic Confidential Computing Framework for Continuous Cloud Data Protection
authors: Varshitha B A, Shravya R, Shreeraksha M, Ajay Vikram Singh, Vijaya Padmanabha
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The Adaptive Cryptographic Confidential Computing framework introduced here aims to keep data protected throughout its entire journey in the cloud whether it is sitting in storage, moving between services or being processed in real time. In many existing systems, encryption tends to stop at storage or transmission which still leaves the data exposed while computations are taking place. The work addresses this gap by bringing together a small simulated confidential execution environment, Azure key vault for handling and rotating keys and the use of Managed Identities so that no passwords or static secrets are involved. A policy engine assigns a sensitivity level to the input and selects one of three AEAD algorithms. AES-256-GCM is applied for high sensitivity data, AES-128-GCM for moderate security needs and ChaCha20-Poly1305 for performance oriented environments. When tested on Azure for Students, the system behaved consistently with predictable throughput and only minor delays caused by key derivation steps. The secure execution checks worked reliably even with larger files of around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ M B}$</tex>. Since the design includes continuous encryption and automatic key rotation it aligns naturally with the requirements of common regulatory standards.

title: An Explainable Hybrid CNN–Transformer Framework for Detection of AI-Generated Images and Videos
authors: K. Bhaskar, K. Madhu Kanth, K. Ajith Kumar, L. Sampath Kumar, G. Nithin Roy
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: The fast growth of generative AI has made it easy to create very realistic fake images and videos. This has created serious problems for digital trust, as such content can spread misinformation very quickly. Because of this, there is a strong need for detection methods that are not only accurate but also easy to understand and explain. In this work, the task of detecting AIgenerated images is studied using the CIFAKE dataset. First, popular deep learning models such as ResNet18, EfficientNet-B0, and ConvNeXt-Tiny are tested as baseline classifiers. Although these models achieve good accuracy, they struggle with explanation and do not always generalize well. To address these issues, a hybrid deep learning model is proposed. It combines CNNs to capture fine image details, Vision Transformers to understand the overall image context, and an attention mechanism to focus on important features. This approach achieves a test accuracy of 94.47% and an AUC score of 0.9833 on the CIFAKE dataset, showing reliable and stable performance. To make the model's decisions easier to understand, Grad-CAM is used to highlight the image regions that influence predictions. The model is also extended to videos by analyzing frames individually and combining their results to make final video-level decisions. A complete system with frontend and backend support allows users to upload images or videos, view explanation maps, and download annotated videos. Overall, the proposed AI-based approach provides a good balance between accuracy, transparency, and real-world usability for detecting synthetic media.

title: INNOVITALS- a Healthcare Application Using Random Forest Algorithm
authors: Riya Karki, Sambhav Goel, Shivani Kumari, Rishita Bhatnagar, Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The need for a more patient-centered digital solution is further emphasized by the fact that conventional outpatient healthcare systems often experience difficulties in meeting the increasing demand for accessible medical services. This article presents the introduction of a healthcare platform, INNOVITALS, which can aid in the enhancement of medical report analysis, appointment scheduling, and patient engagement. The platform integrates a flask-based application, mongoDB, and react native mobile application, which can automate appointment bookings, enable online payment, and provide emergency services through GPS tracking of ambulances. The objectives of this study include integrating a predictive model for the early detection of heart disease through machine learning. The model will utilize the UC Heart Disease dataset, which will then undergo supervised learning using four algorithms: Decision Tree, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). The model will then determine which algorithm can accurately predict heart disease. The study revealed that the Random Forest Classifier algorithm had the highest accuracy of 90%, which further validates the algorithm's effectiveness in risk prediction. This demonstrates the application of machine learning in preventive healthcare, enabling patients to make informed decisions through the application of digital healthcare.

title: Deep Learning-Based Ensemble Approach for Sugarcane Leaf Disease Detection
authors: N. Chitra, T. Deepa, Theeban S, Yuvaraj U
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Sugarcane is an important part of the world's agriculture, but leaf diseases that are often undetected in the early stages pose a threat to its yield and quality. Expert visual inspection is a major component of conventional disease identification, which makes the process laborious, subjective, and prone to human error. A viable substitute is offered by recent developments in deep learning and image-based analysis, which allow for precise and automated disease identification. In order to identify and categorize sugarcane leaf diseases using image data, this study suggests an ensemble-based deep learning framework. InceptionV3 and ResNet50, two potent pre-trained convolutional neural networks, are integrated into the framework to take advantage of their complementary feature extraction capabilities. The ensemble model successfully improves classification robustness and generalization by combining their predictions. According to experimental results, the ensemble approach outperforms the individual InceptionV3 and ResNet50 models, which achieve accuracies of 36% and 48%, respectively, with a classification accuracy of 97%. The suggested approach is a useful and effective solution for precision agriculture and smart farming applications since it not only increases diagnostic reliability but also provides scalability to other crop types.

title: Multifunctional Reconfigurable Millimeter Wave Dielectric Resonator Antenna
authors: Muhamad Afiq Aiman Bin Zulkefli, Rawad Asfour, Muntadher Alsabah, Dariush Mirsheker-Syahkal, Salam K Khamas
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: Amillimeter-wave (mmWave) dielectric resonator antenna (DRA) with multifunctional reconfigurability is proposed. The design integrates two PIN diodes into each arm of the feeding cross-slot, allowing electronic control over the effective arm length. This configuration enables the antenna to switch between three polarization states. Linear polarization (LP) is achieved when all diodes are either forward-biased (ON) or reverse-biased (OFF), resulting in dual-bandwidths. In contrast, circular polarization (CP) is realized when the diodes on one arm are ON and those on the other arm are OFF. Switching the biasing states of the four PIN diodes reconfigures the CP sense from left to right hand CP, or visa versa. The CP mode delivers a gain of over 6 dBic with an impedance bandwidth of ~2 0%.

title: Synchronous Switching Scheme - Bringing Modular Fault Tolerance to Power Converters
authors: Aditya Shirodkar, Satish Naik Banavath, Andrii Chub, Riccardo Mandrioli, Mattia Ricco, Dimitri Vinnikov
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Various critical applications in domains such as electric vehicles, space technology, defence, data-centers and microgrids require high power, uninterruptible and high reliability power electronic converters. This paper focusses on developing a highly modular and topology independent fault tolerant approach, making it an attractive solution for industrial applications. The paper validates the proposed approach through PLECS simulation of a 400 V and 5 kW full-bridge forward converter, and performs hardware validation at 400 V DC bus voltage as well. Further, the paper thoroughly analyses the losses, efficiency, reliability and mean time to failure of the proposed fault tolerance solution, and proves its effectiveness across various key factors in comparison to existing approaches in literature.

title: A Survey on Coded Matrix Computations and Gradient Coding
authors: Aditya Ramamoorthy, Anindya Bijoy Das
publish: IEEE BITS the Information Theory Magazine
abstract: In modern times, processing and storage needs have made distributed computing the technology of choice in various domains. How ever, large-scale distributed clusters (especially within the cloud) often suffer from the problem of stragglers (slow or failed workers). Coded distributed computation focuses on straggler mitigation ideas that have their roots in coding theory, i.e., it judiciously introduces redundancy across tasks assigned to workers such that the desired result can be obtained even in the presence of stragglers. Within distributed matrix computations and distributed parameter learning, it has led to key results on the trade-off between redundancy and the level of straggler mitigation that can be supported. In this survey, we overview the key ideas underpinning coded computation for both these applications. Following this, we offer insights about important considerations such as the numerical stability of recovery, leveraging partial work from slow (but not failed) workers, and considering scenarios where the inputs are sparse; we also discuss several works that have addressed these issues. We conclude with a discussion of open problems and future directions within these broad areas.

title: Optimizing Mission Abort Policy with Threshold Voting of Imperfect Shock Detectors
authors: Gregory Levitin, Liudong Xing
abstract: Shock count is a key parameter used in designing mission abort policies (MAPs) for systems executing their operations under random shock conditions. Existing models mostly assume a perfect mechanism of detecting shocks. In practice, the shock detection system may fail to detect shocks that have occurred (false negative) or flag non-existent shocks (false positive), both leading to wrong shock count and misleading MAP designs. This work's contribution lies in modeling a single-attempt mission system with a fault-tolerant shock detection system that applies threshold voting among multiple imperfect detectors to contribute to the mission abort decision based on shock count and system operation time. A probabilistic approach is put forward for assessing mission performance of the considered system in the form of task success probability (TSP), survival probability of system (SPS), and expected losses of mission (ELM). An ELM minimization problem is further formulated and solved, which aims to determine the optimal tri-parametric MAP, achieving a balance between TSP and SPS. We analyze a drone-based surveillance system to showcase the suggested model. We also examine the impact of key parameters (cost, shock occurrence rate and detection probability) on mission performance metrics and on the best-obtained MAPs, leading to important managerial recommendations.

title: Investigation of Post-Demagnetization Back-EMF in Modular Multi-Three-Phase Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machines
authors: Haolan Zhan, Wei Liu, Zekai Lyu, Jian Song, Shuangxia Niu, K. T. Chau
abstract: Modular multi-three-phase permanent magnet synchronous machines (MMTP-PMSMs) outperform due to their superior fault-tolerant capability. However, irreversible demagnetization of permanent magnets still poses a hazardous threat to the stable and safe operation of motor drive systems. Especially, the back electromagnetic force (back-EMF), as a crucial electromagnetic parameter, can deteriorate with the amplitude reduction and fault-related harmonics generation. This paper demonstrates the configuration modes of MMTP-PMSMs, and then reveals the influence of module arrangements on post-demagnetization back-EMF by the airgap field modulation theory-based analysis. The harmonic cancellation rules of post-demagnetization back-EMF are given based on the comprehensive consideration of module configurations in MMTP-PMSMs. Last but not least, the finite element results validate the above analysis well. The findings offer contributory insights for demagnetization analysis, diagnosis and tolerant control in MMTP-PMSMs.

title: Optimizing Inter-RAT Energy Efficiency in NR-NTNs Using Licensed and Unlicensed Spectrums via Cooperative DQN
authors: Youbin Kim, Heejung Yu, Howon Lee
abstract: This study aims to maximize the inter-radio access technology (RAT) energy efficiency of new radio non-terrestrial networks (NR-NTNs) operating at licensed (LB) and unlicensed bands (UB) simultaneously. Specifically, we jointly optimize UAV movement, transmission power, and duty cycle based on a cooperative multi-agent distributed deep Q-network (DQN) to maximize the inter-RAT energy efficiency while minimizing inter system interference between 5G new radio-unlicensed (NR-U) and Wi-Fi sharing the unlicensed frequency band. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly improves network-wide energy efficiency based on aggregate received signal strength indicator (RSSI) and reward sharing among agents compared to the benchmark algorithms.

title: VRN-Back: an Immersive Music Rhythm Game for Working Memory Training in Virtual Reality_supp2-3680633.mp4
authors: Haiyan Jiang
abstract: The N-back task is widely recognized as a paradigm for cognitive training due to its adaptability and effectiveness in mitigating working memory decline. However, its repetitive and monotonous design often leads to reduced engagement and poor long-term adherence, lowering intervention effectiveness. To address these limitations, researchers have turned to game-based interventions and immersive technologies to enhance user motivation and sustain training participation. In this study, we propose VRN-back, a music rhythm-based gamified cognitive training system in virtual reality (VR). The system combines the adaptive N-back paradigm with rhythm-game mechanics to increase enjoyment and motivation, while immersive interaction fosters engagement and enhances training outcomes. We conducted a seven-day study with 15 young adults to evaluate the system's impact on their working memory, transfer to related cognitive domains, and user experience. Results showed significant improvements in N-back accuracy, reaction time, and maximum N level, as well as transfer effects on attention and inhibitory control tasks. Subjective evaluations indicated good usability and user experience, with the System Usability Scale scoring 80.83/100, the short User Experience Questionnaire rating pragmatic quality as “Good” and hedonic quality as “Above Average”, and the Pleasure-Arousal-Dominance scale confirming sustained positive emotional states. Simulator sickness remained minimal before and after training, ensuring usability. These findings suggest the feasibility and effectiveness of immersive rhythm-based gamified VR systems for cognitive training, laying a foundation for future research on long-term cognitive intervention technologies.

title: Mining Association Patterns From Neighborhood Insight
authors: Honghong Cheng, Yuhua Qian, Xinyan Liang, Jiye Liang, Qingfu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Detecting and identifying complex association patterns between two variables is a fundamental task. This requires association measures that satisfy both generality (the ability to capture a wide range of association structures) and equitability (the absence of bias toward specific association types). Designing such measures is challenging due to the distributional uncertainty, structural diversity, and mixture of association types found in large datasets. Granular computing offers a promising direction, as local neighborhood structures naturally encode multi-scale association information. Inspired by this insight, we introduce the maximal neighborhood coefficient (MNC), an association measure based on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula>-NN granulation. MNC captures a broad range of associations without empirical bias while retaining local structural details often missed by existing measures. Extending this idea, we develop a family of maximal neighborhood nonparametric exploration (MNNE) statistics that supply richer auxiliary information for characterizing associations. Together, MNC and MNNE form a data-driven exploration toolkit that offers strong empirical performance and a new perspective on mining complex association patterns.

title: Shadowless Projection Mapping for Tabletop Workspaces with Synthetic Aperture Projector_supp2-3679111.mp4
authors: Masaki Takeuchi
abstract: Projection mapping (PM) enables augmented reality (AR) experiences without requiring users to wear head-mounted displays and supports multi-user interaction. It is regarded as a promising technology for a variety of applications in which users interact with content superimposed onto augmented objects in tabletop workspaces, including remote collaboration, healthcare, industrial design, urban planning, artwork creation, and office work. However, conventional PM systems often suffer from projection shadows when users occlude the light path. Prior approaches employing multiple distributed projectors can compensate for occlusion, but suffer from latency due to computational processing, degrading the user experience. In this research, we introduce a synthetic-aperture PM system that uses a significantly larger number of projectors, arranged densely in the environment, to achieve delay-free, shadowless projection for tabletop workspaces without requiring computational compensation. To address spatial resolution degradation caused by subpixel misalignment among overlaid projections, we develop and validate an offline blur compensation method whose computation time remains independent of the number of projectors. Furthermore, we demonstrate that our shadowless PM plays a critical role in achieving a fundamental goal of PM: altering material properties without evoking projection-like impression. Specifically, we define this perceptual impression as “sense of projection (SoP)” and establish a PM design framework to minimize the SoP based on user studies.

title: The dimension and Bose distance of some BCH codes of length q m −1/λ
authors: Run Zheng, Nung-Sing Sze, Zejun Huang
abstract: BCH codes are important error correction codes, widely utilized due to their robust algebraic structure, multierror correcting capability, and efficient decoding algorithms. Despite their practical importance and extensive study, their parameters, including dimension, minimum distance and Bose distance, remain largely unknown in general. This paper addresses this challenge by investigating the dimension and Bose distance of BCH codes of length (qm −1)/λ over the finite field Fq, where λ is a positive divisor of q−1. Specifically, for narrowsense BCH codes of this length with m ≥ 4, we derive explicit formulas for their dimension for designed distances 2 ≤ δ ≤ (q⌊(2m−1)/3⌋+1 −1)/λ+1. We also provide explicit formulas for their Bose distance in the range 2 ≤ δ ≤ (q⌊(2m−1)/3⌋+1 − 1)/λ. These ranges for δ are notably larger than the previously known results for this class of BCH codes. Furthermore, we extend these findings to determine the dimension and Bose distance for certain non-narrow-sense BCH codes of the same length. Several optimal linear codes can be obtained from these BCH codes.

title: Autonomous OCT-Guided Robotic System for Vertical Needle Insertion and Big-Bubble Dissection in Corneal Transplantation_supp5-3681869.docx
authors: Yaning Wang
abstract: Autonomous surgical robots can improve consistency and safety in clinical outcomes, independent of surgeon expertise. Deep anterior lamellar keratoplasty (DALK), a partial-thickness corneal transplant that separates Descemet's membrane (DM) from the anterior stroma, demands high precision to avoid perforating the DM during pneumodissection. We introduce AUTO-DALK, a real-time optical coherence tomography (OCT) sensing robotic system for autonomous vertical needle insertion into deep stromal layers. Unlike conventional horizontal approaches that risk incomplete dissection or DM perforation, AUTO-DALK employs a sensor-guided vertical trajectory to achieve more uniform pneumodissection. The system integrates an OCT fiber optic sensor with an anatomical segmentation model to enable real-time depth perception and closed-loop needle control. AUTO-DALK was evaluated in both ex vivo and in vivo rabbit models against 1) freehand, 2) OCT-guided manual, and 3) teleoperated robotic insertions. Results show superior performance in needle placement, dissection accuracy, completion time, and big bubble formation, demonstrating the feasibility of OCT-guided autonomous robotic execution for partial-thickness keratoplasty.

title: Experimental Verification on LoS-MIMO Transmission in 300-GHz Band
authors: Kazuhiko Tamesue, Seiji Nishi, Kunihisa Jitsuno, Toshio Sato, Takuro Sato, Tetsuya Kawanishi
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: In this study, we conducted Line-of-Sight (LoS) $2 \times 2$ multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) orthogonal frequencydivision multiplexing (OFDM) transmission experiments in the 300-GHz band to investigate the potential for channel capacity enhancement. A circularly polarized patch antenna, supporting both cross- and co-polarization, and a $2 \times 2$ MIMO transceiver equipped with a Pre-Correction scheme were developed. Experimental evaluation was carried out under conditions of a stream spacing of 0.3 m and a transmission distance of 1.9 m. As a result, in both co- and cross-polarized antenna configurations, the off-diagonal components were suppressed below –20 dB, and 2-stream transmission with 16-QAM modulation achieved a throughput of 13.1 Gbit/s and a spectral efficiency of 6.55 bit/s/Hz. Furthermore, future research directions toward advanced spatial multiplexing based on channel correlation analysis are discussed. These results demonstrate the feasibility of high-capacity fixed wireless links and provide practical design insights for long-distance transmission.

title: Design of a Circularly Polarized Omni-Directional Antenna with Frequency Reconfiguration Capability
authors: Le-Huy Trinh, Thanh-Tuan Phan, Dinh-Duy Phan, Manh-Thao Nguyen, Fabien Ferrero
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: In this paper, an omnidirectional circularly polarized (CP) antenna based on FR-4 substrate is proposed. It is designed for LPWAN applications operating in the 830–1000 MHz frequency range by reconfiguring the resonance using capacitors placed on three arc-shaped conductors. Circular polarization is achieved through phase-shifted excitation of the radiating elements, while omnidirectional radiation is obtained by combining a central capacitive pin with an electrical load. The simulation results show $S_{11}$ less than −10 dB, an axial ratio below 3 dB across the tuning range, and stable omnidirectional radiation with consistent gain performance.

title: Real Time Detection Sureveillance
authors: Kushagr Saxena, Neetu Mittal
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The internet of things ecosystem has caused a fundamental shift in the field of home security, and this study focuses on shifting from passive recording style surveillance, towards a more intelligent and proactive surveillance with real time identification. Even though automated facial recognition has many advantages, but on the edge based system have trouble with environment instability, especially when it comes to changing lights or inadequate lights. A well built, strong framework is proposed, that is developed with the collection of Multi-Angle Haar Cascades and Local Binary Pattern Histogram to maintain high accuracy detection in a variety of facial orientation and/or posture. The system achieved the lowest latency of around 45 ms and is optimized to work with limited computational resources. During the development phase an issue was faced which was identified as “Identity Flickering,” this was fixed by adding a temporal buffer with image processing methods, Gamma Correction and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization. The system then immediately sends out a secure SSL/TLS notification through email whenever it detects any unauthorized person.

title: A Joint Quotation and Clearing Model for Energy Storage Stations to Participate in Electricity Spot Market and Deep Peak Shaving Ancillary Service Under the New-Type Power System
authors: Jianzhao Li, Wenli Deng, Tao Liu, Yunwang Hu, Hongpeng Qi, Shimin Li, Tong Pan, Jianrong Qin, Lili Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: In the new-type power system, energy storage stations(ESS) serve as critical flexible resources for enhancing system economics and peak-shaving capacity. Addressing the deficiencies in spatiotemporal coupling mechanisms and price transmission models between electricity spot and peak shaving ancillary markets, A joint quotation and clearing model for ESS participation in both electricity spot and deep peak shaving ancillary markets is proposed. The model employs a bi-level optimization framework: at the upper level, the dispatch center operates to minimize system electricity procurement costs through joint clearing of the electricity spot and deep peak shaving ancillary markets; at the lower level, ESS dynamically optimize charge-discharge strategies and adjust quotation to maximize revenue. The case study is conducted based on the IEEE-39 bus system The results indicate that compared to the traditional sequential clearing mode, the proposed joint clearing mode effectively reduces system electricity procurement costs and increases ESS's revenue. This research provides theoretical support and practical examples for the coordinated operation of multiple markets in the new-type power system.

title: Model Predictive Control of Ironless Axial Flux Permanent Magnet Motor
authors: Han Chen, Zhihui Jin, Jian Luo, Chao Fang, Guanglin Li, Chunwei Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The coreless axial-flux permanent magnet synchronous motor (CAFPMSM), with its axial-flux design and coreless structure, offers advantages including high power density, superior efficiency, and low torque ripple, making it particularly suitable for space-constrained applications such as new energy vehicle in-wheel drives. Although model predictive control (MPC) exhibits fast dynamic response, its single-vector strategy suffers from insufficient steady-state accuracy. Compared with conventional three-vector MPC algorithms, this paper proposes an improved four-segment three-vector MPC method. By optimizing the actuation sequence of voltage vectors and reducing computational complexity, the proposed approach significantly enhances steady-state control accuracy while maintaining dynamic performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the method effectively reduces current total harmonic distortion (THD) under steady-state conditions, improves current tracking performance, and achieves fixed switching frequency operation.

title: Multimodal Machine Learning Framework for Quantifying Mental Stress From Wearable Biosensors
authors: Soumya T R, Yella Pranathi, Razia Begam, Vinay B L, Pavithra S, Kommi Himabindu
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: The evaluation of mental stress by wearable devices presents a good opportunity to achieve objective and continuous observation of human health. In this paper, we will present a multimodal machine learning model, which is capable of combining physiological indicators, including heart rate variations, electrodermal activity, and skin temperature to measure the level of mental stress. Complex interdependencies are also represented using feature-level fusion and ensemble learning methods in order to investigate complex interactions between biosignals. The framework is tested on a controlled experimental dataset where stress is annotated by ground-truth and it is shown to have better accuracy and robustness to use than unimodal methods. Also, interpretable feature importance analysis shows important biomarkers that can be used to predict stress. The suggested system offers a scalable platform of real-time stress monitoring within the healthcare, workplace, and behavioral research settings.

title: Intrusion Detection System Based on Multi-Level Feature Extraction Using FCN Transformer Graph SAGE, GAT and Inductive Network
authors: Javvaji Sai Varshitha, Mittaveedi Bhargav, Marupuri Kushal, Mopuri Jagan Mohan Reddy, C. Madhusudhana Rao
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: With the rapid growth of network traffic and the increasing sophistication of cyber threats, traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) face challenges in detecting complex and evolving attack patterns. Many Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) were proposed integrating multi-level feature extraction with FCN-Transformer and inductive Graph Neural Networks. The FCN captures local flow patterns, while the Transformer models long-range dependencies. Limitations include sensitivity to graph constructions. To Overcome above limitations, we are proposing two inductive methods—Graph SAGE and GAT—are applied for relational reasoning, enabling detection of unseen hosts and services. Performance is evaluated using metrics such as accuracy, macro-F1, AUROC, MCC, and inference latency. This method is implemented end-to-end in Python with suitable libraries

title: Rose Leaves Diseases Prediction Using Machine Learning Technique
authors: Ishita Uniyal, Shiv Ashish Dhondiyal
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Rose plants may have a wide range of illnesses, some of which will stunt their development and others that can kill them outright. The only way to effectively treat and stop the spread of these diseases is to find them early and diagnose them thoroughly. The use of machine learning for agricultural disease prediction and detection has demonstrated encouraging outcomes in the last several years. A prediction model for the classification of common illnesses affecting rose leaves using machine learning techniques is the goal of this project. Powdery mildew, black spot, and rust are some of the diseases that can harm rose leaves. The suggested model uses a dataset that includes both healthy and diseased leaves. To improve the dataset's quality and variety, image preprocessing procedures including resizing, standardisation, and augmentation are used. To begin, we use feature extraction methods to extract useful information from the photos. Then, we use tools like principal component analysis (PCA) or feature importance analysis to choose the best features. K-Mean clustering and support vector machines (SVM) are only two of the many machine learning approaches that get insight from the processed information. Measures of performance including F1 score, recall, accuracy, and precision are used to assess the models' efficacy in illness prediction. The experimental findings demonstrate that the proposed technique successfully predicts the presence of various illnesses in rose leaves. An agricultural system or an easy-to-use software can use the proposed prediction model to detect rose leaf illnesses early and monitor them in real-time. Timely management methods may be implemented to avoid crop losses and promote sustainable rose production practices.

title: Design and Simulation Verification of Cooperative Guidance Law for Flying Robots Based on Time Constraints and Yaw Angle Constraints

title: Audit Risk Profiling of Corporate Executives: An Intelligent Model and Effectiveness Evaluation
authors: Li WANG, Xiaowei LIU, Hongkun XIE, Mengqi LI
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional economic responsibility audit evaluations for corporate executives, such as static assessment and subjectivity, this paper constructs a datadriven intelligent audit profiling system. Guided by the principles of multi-layered, contextualized, quantifiable, and explainable design, we develop a structured tag system encompassing four dimensions: basic attributes, responsibility, capability, and compliance. A comprehensive framework for executive audit profiling is designed, covering data governance, feature engineering, model building, and profile generation. A two-stage hybrid modeling strategy integrating logistic regression and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C 5. 0}$</tex> decision tree algorithm is employed to enable the intelligent identification and prediction of performance risks. Results from comparative algorithm performance experiments indicate that the decision tree model achieves the best performance, with a prediction accuracy of 86.96 %. Model robustness evaluation shows an AUC value of 0.91, demonstrating strong generalization capability and the ability to generate interpretable risk decision rules. This study provides a concrete solution and practical pathway for transforming executive audit work from experience-based judgment to data-driven analysis and from ex-post verification to in-process early warning.

title: AI Tool Adoption and Employee Performance in the Education Sector: An HR Analytics Study
authors: Presha Khurana, Shikha Kapoor, Pranay Verma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: This paper examines whether AI tool adoption in the education sector is reflected in improved employee performance, and whether perceived effectiveness of AI-based tools, organisational support, and workload influence this relationship. In this research paper, we present a questionnaire-based empirical analysis focused on AI use in routine work tasks such as drafting, planning, summarizing, communication, and documentation. AI usage alone may not translate into better workplace outcomes, as employees often spend time correcting outputs and may benefit less when support is weak and workload is intense. To gather data, a structured questionnaire will be administered to employees across academic and administrative roles, capturing adoption behaviour, perceived effectiveness (speed, quality, and effort reduction), workplace support, workload conditions, and performance outcomes. The collected responses will be checked for consistency and analyzed using descriptive statistics, correlation tests, and regression to identify the key factors linked to employee performance. The study aims to produce actionable HR insights - showing not only whether AI is used, but whether it is experienced as effective, and what organisational conditions are needed for performance improvement.

title: Distributed-Caustic Synthesis for Near-Field Beam Radiation at Microwaves and Millimeter Waves
authors: Federica Anfuso, Ahsan Ullah Khan, Gino Sorbello, Santi Concetto Pavone
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: We introduce a novel method for the synthesis of focused beams by shaping spatially distributed ray caustics at microwaves and millimeter waves. The proposed approach provides increased flexibility in beam synthesis process in the radiative near field, and enables the design of advanced communication systems and nextgeneration near-field beam launchers.

title: Time Division Coexistence of Wireless Communication and Wireless Power Transfer
authors: Taketoshi Nakajima, Fumiko Ohori, Takeshi Matsumura, Satoko Itaya
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a management system for time-division coexistence between Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) and other wireless systems using Smart Resource Flow (SRF) wireless platform. And we demonstrate that WPT and wireless LAN communication, which do not know each other, can coexist by time division with a cycle of around 100 ms in this system.

title: Explainable Symptom-Based Chronic Disease Assessment Using Hybrid Rule-Based and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Ashish Verm, Ayush Kumar Dhuriya, Om Prakash
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Online Symptom checkers provide quick, preliminary insights into potential health issues based on user-entered symptoms and data from medical wearable devices. These are gaining popularity worldwide because they empower self-assessment, guide triage decisions, and promote timely case-seeking; however, most existing systems rely on opaque, black-box machine learning or deep learning, or on rigid rule-based approaches, which undermine user trust and transparency. In this paper, we propose an explainable hybrid rule-based and machine learning model for the early detection of chronic diseases. Clinical rules, defined by experts and consisting of IF-THEN elements, are used to identify high-confidence, high-risk symptom patterns. In contrast, machine learning models are applied to the inference of complex or ambiguous instances. Let these symptom-based features and ambiguous instances be merged using hybrid fusion techniques and further classified using a machine learning classifier. The system prioritizes explainability by highlighting activated rules and clinical features for every decision. Two different clinical datasets have been selected for Experimental assessments: the first is the Heart Disease Cleveland dataset, and the second is the Chronic Kidney Disease dataset. These results suggested that the framework has the potential to provide accessible, reliable, symptom-based health advice at the initial stage.

title: Optimizing Hospital Appointments: Integrating Genetic Algorithms and Constraint Programming
authors: Bilal Ansari, Rajiv Kumar Nath
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Appointment in hospitals is a challenge that has been experienced severally in healthcare because of resource constraints, wait time and conflicts of preference. The article proposes a hybrid genetic algorithm and constraint satisfaction algorithm optimization model to give effective multi-purpose patterns of solving the problem of scheduling. The constraint propagation to the evolutionary process of the genetic algorithm is introduced in the methodology to ensure that the schedules are feasible, as well as to optimise significant variables that are the utilisation of the rooms, the equity of the appointment and the efficiency of the overall working procedure. The suggested methodology prescribes a special chromosome representation of appointment slots, crossover and mutation operators which enforces hard constraints like doctor availability, no over-lap constraint, crossover constraint, patient priorities, and dynamic arrivals, a fitness function which components the soft constraints which balances the patient priorities and the dynamic arrivals. Evaluation of realistic hospital problem cases confirms that the framework is better than the conventional genetic algorithm and independent constraint solvers in generation of workable schedules. The research improves the health care processes, offers an elastic and extendable tool of real-time schedule, and can be applied in various hospital environments to promote the operational performance and consumer gratification.

title: Predictive AI-Driven Firewall Architecture Using Proactive Xfusion Framework for Proactive and Explainable Threat Detection
authors: Gautam Girish Chawla
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Modern enterprise networks face advanced and rapidly evolving cyber threats that often bypass traditional signature-based firewalls and intrusion detection systems. These conventional approaches lack proactive threat identification, temporal behavior modeling, and explainability, limiting their effectiveness in high-volume, real-time environments. To address these challenges, this research presents ProActive XFusion, a predictive AI-driven firewall that integrates temporal deep learning, ensemble feature-based learning, and hybrid modeling to detect threats with high accuracy and low latency. Explainable AI techniques, SHAP and LIME, provide both global and local transparency, while an interactive Gradio interface enables real-time analyst decision-making. Experimental evaluation demonstrates substantial performance gains, achieving 93.7% accuracy, 96.0% precision, 94.8% recall, 95.4% F1-score, 98.8% ROC-AUC, and 1.3 ms detection latency, confirming the system's reliability and interpretability. These results highlight the potential of ProActive XFusion to advance proactive, scalable, and explainable cybersecurity operations in modern network environments.

title: A Novel Compact Microstrip Patch Antenna Using Bending Lines for Multi-Band 5G mm-Wave Applications
authors: Mohammed El Amine Mahlia, Slimane Mekaoui, Mohamed Lamine Tounsi
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper presents a novel design for a miniaturized microstrip patch antenna optimized for multi-band 5G mm-wave applications. The proposed antenna operates over an extensive frequency spectrum ranging from 23.96 GHz to 119.24 GHz, covering seventeen critical frequency bands while ensuring optimal radiation performance. The antenna exhibits a maximum gain of 11.6 dBi at 87.08 GHz, with a return loss varying between $\mathrm{n}-12.15 \mathrm{~dB}$ and -30.9 dB. The bandwidth spans from 1.04 GHz to 7.23 GHz, making it highly adaptable for diverse 5G mm-wave communication scenarios. Furthermore, its ultra-compact dimensions of (7.00 $\times 21.90 \times 0.25$) $\mathrm{mm}^{3}$ enable seamless integration into nextgeneration mobile devices and wireless communication systems.
