title: Advanced Beam Steering and Shaping via Reconfigurable Transmissive Metasurfaces
authors: Stefano Vellucci, Alessio Monti, Mirko Barbuto, Alessandro Toscano, Filiberto Bilotti
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: Reconfigurable transmissive metasurfaces designed according to Huygens’ principle provide powerful means for tailoring electromagnetic wave propagation in transmission. By locally controlling the phase distribution, they can steer or reshape radiation patterns without requiring modifications to the feed network of the antenna. In this work, we present several implementations of reconfigurable metasurfaces integrated with different radiating structures. Both beam steering and beam shaping capabilities are demonstrated through properly engineered surface configurations, emphasizing their potential for compact, lowloss, and adaptive antenna front-ends.

title: Automated Identification of Zika Virus Mosquito Vectors Using Ensemble Deep Learning
authors: Subhankar Mandal, Parminder Singh
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Identification of mosquito vectors and their proper use to prevent and control the outbreak of Zika virus at an early stage is critical. Traditional morphological identification techniques are however time-consuming, entail expert entomologists and in the field, use damaged specimens and complicated backgrounds. In the present paper, we suggest a computerassisted deep learning-based ensemble system to classify mosquito species with a particular emphasis on the identification of Zika vectors. Two publicly available datasets of real-world and laboratory mosquito images were combined to create a hybrid dataset with 25,912 images of 10 species of mosquitoes. Class balancing technique was class-wise to reduce the extreme class imbalance and increase the learning strength. Using a weighted ensemble strategy to combine two transfer learning models with complementary feature representations, the VGG16 and DenseNet121 were individually optimized and combined together. It has been experimentally shown that the proposed ensemble model has a multi-class classification of 92.43% and a binary Zika vector detection of 99.46% accuracy, being more effective compared to single models and current methods. The confusion matrices and performance per-class analysis confirm that the ensemble greatly decreases the misclassification between different intragenus, but does not decrease the recall of Zika vectors species. The suggested framework promises an effective, scalable, and affordable solution to real-time surveillance of mosquito vectors and can assist the authorities of health of the population to plan the early assessment of the risk of outbreak and control of the vectors.

title: Design of Smart Pedagogy and Decision Making with Augmented Reality and Fuzzy Logic as Paradigm Shift in History Learning
authors: Neelam Rawat, Raju Ranjan, Ismailov Azizbek, Buzrukova Dilorom, Shirin Rakhmonova, Anvar Tursunov
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The practices of history education are usually associated with the traditional and text-focused pedagogical strategies that restrain the learners and do not provide them with the opportunity to capture the rich cognitive mechanisms of contextual understanding, critical thinking, and processing of historical decisions. This study suggests a smart pedagogic model based on an augmented reality (AR) and the use of fuzzy logic-based decision making as one of the paradigm shifts in the learning of history. The application of augmented reality allows the establishment of immersive and engaging historical learning spaces, which allow learners to practically experience the events, artifacts and cultural backgrounds. The pedagogical cues, and data on learners interaction with the system (e.g. level of engagement, understanding the underlying context, interpretation of evidence, general analytical reasoning and accuracy of making decisions) are captured and assessed systematically through the system based on a fuzzy inference machine. The ambiguity and subjectivity during the evaluation of students can be dealt with successfully using the fuzzy logic, where the learner performance is represented as a multi-linguistic variable with fuzzy rules.

title: Postpartum Depression Detection Using Ensemble Learning
authors: Ojas Arora, Dhyanendra Jain, Namita Sahay, Shweta Nanda
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Postpartum Depression (PPD) is an important issue in mental health among new mothers in the world at a rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 2 0}$</tex> percent. Timely intervention in PPD is the best option to live with good health outcomes as mother and child. The paper introduces an all-inclusive machine learning strategy to detect PPD based on 5,000 simulated maternal health records. In this paper, we apply and compare five single machine learning algorithms, including Logistic Regression, random forest, support vector machine (SVM), Naive Bayes, and XGBoost and three ensemble learning strategies: Hard Voting, Soft Voting, and Stacking. The samples were trained considering <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4}$</tex> characteristics such as demographic data, pregnancy complications, delivery type, sleep patterns, and psychological symptoms. Outcomes show that the use of ensemble techniques and specifically Stacking has a better performance with a high accuracy of over 92%. XGBoost and random forest also performed well with minimum accuracy of 91% on an individual basis. The researchers find such major predictive characteristics as frequent sadness, loss of interest, sudden crying, and sleep deprivation. This study can help in the creation of automated PPD screening instruments that will help healthcare professionals to early detect and intervene with at-risk mothers.

title: Comparative Analysis of YOLOv10, YOLOv11, and YOLOv12 for Retail Self-Checkout Systems
authors: Barla Sai Vardhan, Godha Karthikeyan Yadav, K.H. Murali, Nallela Sai Nikhil Tej
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Automated self-checkout systems are pivotal in the modernization of the retail sector, yet their effectiveness hinges on the underlying technology's ability to recognize products with both speed and precision. This paper explores a deep learning framework designed for this application, leveraging the You Only Look Once (YOLO) object detection algorithm, distinguished by its single-stage architecture. Unlike traditional two-stage detectors that require a separate process to first identify regions of interest, YOLO frames object detection as a single regression problem, directly predicting bounding boxes and class probabilities from full images in one evaluation. This unified approach significantly reduces computational complexity, making it ideal for real-time applications. We conduct a rig-orous comparative analysis across three successive generations of this algorithm— YOLOv10, YOLOv11, and YOLOv12—and their respective nano (n), small (s), and medium (m) variants. Performance was benchmarked using mean Average Precision (mAP) and latency to measure speed. Findings show YOLOv12m achieved highest precision with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P} \boldsymbol{@} \mathbf{5 0}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 1 \%}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5. 6 5}$</tex> ms latency, while YOLOv11n offered a balanced trade-off with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.8 \% \text{mAP}$</tex> @ 50 and 17.29 ms latency.

title: Demonstrating Code Mutations Using LLMs: A Sandbox Approach to Safe Malware Testing
authors: Indhuja. E, Noel Ashik.J.S, Dheeraj. K, Surya Prakash.V
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: There is a rapid increase in the use of Large Language Models (LLMs). These models have made advanced code generators democratically available, introducing a dual-use threat where automated agents can be directed to iteratively generate malware. Although existing literature discusses static code obfuscation, few studies investigate the effectiveness of closed-loop execution feedback in evading contemporary behavioral defenses. This paper proposes an autonomous malware mutation framework utilizing an uncensored local LLM (Dolphin 3.0) to produce, execute, and debug malicious payloads within an instrumented Windows 10 sandbox. In contrast to conventional one-shot generation techniques, this system adopts a genetic evolution cycle, using runtime error logs and Antivirus (AV) detection events to guide the LLM in rewriting code. The framework was evaluated in a two-phase longitudinal study against Windows Defender with Real-Time Protection fully enabled and no folder exclusions. Phase I (Exploratory) established a baseline over 14 random runs, where ‘noisy’ ransomware activities were successfully identified by Controlled Folder Access (28.6% detection rate). Phase II (Targeted Evasion), however, refined payloads across four distinct categories of malware-Reconnaissance, Espionage, Command and Control and Sabotage- 100 percent evasion. These results reveal a lethal loophole in currently deployed behavioral heuristics in that they could not identify malicious behavior on Python-based automatically-executed executables, even in the midst of aggressive actions like network tunneling and file encryption attempts. The findings explain that despite the effectiveness of commercial AVs in terms of known signatures, they are susceptible to the polymorphism and iterativeness of code generated by LLMs.

title: Na senda das dimensões da média-arte digital nas narrativas biográficas em Macau: uma perspetiva à luz da diversidade cultural
authors: Selma Pereira, Adérito Fernandes Marcos, Ricardo Alexino
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 12th International Conference on Digital and Interactive Arts: Media Art Cultures, Communities & Territories
abstract: None

title: AFASTAR E APROXIMAR: Narrativa audiovisual sobre a (re)conexão entre o Homem e a Natureza. Presença entre imagem, som e natureza
authors: Sara Cruz, José Bidarra
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 12th International Conference on Digital and Interactive Arts: Media Art Cultures, Communities & Territories
abstract: None

title: Analytical Models for Calculating the Inductances of Electromagnetic Band Gap Structures
authors: Robert Stöcker, Ivan Ndip
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This work presents a novel approach for modeling via inductance in Electromagnetic Band Gap (EBG) structures. The concept of partial inductance is employed to develop an analytical, frequency-dependent model for via inductance. Initially, a single via is analyzed to validate the modeling approach. Following successful validation, the model is extended to a Ground-Signal-Via (GSV) configuration. The proposed modeling technique achieves an accuracy with an error margin of less than 3%. This work lays the foundation for further modeling of inductance in other components of EBG structures, such as the patch elements and the ground plane.

title: Synthesis of Dual-Band Convoluted FSS Using Radial Basis Function Neural Networks
authors: Francisco Carlos G. da Silva Segundo, Alfredo Gomes Neto, Antonio Luiz P. S. Campos
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This paper proposes a dual-band frequency selective surface (FSS) synthesized with a radial basis function (RBF) neural network. A convoluted unit cell was optimized to operate at the ISM (2.45 GHz) and UNII (5.5 GHz) bands. A database of simulated responses was used to train the RBF model, which achieved accurate parameter prediction with a root-mean-square error of 0.02. The fabricated prototype showed good agreement with simulations, exhibiting dual-band operation, polarization independence, and angular stability. The results confirm the effectiveness of ANN-based synthesis for rapid and efficient FSS design in wireless communication applications.

title: Design and Simulation of Self-Organising Antenna Structures based on Computer-Integrated Manufacturing Platforms
authors: Junda Huang, Xiangzeng Kong, Zijian Li, Yanjie Tang, Dong Huang, Chuanyu Wu
publish: 2025 8th World Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Intelligent Manufacturing (WCMEIM)
abstract: The development of modern wireless communication technology puts forward higher requirements for antenna systems. In this paper, a new type of self-organised antenna structure is designed, which realises the unity of wide bandwidth coverage and high radiation efficiency, establishes a complete computer-integrated manufacturing platform, solves the problem of mass production of high-precision antenna, and deeply analyses the influence law of key structural parameters on antenna performance by using multi-parameter electromagnetic simulation technology, which reveals the mechanism of the position of the feed point, the characteristics of the substrate and the temperature factors. The research results provide new ideas for high-performance antenna design and intelligent manufacturing, and are of great significance for promoting the miniaturisation and multifunctionalisation of communication equipment.

title: Research on Dynamic and Economic Performance of Electric Vehicles Based on AVL Cruise
authors: Qihong Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Against the backdrop of energy security challenges and inefficiencies faced by traditional fuel vehicles, this study employs AVL Cruise and MATLAB to systematically explore the dynamic and economic performance of electric vehicles, aiming to provide insights for enhancing their performance. Using the control variable method, a powertrain model was constructed to simulate key dynamic indicators, including the full-load maximum speed, acceleration performance, and climbing capability. The simulation results demonstrate that the vehicle achieves a maximum speed of 200 km/h, accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h in 10.99 seconds, and can climb a slope of up to 31.7%, all of which exceed the average level of comparable models. In terms of economic performance, the study analyzes the impact of variables such as vehicle mass, drag coefficient, and seriesparallel battery ratio on energy consumption. It is found that the drag coefficient and vehicle mass have the most significant influence on energy consumption: a 0.05 reduction in the drag coefficient can lower energy consumption by 3%−5% under highspeed conditions, and power demand increases by 1 kW for every 1000 kg increase in vehicle mass. Additionally, the series-parallel battery ratio affects energy efficiency, with the optimal efficiency achieved when the ratio is around 12. Overall, this research confirms that reducing the drag coefficient, adopting lightweight design, and optimizing battery configuration are key approaches to improving the economic performance of electric vehicles, providing valuable data support for powertrain parameter optimization and performance enhancement.

title: A Probe Calibration Method for Coordinate Measuring Machines based on a Hybrid Temporal-Physical Constraint Model
authors: Xinfeng Lv, Weichao Guo, Xianpeng Zhang, Pengfei Qin
publish: 2025 8th World Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Intelligent Manufacturing (WCMEIM)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of limited accuracy and low efficiency in the probe calibration process of Coordinate Measuring Machines (CMMs), this paper proposes an intelligent calibration method based on a hybrid temporal-physical constraint network. By constructing a deep learning model that integrates multi-scale temporal feature extraction and a physical constraint mechanism, high-precision automatic calibration of probe parameters is achieved. This method first uses a hierarchical convolutional network to extract local and global features of the probe deformation signal, combined with a bidirectional LSTM network to model temporal dependencies; then it innovatively integrates the physical constraints of the measurement system into the network architecture, ensuring the physical rationality of the output parameters. Experimental results show that the RMS error of this method on the test set reaches 0.011 mm. Compared with traditional CNN and LSTM models, the proposed method improves calibration accuracy by 99.5% and 99.0%, respectively, while maintaining the same computational complexity.

title: Enhancing On-Body UHF Passive RFID Tags with an AMC-Inspired Surface
authors: Jéssyca Iasmyn Lucena Araujo, Danilo Freire de Souza Santos, Alexandre Jean René Serres, Antonio Marcus Nogueira Lima
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: In this work, we propose a passive UHF RFID tag for on-body applications, integrated with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> AMC-inspired surface. Finite Element Method electromagnetic simulation results show that the presence of the AMC-inspired surface improves impedance matching, reducing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left|\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1 1}}\right|$</tex> from -16.23 dB to -34.29 dB. A significant gain enhancement is also observed, increasing from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{1 1. 7 0} \mathbf{~ d B i}$</tex> without the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A M C}$</tex>-inspired surface to 1.55 dBi with the AMC-inspired surface. Furthermore, the average SAR on the human torso phantom decreases when the AMC-inspired surface is employed, complying with the ICNIRP limits. These results demonstrate the suitability of AMCinspired surfaces for on-body RFID applications.

title: Neurocognitive Evaluation of CNC Fabricated Islamic Ornamental Panels: Toward Emotionally Supportive Architectural Components
authors: Wardhana Wahyu Dharsono, Floriberta Binarti, Paulus Wisnu Anggoro
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: This pilot study quantifies neurocognitive responses to CNC-fabricated Islamic ornamental panels using electroencephalography (EEG). Seven participants (mean age <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27.4 \pm 4.2$</tex> years) viewed digital 3D renderings and physically interacted with embossed wooden panels featuring Turkish-Islamic floral motifs. EEG data (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \text{Hz}, 14$</tex> channels) measured six cognitive-emotional metrics: attention, engagement, excitement, interest, relaxation, and stress. Paired t-tests with FDR correction revealed that physical interaction increased relaxation by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-23 \%(95 \% \text{CI}: 12.3-25.1 \%, \mathrm{p}=0.008)$</tex> compared with digital observation. Panel <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C$</tex> achieved significantly higher engagement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(M=0.82 \pm 0.13$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.65 \pm 0.15$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d}=0.89, \mathrm{p}=0.027$</tex>) and interest (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{M}=0.84 \pm 0.09$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.69 \pm 0.13$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{d}=1.02, \mathrm{p}=0.012)$</tex> than other designs across both conditions. Results demonstrate that CNC fabrication preserves neurocognitive benefits of traditional Islamic ornamentation, offering empirical insights for culturally responsive architectural design.

title: Optimization of Detection Algorithms on a Mouse Movement Monitoring System Platform to Support Biomedical Research
authors: Sutrisno W. Ibrahim, Shoffin M. Zaky, Nanang Wiyono, Faisal Rahutomo, Subuh Pramono, Joko Hariyono, Muhammad H. Ibrahim, Meiyanto E. Sulistyo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: Biomedical research, particularly studies involving anxiety behavior testing on mouse using the Elevated Plus Maze (EPM), plays a pivotal role in advancing scientific knowledge and health technology. However, data collection and analyzing in these experiments is typically conducted manually, which is time-consuming and prone to bias. To address this issue, computer vision technology can be employed through modern object detection methods, such as YOLO (You Only Look Once), Faster R-CNN (Faster Region-Based Convolutional Neural Network), and RetinaNet, to track mouse movements in the EPM test. The evaluation was conducted by comparing the accuracy and inference time of each model. The experiment was conducted in the Anatomy Laboratory, Faculty of Medicine, Sebelas Maret University. The results of the study indicate that YOLOv8 offers the best balance between accuracy and inference speed, achieving an accuracy of 94.44% and an inference speed of 69 FPS. This advantage positions YOLOv8 as the primary choice for real-time monitoring systems that require rapid responses while maintaining robust detection performance. On the other hand, Faster R-CNN and RetinaNet demonstrated adequate accuracy but exhibited lower inference speeds, making them less suitable for real-time system applications.

title: PIN-Diode-Based Reconfigurable Metasurface for Dynamic Microwave Beam Control
authors: Igor Nogueira Olivio, Muhammad Saleem, Rafael A. Penchel, William O. F. Carvalho
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This paper presents a experimental validation for a PIN-diode-based reconfigurable metasurface (MTS) designed to dynamically control electromagnetic wave propagation. The MTS consists of an array of interconnected split-square-ring resonators (SSqRRs), enabling electronic beam-switching between two distinct spatial directions through controlled diode bias states. Experimental measurements demonstrate robust beam redirection at 5 GHz, with cross-talk (CT) exceeding 20 dB between the two propagation paths. The design demonstrates a compact and cost-effective platform for real-time reconfigurable beam control, suitable for wireless and sensing applications.

title: Toward Deterministic 6G HRLLC: A Multi-Agent DRL-Based Traffic Scheduling Method in HRLLC and TSN Converged Networks
authors: Zheng Sheng, Pengcheng Zhu, Jiamin Li, Dongming Wang, Xiaohu You
abstract: Compared with the fifth generation (5G) ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC), the sixth generation (6G) hyper reliable and low-latency communication (HRLLC) imposes stricter requirements on latency, reliability, and additionally, deterministic latency. The convergence of HRLLC and time-sensitive networking (TSN), termed HRLLC-TSN network, which deeply integrates the wireless network with TSN, thereby enabling cross-layer joint traffic scheduling and guaranteeing deterministic communication, is considered a promising technology to support mission-critical applications such as wireless Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT). This paper considers a wireless IIoT scenario using the HRLLC-TSN network with continuously moving industrial equipments and analyzes the complete data transmission process. Then, the strategy for ensuring deterministic latency is explained, and analytical closed-form expressions for end-to-end latency and reliability are obtained. From these expressions, a dynamic optimization problem is formulated to maximize the number of data frames successfully delivered to industrial equipments and an artificial intelligence method based on multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) is proposed to enable rapid and efficient traffic scheduling. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed MAPPO method outperforms other approaches in both performance and computational complexity, particularly when the number of industrial equipments is large.

title: FETCH: Deep Learning IoT-Fog Framework for Lower-Limb Disorders
authors: S. Madumidha, Mathimalar K, Raghul S, Kavin M, Priyadarshan S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Developed with advanced deep learning technology for portable and intelligent use, the Bone Strength Analyzer will measure bone integrity. The device uses a Node MCU microcontroller, MPU6050 motion sensor, and piezoelectric crystal to capture cerebellar movement and pressure data along with a connected optical sensor to capture either x-rays of the bone surface or nearinfrared images of the bone surface. This data will be combined into one multi-modal dataset, which will then be processed with a hybrid model of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). Using the spatial features extracted using the CNN from the bone images, along with the temporal variation in the sensor data using LSTM will enable to better classify bone strength over time into categories providing greater understanding about the health of bones. Using this system is non-invasive, highly efficient, and cost effective due to the use of intelligent diagnostics from own body's sensors, bridging the gap between biomedical sensing and health diagnostics Smart System.

title: Zero-Knowledge Identity Verification
authors: Pranav Kumar, Param Srivastava, Parth Singh, Nikita Gupta, Vivek Agarwal
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Everybody is these days plunging into decentralized applications, blockchain, and digital identity. And honestly, it's a rendering that the ancient method of authenticating yourself looks nice, outdated and risky. Whenever you give up your personal info, you are just wishing it does not find its way into a data leak or get misused. Precisely, this is the reason that we constructed a new path to manage identity checks that really care about your privacy. This is what we are doing: our framework is based on Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS) and Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs). To begin with, we generate offchain attestations based on the EIP-712 standard. Your signature remains verifiable; however, your confidential information doesn't go anywhere and through which we squeeze these attestations. SP1 using zero-knowledge virtual machine (zkVM) this step checks everything twice, the construction, the encryption, the logic, all of it. When it's all good then the system will spit a short, non-interactive Groth16 or Plonk (if)SNARK proofs known as zero-knowledge proofs (you're curious). This evidence makes things private and at the same time accurate. There is the interesting side of it: you can check it immediately in your local devices using a super lightweight browser or with a Node.js app WebAssembly verifier. It does not require any middlemen and there is no need for extra trust. You can send evidences, in case you wish to using Solidity contract on-chain. This allows the system to issue new fraudulent statements such as isOver18 = true without displaying your actual age or any other personal data. So what does this mean? You earn greater confidence, enhanced interoperability and connections through decentralize systems. Transparency is what you have with this of ZKP attestation and actual privacy. It's actually practical, as well, can be used with KYC, DeFi, age-requiring app checks, and secured access controls. Essentially, it is a huge leap higher on behalf of anonymity and trust in electronic self.

title: Deepfake Detection Using Xception Model with Multi-Modal Ensemble Learning and Bayesian Fusion
authors: Ashutosh Bhushan, Anamica Singh, Anshul Srivastava, Lipi Srivastava, Anshika Prakash Mishra, Riya Yadav, Rita Mansukhlal Kotecha
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Deepfake technology has advanced rapidly, posing significant threats to digital media integrity. This paper presents a robust deep-fake detection system combining multi-modal ensemble learning and Bayesian probabilistic fusion to improve detection accuracy. This program integrates Xception-based deepfake classifier for visual artifacts, Facial emotion analysis using DeepFace, Temporal motion features via MMAction2's SlowFast network and Bayesian uncertainty modeling using Pyro. This program evaluates real-time webcam feeds, static images, and video files, demonstrating improved robustness compared to single-model approaches. This method achieves an F1-score of 0.92 on the FaceForensics++ benchmark while maintaining real-time performance.

title: Design Strategies for Gain Enhancement in ITO-Based Optically Transparent Antennas
authors: Bernardo Dominguez, Fábio Silva, Daniel Albuquerque, Pedro Pinho
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: Optically Transparent Antennas (OTAs) based on Indium Tin Oxide (ITO) enable the integration of wireless functionality into everyday surfaces while preserving aesthetics. However, their performance is fundamentally limited by the high resistivity of transparent conductors. This work investigates two practical gain-enhancement strategies-a transparent superstrate with a directing ring and partial substrate removal-applied to a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4 ~ G H z}$</tex> ITO microstrip patch antenna. Simulation results show that, through careful geometrical optimization, a realized gain above 2 dBi can be achieved while maintaining optical transparency.

title: Research on Distributed Photovoltaic Grid Connection Path Optimization System
authors: Jiaming Duan, Shan Lu, Tinglong Xie, Yingzhang Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: In response to the overvoltage phenomenon caused by the large-scale integration of distributed photovoltaic into the distribution network, the distributed photovoltaic grid-connected system is analyzed, and a model of distributed photovoltaic gridconnected path optimization system is proposed. The simulation calculation was carried out using MATLAB software, with the optimization goals of minimum voltage peak difference and optimal grid loss to obtain the regulation scheme, and finally the simulation based on the actual data of Yingluoba substation verified that the research strategy control was flexible and feasible.

title: A Step-Wise Integral Equivalent Model for Efficient EMT Simulation of Power Converters Under Variable-Step Algorithms
authors: Bohan Pang, Wenzhuo Liu, Ke Xu, Yong Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Accurate and efficient modeling of power electronic converters is critical for electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulation of HVDC transmission and renewable energy sources. However, existing averaged-switching models such as the timeaveraging method (TAM) and voltage interpolation (VI) are incompatible with critical damping adjustment (CDA) or multistep variable-step (MSVS) algorithms due to their reliance on fixed integration schemes. This paper proposes a step-wise integral equivalent (SIE) model that ensures compatibility with variable step-size algorithms and integration method switching. The proposed model is applicable to both voltage source converters (VSCs) and line-commutated converters (LCCs). Simulation results on a 2.5 MW 3-level ANPC-based battery energy storage system and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 800 \text{kV}$</tex> Ximeng-Taizhou UHVDC system demonstrate that the SIE model achieves high accuracy in reproducing switching dynamics and harmonics, even under fixed time steps as large as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 / 6$</tex> of the switching period. Compared to variable-step detailed models, the SIE model achieves a speed-up of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.1 \times$</tex> in the VSC-based case and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5 \times$</tex> in the LCC-based case. The proposed method provides a practical and compatible solution for large-scale EMT simulation platforms like PSModel.

title: SOULSYNC: An AI-Driven Emotional and Cognitive Support System for Alzheimer's Care
authors: Beaulah David, G Krishna, Shafna thasni K, Shreya S, Sona Krishna K
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Alzheimer disease is a progressive disorder that affects memory, cognition, and emotional stability hence increasing care giver dependency and lowering the quality of life. The majority of available mobile health apps are based on the principle of reminders and schedule planning, without emotional and cognitive support. This paper suggests SOULSYNC, an AI-based mobile emotion assistant that would improve both cognitive and emotional health of patients with Alzheimer disease. The system operates offline-first architecture in which patient information such as reminders, mood data, memory content, and activity log are stored locally with the help of SQLite and updated with the cloud backend when the network connection is present. The analysis of emotion trends and routine patterns are applied to create individualized interventions including music therapy, cognitive games, and relaxing activities. A caregiver-related feature is a source of real-time notifications, emergency messages, and health-related information to maintain constant assistance. The flow application encompasses secure authentication, dashboardbased interaction, emotive tracking, cognitive interaction and intelligent feedback.

title: Power Communication Data Mid-End Driven Multi-Source Data Fusion and Power Grid Fault Prediction
authors: Yuanjie Liu, Manchun Lu, Yuben Bao, Bo Li, Zhihua Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Modern power systems feature increasingly complex and heterogeneous communication infrastructures, necessitating the development of intelligent, real-time fault prediction solutions. As diverse telemetry, control, and network signaling continuously generate between substations, routers, and edge nodes, traditional centralized monitoring methods prove inadequate in correlating multi-source data to identify potential failures. This study proposes a power communication data mid-end-driven architecture that fuses heterogeneous communication data via graph-based modeling and applies deep learning to predict grid-level fault scenarios. The framework constructs dynamic fault graphs capturing structural and behavioral relationships among communication devices. These graphs are processed by Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to identify evolving failure patterns and risk propagation within communication topologies. A multi-layer system design encompassing data acquisition, semantic fusion, graph generation, and inference - enables end-to-end predictive orchestration within a secure and scalable architecture. Deployed on a simulated smart grid testbed with five regional nodes, the system achieves significant performance gains: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+10.6 \%$</tex> improvement in fault prediction accuracy versus baseline models, 28.8% reduction in prediction latency, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>60 \%$</tex> decrease in false alarm rates, Enhanced cross-domain fault isolation. These results validate the efficacy of the data mid-end-driven fusion approach in delivering proactive, resilient fault management capabilities tailored to dynamic power communication environments.

title:
Composition and Temperature Control of Spin Canting in Mn
                    <sub>3+x</sub>
                    Pt Noncollinear Antiferromagnet
authors: Dennis J. X. Lin, Bee Chun Lim, Jian Rui Soh, James Louremban, Hui Ru Tan, Hnin Yu Yu Ko, Mingsheng Zhang, Hang Khume Tan, Lisen Huang, Shaohai Chen, Pin Ho
abstract: Noncollinear antiferromagnetic (NCAF) materials such as L12-Mn3Pt are attractive for energy-efficient sensing, memory, and computing applications. Here, (001)-textured Mn3+xPt NCAF films are fabricated using a [Mn (tMn : 0.6 – 0.8 nm)/Pt 0.1 nm]30 multilayer structure, where the Mn thickness (tMn) is systematically modulated to control the composition and magnetic structure. Increasing the tMn adjusts the Mn:Pt ratio from 2.8:1 to 4.3:1, leading to the partial replacement of Pt lattice sites by the excess Mn atoms and thereby modifying the magnetic structure. The anomalous Hall effect loops of the multilayers exhibit polarity reversal at different critical transition temperature (Tcri), reducing from 235 to 135 K when tMn is increased from 0.6 to 0.7 nm. Meanwhile, the X-ray magnetic circular dichroism spectra of these multilayers exhibit similar temperature-driven polarity changes near Tcri. Collectively, these results indicate a composition- and temperature-controlled crossover from a predominantly NCAF to spin-canted NCAF order with ferromagnetic-like contribution.

title: Securing Blue Carbon: A Framework for Transparent MRV in Mangrove Restoration
authors: Devina Fitria Maharani, Andry Alamsyah, Herry Irawan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Traditional Monitoring, Reporting, and Verification (MRV) systems for blue carbon projects, particularly mangrove restoration, suffer from significant challenges. These systems are often centralized, costly, and inefficient, resulting in a lack of transparency, a high risk of data manipulation, and delays in carbon credit issuance. This paper proposes a novel blockchain-based MRV framework designed to address these critical flaws. A Proof-of-Concept (PoC) was developed that leverages a hybrid architecture, integrating Polygon for on-chain transactions, the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) for decentralized off-chain data storage, and registry rule-based Smart Contracts for automated verification. The framework is designed to manage credits as ERC-1155 tokens, which efficiently allows for the differentiation of credit vintages and project types on-chain. Functional validation of the PoC, conducted in a controlled EVM environment, demonstrated a 100% success rate across all on-chain stages, from data anchoring to VVB attestation. Performance analysis confirmed the economic viability of the design, with core operations, such as verification and anchoring, incurring low gas costs. The proposed framework establishes an immutable audit trail and enhances accountability through digital signatures, significantly improving the transparency, efficiency, and integrity of carbon credits for mangrove restoration projects. This work provides a critical foundation for scalable, high-integrity blue carbon markets by solving key data-handling and verification bottlenecks.

title: Machine Learning Comparison for Coffee Plantation Mapping in Jawa Barat’s Mountainous Terrain
authors: Anggun Tridawati, Ketut Wikantika, Trimuji Susantoro, Soni Darmawan, Muhammad Firman Ghazali, Ketut Tomy Suhari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Coffee, a highly traded commodity, is cultivated globally in 70 tropical countries which has decreased from 2019 to 2020 on several continents. Therefore, coffee plantation yields must be monitored using remote sensing. Mapping in remote sensing generally can be done by using digital classification approaches. The choice of algorithm for classifying remote sensing data significantly impacts the accuracy. Random forest and support vector machine are two extensively used machine learning algorithms for mapping coffee plantations. Previous comparisons have shown inconsistent results, and no prior study has specifically compared RF and SVM for coffee plantation mapping. We used the Pansharp GeoEye-1, Digital Elevation Model, and Sentinel-2 (time series) to compare the performance of these two techniques for mapping coffee plantations. The results clearly show that the random forest algorithm outperforms support vector machine in terms of accuracy. The random forest algorithm obtained 79.33% overall accuracy and a kappa index of 0.77, whereas the support vector machine algorithm achieved 72.40% overall accuracy and a kappa value of 0.69. In terms of memory usage, during the classification process, random forest requires less storage space compared to support vector machine. Consequently, it can be concluded that the random forest algorithm is the superior choice for accurately mapping coffee plantations.

title: Vehicle Trajectory Reconstruction Using Variational Inference Amid Measurement Loss and Non-Gaussian Noise: Algorithm and Experiments
authors: Jian Chen, Jiacheng He, Bei Peng, Qing Liang, Hongwei Wang, Mingzhu Wei
abstract: Trajectory reconstruction is essential for localizing and tracking maritime vehicles, but non-Gaussian process and measurement noises, as well as time-varying measurement loss, are present in the marine environment. To enhance TR accuracy in such challenging scenarios, this article proposes a state sequence estimation method based on the Rauch-Tung-Striebel smoother, which fully leverages the distributional characteristics of outliers. This approach models the likelihood density of non- Gaussian noise using two multivariate Laplace distributions. Unknown noise covariances are then modeled via an inverse Wishart distribution, while unknown measurement losses and their occurrence probabilities are addressed using Bernoulli and Beta distributions, respectively. On this basis, Variational Bayes inference is utilized to decouple fused anomalous processes, and infer states within a hierarchical model, yielding the proposed robust smoother. Experiments with an Unmanned Underwater Vehicle and numerical simulations demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of this proposed method for TR tasks.

title: Capacity Analysis and Robust Topology Design of LEO Satellite Constellation with Node/Link Failure
authors: Lei Guo, Junyu Liu, Min Sheng, Yang Li, Jiandong Li
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks are increasingly central to wide-area communication services, yet their capacity is highly sensitive to the inherent vulnerability of satellite and inter-satellite link (ISL) failures. This paper develops an analytical framework for characterizing network capacity under arbitrary failure patterns. Building on bisection cut-set analysis, we formalize capacity bottlenecks through graph partitioning and derive rigorous upper bounds on the network capacity. Within this framework, we further identify and characterize the maximum failure cut set, i.e., a critical subset of ISLs whose removal yields the most severe capacity degradation, and establish its necessity in achieving the worst-case capacity bound. Furthermore, the analytical framework has been shown to be applicable to general LEO networks, and a closed-form expression for capacity degradation has been derived using a 2D torus topology as an example, which takes into account both the number and spatial distribution of the satellite or ISL failure. The results indicate that maximizing the minimum bisection cut set capacity is fundamental to strengthening the intrinsic robustness of the LEO network. This paper provides a theoretical methodology for capacity assessment and offers principled guidelines for topology design in satellite constellations.

title: Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning for Mucormycosis Risk Prediction
authors: Kavitha U, Sreejith S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: High mortality rates and severe angio-invasion are hallmarks of mucormycosis, a potentially lethal fungal illness. Accurate risk assessment and early diagnosis are essential for better patient outcomes. In order to identify mucormycosis by serum analysis, a number of sophisticated diagnostic techniques have been investigated recently, such as those that make use of Fourier Transform Infrared (FTIR) sensors. At the same time, advancements in clinical Natural Language Processing (NLP) have made it possible to glean insightful information from unstructured electronic health records (EHRs). Clinical narratives can be converted into structured data that machine learning (ML) algorithms can use by using feature engineering models and word embedding techniques. For patients with mucormycosis, these AI-powered methods aid in risk classification, severity evaluation, and early detection. Additionally, the incorporation of explainable AI facilitates clinical decision-making and improves transparency. This paper looks at how NLP and ML are currently used in healthcare, specifically in relation to predicting the risk of mucormycosis.

title: Detection and Classification of Retinopathy of Prematurity on Multi-Source Fundus Images Using DenseROPNet
authors: Sadad Hasan, Yasir Arafat Prodhan, Mohammed Imamul Hassan Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: AI-based disease detection and stage classification is becoming a norm in today's technological world. Unlike the traditional diagnosis, modern diagnostics are showing better accuracy, faster results and accurate predictions using different CNN models. This study focuses on developing and training a CNN model with attention mechanisms which is capable of diagnosing Retinopathy of Prematurity (ROP), a major reason of blindness in infants. Accurate detection of this disease and its stages can make the treatment of it more effective. More importantly, manual detection of early stages of ROP can be quite challenging as the signs are subtle and ROP diagnosis relies on qualitative analysis which may create confusion while taking decisions. Keeping all these in mind, DenseROPNet has been trained and tested on two different fundus image datasets of subjects with two different ethnicities. The proposed model reached the highest accuracy of 99.13% overcoming the accomplishment of most of the existing studies. The motto of this study has been to propose and develop a CNN model which beside performing significantly well, will be explainable. With that in mind, analysis using Explainable AI has also been done to ensure the model's understanding of the dataset is not only accurate but also meaningful.

title: Experimental Methodology for Evaluating Electroporation Effectiveness in Bacterial Control
authors: N. Rodríguez, G. Talero, J. Sierra, S. Huertas, E. Quintero, S. Avila, N. Mora
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: Pulsed electromagnetic fields have been used for cell electroporation in different contexts. The most used criteria for designing electroporation waveforms are usually the electric field peak amplitude and pulse duration. In this work, sinusoidal electromagnetic pulses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48 \text{kV} / \mathrm{m}$</tex> and variable duration are proposed for controlling bacterial growth (E. coli) in drinking water. The number of pulses is increased to achieve the same energy density as in a commercial electroporator, allowing for the evaluation of the effectiveness of this waveform in the electroporation process.

title: Mapping the Evolution of AI Applications in Sustainable Supply Chain Management: A Bibliometric Review
authors: Kapil Garg
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Sustainable supply chain management is becoming more important as organizations aim to meet global sustainable development goals. Moreover, the incorporation of artificial intelligence in the sustainable supply chain management sector not only fosters the sustainability of the supply chain but also handles the massive amounts of data generated by various industrial operations. However, the field is still evolving and understanding how artificial intelligence is being applied to sustainability challenges requires a review of existing research. Therefore, this study reviews the literature and map the evolution of artificial intelligence applications in sustainable supply chain management. For the purpose, a bibliometric analysis of publication trends, keyword cooccurrence, co-authorship, co-citations, and bibliographic coupling of 464 publications indexed by Scopus related to artificial intelligence applications in sustainable supply chains have been conducted. Scopus and VOS viewer has been used to extract the data and graphically represent the results respectively. The results indicate that this is an emerging field of research wherein India leads in number of publications, citations and global research collaborations, followed by the United States. Other active countries include China, United Kingdom, Germany, and France. Despite, no single Institutions dominates the research field, which indicates that research in this field is dispersed. Future research can discover deeper integration of artificial intelligence with real-time data and circular supply chain models to enhance sustainability outcomes.

title: Research on Semi Automated Metrology Technology Based on Matrix Formula of Rotary Parts
authors: Dianliang Chen, Wenbiao Yang, Xianjian Tan, Yuanjun Gong, Yuanyuan Liu, Xu Zhang
publish: 2025 8th World Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Intelligent Manufacturing (WCMEIM)
abstract: At present, for small diameter rotary parts, the problems such as large workload of process detection and final measurement, frequent manual loading and unloading, and long waiting period of measurement detection have seriously affected the production and delivery of parts. In order to achieve an economically efficient and quality stable measurement and testing method, this paper proposes a research and application method of matrix based semi-automatic measurement technology for rotary parts. The device adopts fixture design, tray design, conveyor line design, and semi-automatic control system, with baffles and bearing seats as typical parts, to achieve the detection logic circuit of the entire part. After calculation, the efficiency of semiautomatic measurement has increased by more than 28% compared to manual detection methods, and the measurement efficiency has significantly improved, providing new ideas for the matrix based semi-automatic measurement field of rotary parts.

title: Design and Simulation Analysis of Electricity Fee Settlement Mechanism in Electricity Market Based on Central Counterparty
authors: Jianghong Nie, Ling Gan, Qing Xie, Ze Ye, Minting Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The construction of the electricity spot market has raised issues regarding the corresponding electricity fee settlement models and mechanism transformation. Currently, China's electricity market adopts a model where market entities entrust grid enterprises with electricity fee settlement, and grid enterprises implement decoupled settlement between the generation and user sides. Although this model partially reflects the objective requirements for electricity fee settlement in the electricity market, it has problems such as unclear legal relationships in electricity fee settlement, insufficient functional combination of the settlement mechanism, and ambiguous settlement risk management mechanisms. To meet the national needs of strengthening metering and settlement work in the electricity market, based on summarizing and analyzing the characteristics of electricity fee settlement in the electricity market, this paper proposes introducing a central counterparty (CCP) settlement mechanism into electricity fee settlement in the electricity market. It systematically standardizes and optimizes the current electricity fee settlement mechanism in the electricity market from four aspects: contract substitution, multilateral netting settlement, margin system, and settlement risk management. Metering models for these four mechanisms are established, and a simulation calculation analysis is conducted using market data from a certain province as an example. The advantages of the electricity fee settlement mechanism based on CCP in improving settlement efficiency and reducing settlement risks are verified. The design of the electricity fee settlement mechanism in the electricity market based on CCP has important reference value for standardizing and optimizing the current electricity fee settlement work in China's electricity market.

title: The Smart Fall Detection and Emergency Alert System
authors: M.E. Shankar R, Avishkar G, Mohamed Irfan S, Sathiya C
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Falls are one of the leading causes of serious injury and accidental death, particularly among elderly individuals, patients with mobility or neurological disorders, and people engaged in daily activities such as walking, working or driving. Delays in receiving medical assistance after a fall can significantly increase health complications and fatality risks. To address this problem, this project proposes a Smart Fall Detection and Emergency Alert System implemented as a wearable glove, designed for continuous and comfortable use. The system uses an ADXL345 accelerometer to monitor motion and detect sudden falls, while an Arduino process sensor data and control system operations. Upon fall detection, an audible alert is generated through a speaker to notify nearby individuals. If the user does not respond within 1 minute, the system automatically sends an SMS alert with GPS location to a caregiver using a GSM module. If there is still no response after 2 minutes, the system escalates the emergency by placing a direct call to ambulance services (108). Experimental testing under various movement and fall scenarios demonstrates reliable detection, timely alert generation, and effective emergency communication. The proposed system reduces response time, minimizes injury risk, and enhances personal safety. This wearable solution is suitable for elderly care, patient monitoring, workplace safety and personal emergency protection, offering a practical and affordable approach to fall management.

title: Numerical Design of a 26 GHz Exposure System for Real-Time Electrophysiological Experiments
authors: Carmen Pisano, Noemi Dolciotti, Alessia Toschi, Alessandra Paffi, Micaela Liberti, Francesca Apollonio
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: The rapid development of wireless technologies has led to the adoption of 5G networks particularly in high-frequency bands such as FR2, which operates in the millimeter-wave (mmW) region. The addition of new frequencies, such as 26 GHz, has once again raised the issue of radiofrequency electromagnetic field (RF-EMF) exposure in both public and occupational environments. However, existing biological data on mmW frequencies are insufficient for thorough health evaluations. To bridge this gap, new in vitro studies are required. This work introduces the design and electromagnetic characterization of a 26 GHz exposure system tailored for real-time electrophysiological analysis of living cells.

title: Blockchain-Enabled AI System for Fake News Detection and Trustworthy Verification
authors: Sumit Kumar Mishra, Mohit Kumar, Naman Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The issue of fake news has become a regular occurrence in these digital times, impacting the way people make their choices and spreading mistrust in the virtual world. Previous studies have extensively researched various machine learning and deep learning techniques for recognizing deceptive information; however, most of them fail to provide clear validation, avoid data manipulation, and engage users in the validation process. The current research presents a blockchain powered AI system that will enhance the authenticity of online news. The system unifies the user interface on the MERN stack, a special machine learning-focused service to analyze news content, and blockchain smart contracts to maintain the verification records. Every article provided is analyzed with the help of an AI model, which analyzes linguistic and semantic clues, and the result is recorded on a blockchain ledger, which is impossible to alter. It also has a mechanism based on reputation to ensure a responsible participation of both the publishers and the readers. The proposed architecture will be more reliable and transparent in the process of news authentication in massive online ecosystems because it combines decentralized storage, automated checks, and personal responsibility of users.

title: Marine Debris Segmentation Using Swin U-Net with Spectral-Spatial Attention
authors: J Jeyalakshmi, Pasumarti Vamsi Krishna, Chavva Bala Kuswanth Reddy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Ocean pollution is a major global issue, threatening marine life, ecosystems, and coastal communities. Large amounts of plastic waste enter the oceans yearly and break into small, widespread debris that is hard to remove. Traditional detection methods using ships, aircraft, or manual image labeling are slow, costly, and limited in coverage. Satellite imagery offers a faster and wider alternative, but detecting marine debris automatically remains challenging due to changing ocean conditions and similar-looking natural objects. This research proposes a deep learning approach using Swin U-Net enhanced with a Spectral-Spatial Attention module to improve debris detection. The model considers both spectral (color) and spatial (texture and shape) features, allowing it to focus on relevant areas and reduce background noise. It was trained on the MARIDA dataset using five Sentinel-2 bands (red, green, blue, NIR, SWIR) to better distinguish plastics from natural materials. The system produces accurate segmentation maps showing debris locations, outperforming traditional U-Net or CNN models in handling complex ocean conditions. This method supports large-scale monitoring, real-time ocean surveillance, and informed cleanup efforts, providing a foundation for automated global marine debris tracking and sustainable ocean management.

title: AI.R Taletorium: Preserving Culture Through AI Storytelling

title: Adoption of AI-Enabled Educational Tools in Schools: A Big Tech—Driven Transformation
authors: Meghna Sharma, Samarth Sharma, Ved Prakash, Vineet Senger, Manikant Dubey, Vandana Gupta
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has witnessed a rapid evolution in its field and has brought forth a revolution in the educational segment among schools all over the world. However, the adoption of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A I}$</tex> in educational institutions can be very unequal as there are a variety of internal and external determinants. In an attempt to bridge the gap that exists among these determinants and the adoption process of AI in educational institutions, this study hypothesizes and verifies an overall model that tests the impacts of the antecedent determinants such as the determinants of technological readiness, teacher competency and acceptance, institutional support and leadership, influences of big tech platforms, and assurance regarding data security and ethics in the adoption process of AI and the overall impacts of adoption as an antecedent towards the determinants influencing performance improvement. With a quantitative cross-sectional research design and a target population of 1,500 education stakeholders, including faculty, students, and EdTech practitioners, the study was conducted via a structured questionnaire utilizing a fivepoint likert scale. The results highlight the crucial mediating function of AI adoption intention as a behavioral driver connecting technology readiness, support, views on ethics, and platform power to realworld learning outcomes.

title: Intelli-Query: A Large Language Model System for Semantic Retrieval and Document Understanding
authors: Suhana Bansal, Tanisha Choudhary, Ravi Shankar Pal
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The rapid growth of unstructured digital documents has created significant challenges in retrieving accurate information using traditional keyword-based search systems. Organizations often struggle to efficiently interpret lengthy reports, contracts, and policy documents, resulting in increased manual effort and delayed decision-making. This paper presents INTELLI-QUERY, an intelligent document understanding and semantic retrieval platform designed to provide context-aware query responses. The system integrates a Python-based backend, transformer-based sentence embeddings, FAISS vector indexing, and a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline powered by Large Language Models (LLMs). Intelli-Query automatically ingests documents, performs preprocessing and semantic chunking, and stores embeddings in a vector database to enable similarity-based retrieval. When a user submits a natural-language query, the system identifies intent, retrieves the most relevant document sections, and generates evidence-grounded responses. The platform also supports multi-question batch processing and structured JSON outputs for downstream applications. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that embedding-based retrieval combined with LLM reasoning significantly improves contextual relevance and reduces hallucination compared to direct LLM answering. The system achieved a context relevance score of 0.81 and reduced incorrect responses by approximately 70 %. This work highlights the effectiveness of combining semantic search and language models in transforming static documents into interactive knowledge systems, enabling efficient enterprise information access and supporting accurate decision-making.

title: The Nasreddin Hodja Principle and the Mathematics of Deep Learning [From the Editor]
authors: Tülay Adali

title: An End-to-End Multimodal Deep Learning Model for Fake Video Detection and Authenticity Scoring
authors: GopalaKrishnan K, Kavitha K, Karthika S, Umamaheswari A
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Deep fake videos are often manipulated to be a realistic content which becomes a huge threat to security, media and forensic fields. This paper uses a stage by stage deep learning models pipeline to detect the manipulated content in a efficient and accurate way. The stages include the CNN and LSTM model to process the frame level feature extraction and temporal dependencies. GAN-based discriminator is the next stage where the deeper inconsistencies are focused. Contrastive learning is to compare the output that is generated by the two stages with the real video for better accuracy training. The proposed method will give an effective performance rather than the non-hybrid model and suitable for diverse and real-time datasets.

title: Evaluation of the Radiated Electric Field of a Tri-band Circularly Polarized Antenna Integrating a Ferrite Material using Infrared Thermography
authors: Carrizales-Juarez Ricardo, Laffont Adrien, Faure Stéphane, Leon-Valdes Jehison, Monediere Thierry, Huitema Laure
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper evaluates the radiated electric field (E-field) intensity of a tri-band circularly polarized antenna using infrared thermography (IRT). The radiated fields at each operational band are measured and compared with electromagnetic simulations. We demonstrate how IRT measurements’ fast, non-invasive nature enables rapid validation of the antenna’s operating modes. Additionally, the antenna performance is analyzed for the case of biased and unbiased ferrite, demonstrating how ferrite substrates allow for obtaining radiation characteristics unavailable on conventional dielectric substrates. Furthermore, we show how a proper interpretation of the E-field cartography obtained at a very short distance from the antenna provides insight into the radiated polarization state and gives enough information to evaluate the on-axis gain of the antenna.

title: Lightning Image Segmentation Method Based on Improved U-Net
authors: Ziyi Zhang, Zeliang Wu, Hong Wu, Fangwei Ren, Zhengxian Du, Yongpeng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: To address the problems of low segmentation accuracy and the inability of existing methods to meet engineering application requirements, this paper proposes a lightweight lightning image segmentation model called CMM-UNet. Based on the encoder-decoder architecture of U-Net, the model employs MobileNetV3 as the encoder to reduce model complexity, and integrates multi-scale convolution (MSCB) and convolutionattention fusion mechanisms (CAFM) to enhance the perception of multi-scale lightning features and weak, elongated discharge structures. To tackle the common class imbalance issue in lightning images, a hybrid loss function is adopted. Ablation experiments validate the effectiveness of these improvements, showing significant enhancements in both computational efficiency and segmentation accuracy. The final CMM-UNet model achieves a Dice coefficient of 0.732, an improvement of 0.088 over the original U-Net's 0.644, while reducing the number of parameters from 1 7. 2 6 M to 3. 8 4 M, demonstrating its suitability for lightweight and high-precision engineering applications.

title: AI-Driven Smart Mesh Networks for Sustainable Rural Connectivity
authors: S. Umamaheswari, Pranesh. S, Krithikesh. S, Rithika. K, Neha Fathima. S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: In the modern world, connectivity plays an important role. There are a lot of challenges faced in both rural and urban areas without a proper network connection. Mesh networks are essential in providing full network coverage, particularly over undulating terrains which present immense challenges. While mesh networks themselves are quite popular, the introduction of artificial intelligence into route optimization will speed this connection to a new level. AI based network will go under self-learning on changing network conditions to deliver the best route available for better transmission. In the city, they improve connectivity in an itinerant environment, whereas in rural areas, they can establish stable connectivity over heterogeneous terrain.

title: Optimising I2V Propagation in VANETs-A Broadcast Frequency and Mobility Analysis for BSAD Protocol
authors: Kifayat Ullah, Waqar Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: The performance of Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) is largely dependent on robust data propagation methods. This paper addresses the specific challenges of the Infrastructure-to-Vehicle (I2V) communication for a new Bus-based Services Advertisement (BSAD) Protocol. BSAD utilises the predefined routes of public buses to act as data units, disseminating service information from a fixed Road Side Unit (RSU). Employing a state-of-the-art simulation framework, this paper evaluates the performance of BSAD on a multi-lane highway, focusing on the effects of broadcast frequency and bus speed on propagation success. A key finding is that a transmission rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> ensures complete packet delivery to all buses, thereby resolving the issue of data loss in fast-moving environments, improving reliability and operational efficiency for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I 2 V}$</tex> propagation.

title: An Evaluation and Optimization Model for Enterprise Carbon Emission Performance Based on Carbon Efficiency Code
authors: Xiang Lv, Hui Chen, Yunwang Hu, Xiaofeng Zhao, Hui Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: With the country's carbon peaking and carbon neutrality goals, the power industry, with high proportion of carbon emissions, puts forward higher requirements for scientific carbon emission performance evaluation and low-carbon optimization. In this paper, a carbon emission performance evaluation and optimizing model for power enterprises based on CEC (Carbon Efficiency Code) is proposed. Firstly, a multidimensional index system of carbon emission performance including corresponding influencing factors on carbon intensity, raw fuel structure, power generation efficiency and proportion of clean energy is constructed. The entropy weight method is introduced to determine the weight of each index and evaluate the carbon efficiency level of enterprises quantitatively. Then, a DEA (Data Envelopment Analysis) model is established to determine the carbon efficiency frontier and analyze the key low-carbon influencing factors via GRA (Grey Relational Analysis). Finally, a multi-objective optimization model for minimizing carbon emission and maximizing power generation economy is established and solved by an IPSO (Improved Particle Swarm Optimization) algorithm. The case study of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> typical coal-fired power plants shows that the proposed model can accurately and efficiently identify the units with high emissions and low CEC, and put forward targeted optimizing strategies of low-carbon operation with an average improvement of 6.8%. The research results can provide theoretical basis and practical guidance for precise carbon emission control and low-carbon operation in the power industry.
