title: CurbAI: Making Policy-Safe LLM Agents Work in Real-World Curbside Delivery
authors: Dibya Jyoti Mishra, Minav Suresh Patel, Likhesh Bramhanwade
abstract: Curbside delivery is one of the most operationally constrained and policy-sensitive layers in last-mile logistics. Although recent large language model (LLM) agents have demonstrated promise in decision-making tasks, their unconstrained use in deployment-oriented delivery operations raises concerns about safety, compliance, and reliability. This study presents CurbAI, a policy-safe LLM-orchestrated control plane that integrates probabilistic reasoning with deterministic policy validation and fallback mechanisms for curbside delivery decisions. Unlike end-to-end LLM planners, CurbAI constrains LLM reasoning to bounded candidate sets and enforces municipal, operational, and safety constraints through explicit policy gates. We evaluated CurbAI using a simulation-based study across 30 delivery routes spanning three regions (the US, EU, and JP), with heterogeneous stop densities, curb conditions, and operational profiles. The results demonstrate consistent route completion, controlled fallback behavior under uncertainty, and stable dwell-time distributions across regions, validating the robustness of policy-constrained LLM orchestration for real-world curbside delivery. These findings suggest that policy-constrained LLM control planes can augment last-mile logistics systems while preserving deterministic safety, compliance, and operational guarantees.

title: Attention-ResUNet and EfficientSASM-UNet: UNet-based frameworks for Lung and Nodule segmentation
authors: Muhammad Abdullah, Furqan Shaukat, Muhammad Hamza Zafar, Said Jadid Abdulkadir, Ahmed Omer Ahmed Ismail
abstract: Lung cancer has been one of the major threats across the world, with the highest mortalities. Computer-aided detection (CAD) can aid early detection and thus increase the survival rate. Accurate lung parenchyma segmentation (to include the juxta-pleural nodules) and lung nodule segmentation, the primary symptom of lung cancer, play a crucial role in the overall accuracy of the Lung CAD pipeline. Lung nodule segmentation is quite challenging due to the diverse nodule types and other inhibiting structures within the lung lobes. Traditional machine/deep learning methods suffer from generalization and robustness. Recent Vision Language Models/Foundation Models perform well at the anatomical level but struggle on fine-grained segmentation tasks, and their semi-automatic nature limits their effectiveness in real-time clinical scenarios. In this paper, we propose two novel, fully automatic methods for accurate 3D segmentation of lung parenchyma and lung nodules. The proposed architecture is an attention-based, fully convolutional UNet with residual blocks at each encoder-decoder state. Dilated convolutions at each encoder-decoder stage allow the model to capture broader context without increasing computational cost. For Lung Nodule Segmentation, an Efficient-SASM (Self-Attentive Similarity Module)- based 3D-UNet is proposed, optimized for easy deployment. The proposed methods have been extensively evaluated on one of the largest publicly available datasets, namely LUNA16, and compared with recent notable work in the domain using standard performance metrics such as Dice score and IOU. The results show that the proposed Lung Segmentation method outperforms state-of-the-art methods, while the proposed Nodule Segmentation technique achieves comparable results. The source code, datasets, and pre-processed data can be accessed using the link: https://github.com/EMeRALDsNRPU/Attention-Based-3D-ResUNet.

title: Machine Learning-Based Evaluation of Cleft Lip and Palate Severity from Facial Symmetry and Speech Analysis
authors: Malena Loza, Felipe Grijalva, Diego S. Benítez, Francisco Manuel Melgarejo-Meseguer, José Luis Rojo-Álvarez
abstract: Cleft Lip and Palate (CLP) significantly affects facial esthetics and speech production, presenting challenges for objective severity assessment, particularly after surgical interventions. Current evaluations rely on subjective clinical judgment, limiting reproducibility. To address this issue, this study proposes a dual-modality machine learning based framework that integrates facial symmetry and speech-derived features for the objective classification of the severity of CLP. The research follows an observational, cross-sectional, and exploratory design with a quantitative approach, aiming to identify and characterize visual and auditory features extracted from facial videos of children with and without CLP, to explore potential severity patterns. A novel multimodal dataset was constructed from 99 Spanish-speaking children, of whom 39.4% were diagnosed with CLP condition. Severity labels (mild, moderate, severe) were assigned at the subject level by three raters: an experienced CLP surgeon and two trained non-clinical raters, using majority voting. Inter- and intra-rater reliability were assessed using established agreement metrics, demonstrating consistent severity labeling across raters and sessions. Facial features were extracted using a Convolutional Neural Network and reduced through Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection to compute a Symmetry Index quantifying facial asymmetry. Speech representations were obtained from a pre-trainedWav2Vec2 model, and audiovisual features were combined for supervised severity classification. The proposed Symmetry Index effectively distinguished the CLP from the control participants (Bhattacharyya distance = 2.45, p < 0.01). The multimodal classifier achieved 76.5% accuracy, outperforming single-modality baselines. The proposed framework, strengthened by class-balancing strategies, demonstrates the feasibility of automated severity assessment and shows potential as a future clinical decision-support tool.

title: A Planar Dual-Polarized Wide-Scanning Phased Array using Wide-Beam Elements for the Ku-band Satellite Terminal Applications
authors: Hao-Yu Yin, Zhao-Chen Li, Zi-Jun Guo, Sheng-Qi Gao, Zhang-Cheng Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This communication proposes a planar dual-polarized wideband and wide-beam element for Ku-band satellite terminal phased array applications, featuring the wide-angle scanning capability. By introducing a cylindrical-stepped ground (CSG) beneath the stacked patch antenna for exciting image magnetic currents and vertical radiation currents, an extremely wide beam over a wide operating bandwidth is obtained for the dual-polarized element, which has a 3-dB beamwidth of over 120 degrees for both E-plane and H-plane radiation patterns. Moreover, the CSG significantly confines the electromagnetic (EM) field beneath and around the radiation patch, which is helpful to suppress the surface wave among elements, and then is promising for achieving a wide scanning performance in array applications. To demonstrate the promising array application capability of the proposed element, a 250-element sparse phased array prototype is designed, fabricated, and measured with an operating frequency from 10.5 GHz to 12.8 GHz. Experimental results show that the prototype achieves ±75° beam scanning for dual polarizations over the 10.5–12.8 GHz frequency range in all cutting planes, with sidelobe levels below –10 dB and scan gain variation smaller than 6 dB. The proposed phased array antenna shows promising applications for the Ku-band satellite communication.

title: Distributed Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization: Deterministic and Stochastic Zeroth-Order Algorithms with Decaying Step Sizes
authors: Jie Hou, Xia Jiang, Xianlin Zeng, Lulu Zhao, Jian Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This paper addresses distributed nonsmooth nonconvex optimization over time-varying networks. Unlike prior works, we consider a more general formulation that does not require the nonsmooth nonconvex objective function to possess composite structures. While existing algorithms for such problems typically provide asymptotic convergence guarantees, we establish non-asymptotic rates and oracle complexities by introducing the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(\delta,\epsilon)$</tex-math></inline-formula>-Goldstein stationarity. For the deterministic setting, we propose a Distributed Zeroth-Order algorithm over Time-Varying networks (DZO-TV) with a decaying step size. Combining the averaged consensus protocol, randomized smoothing, and two-point function queries, the algorithm achieves a sublinear convergence rate of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(d^{3/8} \delta ^{-1/4}T^{-1/4})$</tex-math></inline-formula> to a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(\delta,\epsilon)$</tex-math></inline-formula>-Goldstein stationary point. For the stochastic setting, we develop a stochastic variant (DStoZO-TV) that employs either increasing-batch or single-batch data sampling, achieving an improved convergence rate of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(d^{1/3} \delta ^{-1/2}T^{-1/3})$</tex-math></inline-formula> and enhancing the function query complexity to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(d^{3/2} \delta ^{-4/3} \epsilon ^{-4})$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Finally, we demonstrate the efficacy of our algorithms through several numerical experiments.

title: Dynamic Memory Event-Triggered Optimal Secure Consensus Control of Multiple WMRs Under FDI Attacks
authors: Qian Qiu, Chenlong Li, Chunbin Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This paper proposes an intelligent safety learning control strategy based on an improved dynamic memory event-triggered mechanism (DMETM) to solve the optimal consensus control problem of multiple wheeled mobile robots (WMRs) under false data injection (FDI) attacks. First, we adopt the zero-sum graph game framework, model the controller and the attacker as competing agents, formulate their interaction as a minimax optimization problem, and improve an optimal security consensus control strategy based on the zero-sum graph game framework by analyzing the stable Nash equilibrium of the zero-sum graph game. This method effectively mitigates FDI attacks. Second, DMETM is improved by introducing a decreasing function of the consensus error, which significantly reduces the consumption of communication resources while mitigating its adverse impact on tracking performance. Moreover, this mechanism eliminates the occurrence of Zeno behavior. Furthermore, under the framework of adaptive dynamic programming (ADP), the coupled Hamilton-Jacobi-Isaacs (HJI) equations are efficiently solved to obtain the optimal security consensus control strategy. Finally, simulation outcomes indicate that every follower successfully follows the leader's path, even when FDI attacks occur.

title: Intelligent Serial Interactive Beam Search For Millimeter-Wave Beam Training
authors: Peiyuan Gao, Lin Yang, Haotian Xiu, Guangrong Yue
abstract: Beam prediction, which probes a subset of the entire beam search space to predict the optimal beam, has been employed to reduce the overhead of beam training. In this paper, we propose a novel serial interactive beam search (SIBS) algorithm. Rather than probing a fixed subset of candidate beams, SIBS flexibly selects probing beams based on real-time prediction feedback. Specifically, SIBS adopts a serial search strategy, performing beam prediction after searching each beam and utilizing the predicted beam as the target for the next search. To avoid the additional feedback latency overhead caused by serial search, an interactive search is conducted between the base station and the user equipment. We demonstrate the superiority of serial search by leveraging the beam-channel correlations. Furthermore, we design a novel neural network model based on the multi-head self-attention mechanism for beam prediction and propose a new model parameter training method tailored to the SIBS algorithm. To handle multi-user scenarios, we extend the SIBS algorithm by employing multi-peak beams capable of steering towards multiple directions. A neural network model for multi-peak beam decomposition is designed to facilitate processing by the beam prediction model. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed SIBS algorithm achieves more accurate results with fewer probing beams compared to the state-of-the-art methods.

title: Move-LLM: Multimodal LLM-assisted DRL Framework for On-Demand Service Deployment in 6G Vehicular Networks
authors: Gordon Owusu Boateng, Amine Kidane Ghebreziabiher, Rabeb Mizouni, Azzam Mourad, Hadi Otrok, Jamal Bentahar, Sami Muhaidat
abstract: The Sixth-Generation (6G) vehicular network paradigm demands intelligent solutions for dynamic on-demand service deployment. Yet, current approaches are limited by generalized services for specific traffic events, fixed Roadside Unit (RSU) locations, and insufficient context awareness in unimodal data inputs. This paper proposes Move-LLM, a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM)-assisted Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) framework for context-aware on-demand service deployment in 6G vehicular networks. The framework synergizes three core tasks: Perception (P), Recommendation (R), and Deployment (D). For the P task, raw multimodal (visual, textual) data is fed to a vehicular MLLM (Qwen2-VLM fine-tuned on vehicular events datasets) to enhance its inference via environment perception. For the R task, the inference result is utilized to recommend customized on-demand services capable of tackling the traffic event. Considering the fixed locations of RSUs, we introduce an Onboard Unit (OBU) cluster formation mechanism, where OBUs form clusters as alternative node options for hosting the recommended services. For the D task, we develop an improved Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG)-based algorithm with the service recommendations as its partial input state, allowing the agent to select the optimal node and resource allocation strategy to deploy on-demand services in a traffic event. Comprehensive simulation results and analysis reveal that our vehicular MLLM significantly improves P and R task accuracy by about 18.52% and 4.07%, respectively, in terms of Google BLEU (GLEU) score compared with GPT-4o-mini. Moreover, Move-LLM selects the optimal node and allocates optimal resources, achieving at least 11.76% expected utility under the hybrid (OBU clusters + RSUs) deployment mode compared to the RSU-only mode.

title: HENet: A Heterogeneous Encoding Network for General and Robust Adversarial Example Generation
authors: Jiawei Zhang, Hao Wang, Hao Wu, Bin Li, Xiangyang Luo, Bin Ma, Jinwei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Generator-based adversarial attack methods aim to fool deep neural networks (DNNs) by training a generator for crafting adversarial examples (AEs). However, as DNNs evolve from Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to Transformers, the existing generator-based methods can hardly achieve satisfactory attack performance against different target model architectures in semi-whitebox attack scenarios. In addition, the generated AEs are susceptible to various distortions (especially for JPEG compression with low quality factors), which deteriorate the attack ability and increase the unreliability. To address these issues, we propose a dual-branch guided generative model called Heterogeneous Encoding Network (HENet) to form a robust generator-based adversarial attack framework. Specifically, our HENet introduces an Adaptive Feature Fusion Module (AFFM) to solve the dimensions and representativeness contradictions between CNNs and Transformers, which steers the perturbation generation based on a richer latent space and achieves better general attack ability. To further improve the robustness against JPEG compression, we design and integrate a Dynamic Differentiable JPEG Simulator (DDJS), which introduces an adaptive quantization mask to determine the flow of the gradient backpropagation in each frequency position. Extensive experiments prove the proposed method achieves a better attack success rate, lower perturbation magnitude, and higher robustness for various target network architectures under compressed, distorted, and lossless scenarios. Our codes will be made publicly available.

title: DC-Biased Homogenized Harmonic Balance Finite Element Method
authors: Jan-Magnus Christmann, Laura A. M. D’Angelo, Herbert De Gersem, Sven Pfeiffer, Sajjad H. Mirza
abstract: The homogenized harmonic balance finite element (FE) method enables efficient nonlinear eddy-current simulations of 3-D devices with lamination stacks by combining the harmonic balance method with a frequency-domain-based homogenization technique. This approach avoids expensive time stepping of the eddy-current field problem and allows the use of a relatively coarse FE mesh that does not resolve the individual laminates. In this paper, we extend the method to handle excitation signals with a dc bias. To achieve this, we adapt the original homogenization technique to better account for ferromagnetic saturation. The resulting formula for the homogenized reluctivity is evaluated using a look-up table computed from a 1-D FE simulation of a lamination and containing the average magnetic flux density in the lamination and the corresponding skin depth. We compare the results of the proposed method to those from a fine-mesh transient reference simulation. The tests cover different levels of ferromagnetic saturation and frequencies between 50 Hz and 10 kHz. For moderate ferromagnetic saturation, the method gives a good approximation of the eddy-current losses and the magnetic energy, with relative errors below 10%, while reducing the required number of degrees of freedom at 10 kHz by 1.5 orders of magnitude. This results in a reduction in simulation time from 2 days on a contemporary server to 90 minutes on a standard workstation.

title: Rethinking Maturity in Next-Generation Maturity Models_supp1-3635020.docx
authors: Nuno Alexandre Seixas
abstract: This article introduces a maturity model architecture to address the limitations of traditional models, particularly in dynamic and multidomain environments. By redefining maturity as an adaptive and context-aware process, the model offers a flexible tool for enhancing performance across diverse organizational settings.

title: EgoPoseVR: Spatiotemporal Multi-Modal Reasoning for Egocentric Full-Body Pose in Virtual Reality_supp2-3680618.mp4
authors: Eng Tat Khoo
abstract: Immersive virtual reality (VR) applications demand accurate, temporally coherent full-body pose tracking. Recent head mounted camera-based approaches show promise in egocentric pose estimation, but encounter challenges when applied to VR head-mounted displays (HMDs), including temporal instability, inaccurate lower-body estimation, and the lack of real-time inference. To address these limitations, we present EgoPoseVR, an end-to-end framework for accurate egocentric full-body pose estimation in VR that integrates headset motion cues with egocentric RGB-D observations through a dual-modality fusion pipeline. A spatiotemporal encoder extracts frame- and joint-level representations, which are fused via cross-attention to fully exploit complementary motion cues across modalities. A kinematic optimization module then imposes constraints from HMD signals, enhancing the accuracy and stability of pose estimation. To facilitate training and evaluation, we introduce a large-scale synthetic dataset of over 1.8 million temporally aligned HMD and RGB-D frames across diverse VR scenarios. Experimental results show that EgoPoseVR outperforms state-of-the-art egocentric pose estimation models. A user study in real-world scenes further shows that EgoPoseVR achieved significantly higher subjective ratings in accuracy, stability, embodiment, and intention for future use compared to baseline methods. These results show that EgoPoseVR enables robust full-body pose tracking, offering a practical solution for accurate VR embodiment without requiring additional body-worn sensors or room-scale tracking systems.

title: AmI Still Me? Visual Congruence across Reality–Virtuality and Avatar Appearance in Shaping Self-Perception and Behavior_supp1-3679890.pdf

title: Fast Partial-Sum Update Method for Successive-Cancellation Decoding of Polar Codes and PAC Codes
authors: Xun Li, Yucheng Wu
abstract: The decoding complexity of polarization-adjusted convolutional (PAC) codes and Polar codes is significantly affected by the efficiency of partial-sum updates. In this letter, we propose a fast partial-sum update method for the successive-cancellation decoding (SCD) of PAC and Polar codes by exploiting the Pascal-triangle structure of the Polar generator matrix. Simulation results show that the proposed method reduces the number of partial-sum updates compared with conventional SCD, particularly for short code lengths and in the low-rate regime. Compared with representative fast decoding methods, the proposed method also shows a complexity advantage in the low-rate regime.

title: Dynamic Event-Triggered Critic Neural Network Optimal Control of Euler–Lagrange Systems With Full-State Constraints
authors: Jie Ruan, Songsong Cheng, Yuan Fan, Tianhong Pan, Jianbin Qiu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents an optimal control strategy for Euler–Lagrange systems subject to full-state constraints, based on a dynamic event-triggered critic neural network. To handle the constraints, an error transformation technique is introduced to convert the constrained error dynamics into an unconstrained form. Leveraging the transformed system, a critic neural network is utilized to approximate the performance index and derive the corresponding optimal control law. The specific update mechanism is formulated for the weight matrix of the neural network. To alleviate computational load, a dynamic event-triggered condition is designed to limit the frequency of weight updates, thereby mitigating unnecessary computations while effectively eliminating Zeno behavior. Comprehensive stability analysis proves the proposed approach is feasible and ensures that the approximation error of the critic neural network remains uniformly ultimately bounded. Numerical simulations on a robotic manipulator demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method and further verify its disturbance rejection capability under parametric perturbations and external disturbances.

title: CTMSA-ModernTCN Based Prediction Method for the Measurement Error of Current Transformer Automatic Verification Assembly Line
authors: Jing Fang, Yanlin Sun, Wenjun Luo, Xiong Gu, Huixuan Shi
publish: 2025 8th World Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Intelligent Manufacturing (WCMEIM)
abstract: The verification error of Current Transformer automatic verification assembly line plays a significant role in assessing the operational health of the line. Predicting measurement errors in advance enables timely maintenance and risk management of the line, thereby preventing large-scale product recalls. Consequently, this paper proposes a CTMSA-ModernTCN based predictiion model. This model adapts to complex influencing factors, multi-scale temporal coupling, and information leakage during measurement error prediction. Experimental validation demonstrates that the model achieves approximately 30% lower Mean Squared Error (MSE) and around 20% lower Mean Absolute Error (MAE) compared to commonly used Transformer. This model effectively assists the current transformer automatic verification assembly line in risk management and enhances its operational efficiency.

title: A UAV Autonomous Inspection Technology Driven by Electromagnetic Characteristics of Distribution Lines
authors: Zhenqi Ma, Haodong Ren, Jie Chen, Qingyun Chen, Bin Sun, Xuedong Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Against the issues that current UAV-based distribution line inspection relies heavily on Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) for navigation. In complex terrain areas, GNSS signals are susceptible to obstruction and attenuation, and the high-voltage electromagnetic field generated by distribution lines interferes with the UAV's sensitive sensors, resulting in positioning drift and abnormal attitude control. This paper proposes a UAV autonomous inspection technology driven by the electromagnetic characteristics of distribution lines. This technology equips the UAV with an electromagnetic sensor array to collect electromagnetic field signals from the distribution lines. By leveraging the spatial distribution law of the electromagnetic field, it estimates the relative positional relationship between the UAV and the distribution lines, thereby achieving autonomous navigation without relying on GNSS. A 10 kV three-phase distribution line experimental platform was constructed in the laboratory, and tests were conducted using a quadrotor UAV. The results demonstrate that when the UAV is far from the edge of the distribution line, the electric field strength exhibits an excellent inverse proportional relationship with the lateral distance (with a goodness of fit R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0. 9 9 4). The lateral deviation of the UAV during flight remains within a narrow range (with a standard deviation of 0. 0 2 2 m in the 4 5-6 0 m interval), which can meet the requirements of high-precision autonomous inspection.

title: Travel Buddy Using AI and Machine Learning
authors: Yashodeep Chavan, Mahesh Deshmukh, Gunjan Patil, Rushikesh Tat, Rushali A. Deshmukh, Shital Nalgirkar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: This review examines the recent advancements in AI-driven trip planning systems designed to enhance user experience through intelligent itinerary generation. Traditional trip planning is often limited due to the large number of fragmented tools and applications involved in the process. AIbased systems, particularly those utilizing machine learning techniques such as Convolutional Neural Networks, offer a more integrated and data-driven approach for identifying tourist attractions and incorporating real-time information, including weather conditions, traffic updates, and dining recommendations. This paper explores the existing methods used in AI travel assistants, comparing their performance, accuracy, and overall user experience across different models and approaches. By analyzing the strengths and limitations of various techniques, this review highlights key challenges in the development of intelligent trip planning systems and identifies emerging trends for future research in AI-driven travel optimization.

title: Autonomous UAV System for Human Detection and Tracking Using YOLOv8 and Real-Time Navigation
authors: Asim M. M. Ibrahim, Fauzan K. C. Harun, Muhammad A. N. Hamzah, Nurul A. Abdul-Kadir, Eileen S. L. Ming, Mohd A. Othman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: This paper presents a lightweight, fully autonomous Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) system designed to enhance Search and Rescue (SAR) missions through onboard human detection, tracking, and adaptive navigation. Traditional SAR methods are often slow, resource-intensive, and limited by terrain and environmental hazards. In contrast, UAVs offer rapid deployment and wide-area coverage but typically rely on ground control or cloud-based inference, introducing latency and operational constraints. To address these challenges, the proposed system runs a YOLOv8 object detection model and DeepSORT tracking algorithm entirely onboard a Raspberry Pi 5. An onboard camera captures aerial imagery for real-time inference without external computation, and the Raspberry Pi communicates with the UAV's flight controller via MAVLink to dynamically adjust waypoint missions. DroneKit manages autonomous navigation and behavior switching based on detection outcomes. The system was validated through QGroundControl and Software-In-TheLoop (SITL) simulations, as well as partial field tests to assess detection accuracy under real-world conditions. Results demonstrate effective onboard detection, responsive path adjustment, and reliable integration of tracking and control logic. This work highlights the potential of low-cost, AI-enabled UAV platforms to support time-critical SAR operations with increased autonomy and minimal reliance on external infrastructure.

title: Control Strategy for Direct-Drive Wave Energy Converter System Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ying Tang, Chuan Qin, Wei Li, Anni Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Wave energy capture control is a key focus in wave energy conversion systems. Due to the complex and variable nature of waves, accurate models often fall short of control needs, pushing the trend toward model-free control strategies. This study introduces a DQN-based control framework for wave energy systems, dynamically adjusting stiffness and damping coefficients to optimize energy capture. Experimental results show that the DQN approach achieves significantly higher energy capture compared to conventional resistive control and MPC methods.

title: Compact Microstrip Sensor Based on Hexagonal Matryoshka Structure
authors: Lucas M. C. Branco, Camila S. Cavalcanti, Marcos T. de Melo, Elias M. F. de Oliveira, Lauro R. G. S. L. Novo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: Compact microstrip sensors are essential for various applications in the X-band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(8-12 \text{GHz})$</tex>, such as radar and communications. This work presents the design, simulation, and experimental characterization of a new resonant sensor based on a concentric hexagonal ring architecture that follows the matryoshka nesting concept. This topology aims to optimize the compactness and quality factor within the operating band. The sensor was designed to operate around 10 GHz. After simulation, the structure was fabricated on a substrate and subjected to rigorous measurements. The experimental results of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> parameters show excellent agreement with the simulated data, validating the design methodology. Specifically, the measured resonant frequency presented a variation of only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0,1 { \%}$</tex> relative to the simulated frequency, confirming the effectiveness of the approach for the development of compact, high-performance, and low-cost microwave sensors.

title: A New Multi-Risk Assessment Model for Line Wave Positioning Accuracy of Distribution Network Based on FAHP and GRA
authors: Dajiang Ju, Pengchao Zhang, Shaoxian Zhang, Haoxuan Zhang, Gang Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: To reflect the influence of multiple factors on the accuracy of wave fault location in the new type of distribution network and to enhance the reliability of fault location in the power system, this paper proposes a risk assessment method that integrates the Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (FAHP) and the Grey Correlation Analysis (GRA). Firstly, this method uses GRA to determine the weights of each influencing factor, and then uses FAHP to classify the risk levels. This effectively solves the problems of fuzziness and uncertainty of the evaluation indicators. Based on the PSCAD, a simulation model of the new type of distribution network is built to simulate the influence of different factors on the wave waveform, thereby reflecting the effect of each factor on the accuracy of wave fault location, and collecting key wave features as evaluation data. After the importance ranking of factors is output by the random forest algorithm, the feature importance score is used as the correction parameter to optimize the initial weights obtained by GRA, and finally, the risk levels of each factor are determined by FAHP.

title: A Digital Speech Therapy Platform for Pronunciation Evaluation
authors: Nivedha D, K Amuthabala, Pranathi M, Praisy S, Roshan Reji
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: The healthcare sector is increasingly using digital tools such as applications, artificial intelligence, and remote platforms, leading to a growing demand for accessible and datadriven speech therapy solutions, particularly for children who require long-term pronunciation and fluency support. This paper presents THERA-SMART, a comprehensive web-based speech therapy platform designed to support patients, therapists, and guardians. Each user receives a role-specific dashboard that enables different features depending on who is using the system. The system enables browser-based speech acquisition using the Web Audio and MediaRecorder APIs, allowing speech to be captured directly in the browser. This design supports both in-clinic and remote therapy sessions. The recorded speech is processed using pre-trained Automatic Speech Recognition models. These models convert speech into textual phoneme-level representations, after which the application-level algorithms are employed for performance evaluation. A Levenshtein distance-based pronunciation evaluation algorithm is implemented to quantify pronunciation accuracy. This evaluation is done by comparing recognised speech with the predefined target sequence. In addition, rulebased phoneme scoring and timing-based fluency scoring are applied to provide transparent and interpretable performance metrics. The analysed results are visualized using graphs and charts that allow patients, guardians, and therapists to track performance improvements. By combining speech acquisition, evaluation algorithms, and role-specific visual feedback, THERA-SMART provides a practical and user-friendly digital platform for continuous speech therapy and progress monitoring.

title: Design and Implementation of Low-Cost Analog and Digital Li-Fi Systems for Short-Range Optical Communication
authors: Mayur Makwana, Hardik Modi, Dharmendra Chauhan, Sagar Patel, Heet Shah, Siddhrajsinh Rana
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Light Fidelity (Li-Fi) is an emerging optical wireless communication technology that utilizes visible light as a transmission medium, offering an alternative to conventional radio-frequency-based wireless systems. This paper presents the design, implementation, and experimental evaluation of both analog and digital Li-Fi communication systems developed using low-cost and readily available hardware components. The analog system demonstrates real-time audio transmission through an LED–photodiode link, while the digital system supports reliable file transfer using Arduino Uno and Raspberry Pi platforms. The proposed approach emphasizes secure, interference-free, and cost-effective communication, making Li-Fi suitable for environments where RF communication is limited or restricted. Experimental results validate successful short-range data transmission with clear audio reproduction and error-free digital file reception. Novelty: The key contribution of this work lies in the unified implementation of analog, microcontroller-based, and embedded Li-Fi systems within a single experimental framework, demonstrating the scalability of Li-Fi from basic circuit-level communication to IoT-oriented applications.

title: Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Mental Health Conditions From Online Posts
authors: Tanvir Kaur, Tejas Upadhyay, Partha Sarathi Chakraborty, Garima Bohra, Misbah Anjum
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Mental health disorders show a critical public health obstacle in the world, and their initial detection is a challenging problem to address because of the social stigma and restricted access to clinical services, as well as the use of subjective evaluations of mental conditions. As online platforms are growing at a quick pace, people can now reveal their mental health issues through text, providing new chances to analyse them with the help of AI. This paper proposes an ensemble deep learning model for classifying mental health conditions using online posts. The architecture combines CNN, BiLSTM, as well as transformer-based representations using the weighted voting and stacked ensemble approaches to ensure that the local patterns of the semantic features are captured, as well as the long-range contextual dependencies. A large-scale dataset of Reddit posts (more than 700,000 posts on six mental health categories, such as anxiety, depression, bipolar disorder, borderline personality disorder, schizophrenia, and many other mental illnesses) is being experimented with. The empirical shows that the proposed ensemble model has consistently better perform over the conventional machine learning models as well as individual deep learning models, and it provides better accuracy, F1-score, and class-specific robustness. Its results indicate that ensemble learning is successful in reducing the problem of class imbalance and the risk of overlapping symptom expression, which provides a real-world answer to the detection of automated mental health conditions when presented in textual data on the Internet.

title: Design and Development of Low Profile High Gain Wideband Wearable Antenna with Metasurface
authors: Pooja Sharma, Ajay Kumar Poddar, Arvind Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This research paper presents the design, simulation, fabrication, and characterization of a compact antenna operating in the Industrial, Scientific, and Medical (ISM) band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4.65 \text{GHz}-6.075 \text{GHz})$</tex> and provides a high gain wideband (1.4250 GHz) for wearable devices. This antenna is designed for wearable medical devices. The designed antenna has shown good performance including its return loss (>-35dB), Bandwidth <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1.425 \text{GHz})$</tex>, radiation pattern, gain 10 dB, and efficiency.

title: Machine Learning Classification for Contaminants in Urban Water Using a Planar Microwave Sensor
authors: Bruno Matias de Sousa, Valdemir Praxedes da Silva Neto
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This work presents a planar microwave sensor based on a SRR-IDC resonator for detecting nitrate and phosphate in water. The sensor operates at 2.0 GHz and measures changes in resonance frequency and phase. The Decision Tree and Random Forest models achieved accuracies above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 \%}$</tex>. The results demonstrate that combining microwave sensors with machine learning provides an effective and lowcost method for identifying contaminants in water.

title: Optical Fiber Link Monitoring in Passive Optical Networks: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Classifiers
authors: Shubham Singh, Lakshay Chawla
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Passive Optical Networks (PONs) are critical to nextgeneration telecommunications, offering cost-effective, highbandwidth connectivity. However, the point-to-multipoint architecture of PONs introduces significant challenges in physical layer fault monitoring. Traditional Optical Time Domain Reflectometry (OTDR) techniques often fail to distinguish faults in specific distribution branches due to the overlapping of backscattered signals from fibers of equidistant lengths. This paper proposes a non-intrusive, Machine Learning (ML) based fault identification system for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{4}$</tex> Gigabit PON. By leveraging downstream performance metrics-specifically Transmitted Power, Received Power, Q-Factor, and Bit Error Rate (BER)-collected at the Optical Network Units (ONUs), we eliminate the need for costly external monitoring modules at the Central Office. We designed and simulated the system using OptiSystem software to generate a dataset of 20,000 test instances across 32 distinct fault scenarios. Two supervised learning algorithms, Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Logistic Regression (LR), were implemented. Simulation results indicate that Logistic Regression outperforms SVM, achieving a classification accuracy of 99.93 % compared to SVM's 99.00 %. This study validates the feasibility of using existing transceiver data for highly accurate, automated fault diagnosis.

title: Simulation Study of a Half-Open Outdoor Hydrogen Comprehensive Utilization Platform
authors: Neng Tao, Honghao Wu, Qianyi Chen, Ru Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: With the continuous development and utilization of hydrogen energy, the number of hydrogen refueling stations within China's cities has been increasing. Currently, half-open outdoor hydrogen energy utilization stations remain in the demonstration and experimental phase. Their internal functions are complex, and relevant research is lacking. Hydrogen itself possesses properties such as low density and flammability/explosiveness. In the event of a leak, it can mix with air and, in the presence of an ignition source, potentially trigger an explosive reaction. This significantly increases the risk of irreversible damage to nearby personnel, equipment, facilities, and the operational environment. This study employs a near-scale replica model of an outdoor hydrogen utilization station and utilizes CFD numerical simulation to investigate the spatiotemporal diffusion of hydrogen clouds following leakage incidents under varying influencing factors.

title: Impact on Supply Chain Resilience: Analyzing Regional Trade Diversification and Supplier Optimization in Emerging Markets Under Tariff Volatility (2020–2025)
authors: Animesh Singh, Sakshi Sharma, Unnati Negi, B Aparna, Aman Kapoor, Madhav Sharma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Since 2020, global supply chains have changed significantly due to COVID-19 disruptions, rising geopolitical conflicts, and frequent changes in tariff policies. These challenges have exposed the risks of highly globalized supply chains and increased interest in nearshoring, where companies move production and sourcing closer to their main markets. This study examines how nearshoring helps improve supply chain resilience in emerging markets like Latin America, Eastern Europe, the Middle East–North Africa (MENA), and sub-Saharan Africa [1]. The research uses secondary trade data from the United Nations Comtrade database, World Bank-World Integrated Trade Solution (WITS), and the International Trade Centre Trade Map to analyze the relationship between tariff volatility during 2022–2025 and changes in regional import diversification [2]. The results show that emerging economies adopting nearshoring strategies achieved a 23% increase in supplier diversification and a 31% reduction in supplier concentration compared to countries that continued to rely on distant global suppliers. Sector-level analysis indicates that the automotive and electronics industries benefited the most, with lead times improving by approximately 45% through nearshoring. The findings provide empirical evidence that nearshoring, when supported by technology adoption and infrastructure development, can significantly strengthen supply chain resilience in emerging markets. From a policy perspective, the study highlights the importance of regional trade facilitation, investment in logistics and last-mile infrastructure, and the reduction of non-tariff barriers to successfully support nearshoring initiatives in developing economies.

title: Borage Leaf Disease Identification and Pesticide Recommendation Using Machine Vision
authors: Joel Sam, Pramod Mathew Jacob
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Cultivation of medicinal leaves like Borage (commonly called as Mexican mint) has been practiced widely over many regions for years. These leaves are widely used for medicinal uses, culinary uses as well as agricultural and industrial uses. Plectranthus amboinicus is the scientific name for Borage and is cultivated mainly in tropical and temperate zones like Europe, North America, Australia and India. While cultivating such herbs the main issue we face is to find the ones with disease and also to find if they are affected by aphids. This project aims in classifying Borage leaf as healthy, unhealthy or is infected by aphids. We have developed a machine vision based mobile application in which the user can upload or scan the borage leaf image. Once uploaded, the ML model classifies the borage leaf based on various selected feature set. The experimental results shows that our mobile application can be used for accurately classifying borage leaf into healthy and unhealthy categories.

title: Research on Flexibility Adjustable Load Resource Aggregation Method
authors: Liang Yue, Long Yu, Xiaoming Wang, Lishi Du, Xirun Zhang, Zhaoqing Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: In recent years, demand-side flexibility resources such as energy storage and electric vehicles have developed rapidly. These resources can regulate energy consumption and storage without compromising power quality or user experience, thereby maintaining grid stability. However, due to their small capacity, diverse characteristics, and dispersed nature, these resources must be integrated into an aggregated model during actual dispatch. This paper proposes an improved Zonotopebased approach for approximating the feasible domain of demand-side resources and an efficient aggregation method. By adopting a bottom-up approach to approximate the feasible domain of individual resources, the accuracy of the approximation is improved. The aggregation model is then derived using the Minkowski sum. Case studies validate the model's effectiveness in terms of computational accuracy and speed.

title: Short-Term PV Power Prediction Based on Similar Daily Clustering and CEEMDN-IGWO-SNN Model
authors: Gaoxiang Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: To enhance the accuracy of photovoltaic power forecasting, a spiking neural network (SNN) based on an improved grey wolf optimisation (IGWO) algorithm is proposed and applied to short-term PV output prediction. Firstly, following data preprocessing, the FCM algorithm is employed to select similar days, which are then reconstructed into a similar day set. Subsequently, the data within the similar-day set undergoes CEEMDN decomposition, with an IGWO-SNN prediction model constructed for each subcomponent. Finally, the predicted values are stacked and reconstructed to yield the final forecast result. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed CEEMDN-IGWO-SNN combined model outperforms the comparison model under various weather conditions, validating its robust accuracy and reliability across differing meteorological scenarios.

title: Huygens’ Metasurfaces Analysis Through Electric and Magnetic Surface Impedance Modeling
authors: Ghiles Medrar, Anne Claire Lepage, Xavier Begaud
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper summarizes the theory of Huygens’ metasurfaces and analyzes the effect of the electric and magnetic surface impedance on transmission, reflection, and absorption properties of these metasurfaces, providing a foundation for designing metasurface antennas for beamsteering, lensing and other electromagnetic wave manipulation applications.

title: Modelling Causal Bottlenecks to IoT-Enabled Circular Economy: A Multi-Stakeholder Perspective Using MCDM Framework
authors: Harshit Kohli, Sheshank Kodam, Aakash, Nithesh Gudipuri, Abhishek Bhushan Singhal
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The process of returning end-of-life electronics is among the most difficult steps of the circular economy (CE). IoT technologies offer power, scalability, and visibility; however, the real return rates do not allow leaving the problematic state. In the triangular barrier framework, the issue being discussed in this paper spearheads the consumer behaviour, infrastructure, and policy triangular and empowers them by means of a mixed-method design. We (i) compile cross-sector based evidence of IoTbased, logistics-based, and privacy/security studies recently released; (ii) conduct a 3-round Delphi meeting involving 20 professionals; and (iii) take out a multi-nation scale study <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(n=512)$</tex> which is evaluated via PLS-SEM and using the fsQCA. Low return convenience has been cited as the biggest barrier, especially in the collection networks, weak interoperability, and ambiguous take-back rules; privacy-security promotes such barriers. We offer a prioritized barrier portfolio (Fig. 1), measures, and playbooks of policies/managerial that align CE goals with realistic demands of the deployment of the Internet of Things. Results are consistent with recent findings on IoT-made returns of e-waste and intelligent logistics and the neglected policyinfrastructure interplay.

title: Recent Developments and Emerging Directions in Static Passive Skins for Smart EM Environments
authors: Aaron Angel Salas Sanchez, Giorgio Gottardi, Giacomo Oliveri
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: The Smart Electromagnetic Environment (SEME) paradigm is reshaping wireless systems by enabling engineered control of wave propagation. A key enabler of this vision is the use of static passive Electromagnetic Skins (EMSs)—planar, artificial structures designed to manipulate electromagnetic waves through fixed, application-specific surface responses. These EMSs provide advanced wave control functionalities, such as anomalous reflection and beam shaping, without requiring active tuning. Recent developments have focused on cost-effective, structurally integrated designs, including optically transparent implementations and nonconventional substrates, to facilitate seamless deployment in real-world environments. This work reviews the latest progress in static passive EMSs for SEME applications, highlighting their role in next-generation wireless networks.

title: Machine Learning for Early Prediction of Preeclampsia: A Meta-Analysis of Clinical and Digital Health Approaches
authors: Raissa Fatiny Maharani, I Dewa Gede Agung Eka Putra, Hafda Setyawan, Komang Rama Satria Mahotama, I Nyoman Adi Mahendra Putra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: Preeclampsia remains a major cause of maternal and perinatal morbidity worldwide, yet conventional screening methods demonstrate limited predictive accuracy. Advances in machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) have opened opportunities for early and noninvasive prediction of preeclampsia and hypertensive disorders of pregnancy (HDP). We conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of studies published between 2019 and 2025 that evaluated ML/DL-based prediction models for HDP. Seven eligible studies involving more than 25,000 pregnancies and over 180,000 antenatal records across Indonesia, the United States, China, Korea, Japan, and Tanzania were included. Extracted data covered study design, algorithm type, input features, and diagnostic performance, with reconstructed contingency tables where available. Pooled analysis using a random-effects model showed variable sensitivity across studies (0.42-0.92, median 0.77) but consistently high specificity (0.57-0.99, median 0.93). The pooled estimates yielded a sensitivity of 0.71 (95% CI: 0.58-0.82), specificity of 0.95 (95% CI: 0.89-0.98), and an AUROC of 0.89 (95% CI: 0.79-0.94). Substantial heterogeneity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{I}^{\mathbf{2}} \approx \mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>) was observed, largely influenced by one small study reporting low sensitivity. The summary ROC curve suggested a threshold effect, with most models tuned to minimize false positives. These findings indicate that ML/DL-based systems hold promise as confirmatory or risk-stratification tools for early detection of preeclampsia, with potential integration into digital health platforms, particularly in low-resource settings.

title: Design and Development of PaDyViR (Particle Dynamics Virtual Reality): From Storyboard to Prototype
authors: Supurwoko Supurwoko, Kadek Dwi Hendratma Gunawan, Muhammad Nur Hudha
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Physics education often struggles to convey abstract concepts such as force, mass, acceleration, friction, and collisions, which students frequently find difficult to visualize. Virtual reality (VR) offers promising opportunities to address this challenge by providing embodied, interactive representations of otherwise invisible physical phenomena. This study aims to describe the design and development of PaDyViR (Particle Dynamics Virtual Reality), a VR module created to support the teaching of particle dynamics through a storyboard-driven instructional design process. Employing a Research and Development (R&D) approach based on the 4D model (Define, Design, Develop, and Disseminate), the study focused on the first three stages. Activities included needs analysis, storyboard creation and validation, and prototype development using Blender for 3D modeling and Unity’s physics engine for realistic simulations. The results yielded a functional VR prototype aligned with Newtonian mechanics, consisting of sequential learning scenarios on force and acceleration, static and kinetic friction, and elastic collisions. Screenshots documented the transition from storyboard to immersive interface, demonstrating fidelity to curricular standards and expert validation. The findings indicate that PaDyViR is pedagogically coherent, technically stable, and ready for future empirical validation. Subsequent research should evaluate its usability, effectiveness, and impact on learning outcomes.

title: An Enhanced Electromagnetic-Thermal Multiphysics Coupling Scheme with a Subgridding Algorithm for Chiplet Architecture Simulation
authors: Xiangli Huo, Jing Shi, Guanghuan Zhang, Xingang Ren, Zhixiang Huang, Xianliang Wu, Kaikun Niu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper introduces the electromagnetic subgridding algorithm into thermal calculations, deriving the subgridding algorithm equations for both the electromagnetic field and thermal field, as well as the electromagnetic-thermal bidirectional coupling mechanism. The proposed approach enables accurate and efficient computation of the electromagnetic-thermal coupling problem in multiscale chiplet, significantly saving memory and computation time. The accuracy of the electromagnetic-thermal subgridding method is validated through simulations of a sphere in air and a Through Silicon Via (TSV) pillar model. Additionally, the efficiency and low memory usage of the algorithm, while ensuring accuracy, are demonstrated through simulations of a ball grid array (BGA) packaging structure and TSV packaging structure. This computational method has significant application value for improving microsystem structures and maintaining long-term stable operation under high-frequency conditions.

title: Compression-Oriented Video Super-Resolution
authors: Shuyun Wang, Yanbin Liu, Ming Lu, Zhuojie Wu, Senmao Tian, Yandong Guo, Xin Yu
abstract: Current compressed video super-resolution methods have achieved promising performance, but they often assume that an input video is compressed under low-delay configurations. However, under random access configurations, those methods might struggle to leverage the metadata effectively due to the large variations of metadata in different compression configurations. In this work, we propose a Compression-Oriented Video Super-Resolution (COVSR) method that can address video superresolution for both low-delay and random-access configurations. Specifically, we first introduce an efficient compression-aware propagation (ECAP) module that dynamically adjusts propagation routes in accordance with the compression configurations. Since existing methods require reconstructing frames in a frameby- frame manner, it is difficult to achieve efficient parallelization. However, we find that by slightly relaxing sequential dependencies, our ECAP can significantly improve inference speed. Furthermore, existing methods typically perform alignment between adjacent frames or adjacent features. However, since ECAP may propagate features along non-adjacent reference routes, it introduces new challenges for accurate cross-frame feature alignment. In response, we propose a metadata-driven alignment (MDA) module that refines cross-frame motion vectors into dense, feature-level flow offsets, enabling precise alignment across temporally distant features. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our COVSR not only achieves efficient and superior super-resolution performance but also is generalizable to various compression configurations. Our code will be available and the project page is at https://covsr.github.io.

title: Relative hulls and their variations of narrow-sense primitive BCH codes
authors: Chunyu Gan, Chengju Li, Sihem Mesnager
abstract: The relative hull of a linear code C1 with respect to another linear code C2 is defined as the intersection of C1 and the dual of C2, i.e., C1 ∩ C⊥2. Anderson et al. [2] demonstrated that the dimension of the relative hull can be adjusted, either repeatedly increased or decreased by one, until a certain bound is reached by substituting either C1 or C2 with its equivalent code. As a special class of linear codes, BCH codes are both theoretically significant and practically valuable for communication and storage systems due to their good algebraic structures and flexible error-correcting capabilities. This raises an important question: how does the dimension of the relative hull change when C1 and C2 are BCH codes or their equivalent codes? This paper focuses on the relative hull dimensions of narrow-sense primitive BCH codes, building on the insights from [2]. We present several sufficient and necessary conditions based on the designed distances of BCH codes, which ensure that the dimensions of the relative hulls reach the lower or upper bounds for linear codes. Additionally, we study the parameters of the relative hulls of various classes of BCH codes, providing details on their dimensions and developing lower bounds for their minimum distances. Furthermore, we investigate how the dimensions of the relative hulls are affected when the BCH codes C2 are replaced by their equivalent codes C′2.

title: Mode Interference Effect of a High-Frequency Linear Antenna in an Ionospheric Plasma
authors: Xue Wei Zhang, Nan Nan Sun, Li Na He, Tong He, Kai Li
abstract: With the development of satellite launching technology, spaceborne high-frequency (HF) linear antennas are being widely used in fields such as Earth observation and ionospheric sounding. In this article, we carry out an in-depth study on the effects of mode interference for an HF linear antenna immersed in a homogeneous anisotropic ionospheric plasma. Closed-form expressions for the radiated field by a finite-length linear antenna with nonuniform current distribution are derived analytically. The results show that HF linear antennas operating in the ionospheric plasma would exhibit disparate radiation characteristics from those in free space, where the ordinary wave and extraordinary wave contribute to the antenna’s current distribution and radiation pattern simultaneously and induce interferences. Pronounced minima at certain intervals of propagation distances in the total field are observed under substantial interference between the two waves. Additionally, the field pattern of each eigenmode varies slowly with the radiation direction, while the synthetic field is greatly affected by mode interference effects in both the near-zone and far-zone. This work may provide a theoretical foundation and guidance to the design and optimization of practical spaceborne HF antenna applications.

title: Large-Scale Empirical Analysis of Continuous Fuzzing: Insights from 1 Million Fuzzing Sessions
authors: Tatsuya Shirai, Olivier Nourry, Yutaro Kashiwa, Kenji Fujiwara, Yasutaka Kamei, Hajimu Iida
abstract:
Software vulnerabilities are constantly being reported and exploited in software products, causing significant impacts on society. In recent years, the main approach to vulnerability detection, fuzzing, has been integrated into the continuous integration process to run in short and frequent cycles. This continuous fuzzing allows for fast identification and remediation of vulnerabilities during the development process. Despite adoption by thousands of projects, however, it is unclear how continuous fuzzing contributes to vulnerability detection.

This study aims to elucidate the role of continuous fuzzing in vulnerability detection. Specifically, we investigate the coverage and the total number of fuzzing sessions when fuzzing bugs are discovered. We collect issue reports, coverage reports, and fuzzing logs from OSS-Fuzz, an online service provided by Google that performs fuzzing during continuous integration. Through an empirical study of a total of approximately 1.12 million fuzzing sessions from 878 projects participating in OSS-Fuzz, we reveal that (i) a substantial number of fuzzing bugs exist prior to the integration of continuous fuzzing, leading to a high detection rate in the early stages; (ii) code coverage continues to increase as continuous fuzzing progresses; (iii) changes in coverage contribute to the detection of fuzzing bugs; and (iv) developer-provided seed corpus exhibit long-term effectiveness. This study provides empirical insights into how continuous fuzzing contributes to fuzzing bug detection, offering practical implications for future strategies and tool development in continuous fuzzing.

title: Forecasting of Charging Demand for New Energy Vehicles in Industrial Parks Based on LF-PSO Assisted Optimized LSTM
authors: Mingtai Shi, Kai Liu, Koyu Wu, Jianyun Li, Fangyan Pen, Junwei Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Against the backdrop of escalating global climate change and the “dual-carbon” goals, zero-carbon parks, as critical carriers of new energy systems, have emerged as a pivotal direction for energy transition practices. To address the accuracy challenges posed by the deep interactions of source-grid-loadstorage within zero-carbon parks and the resulting uncertainties in traditional load forecasting, this paper proposes a load forecasting model optimized using a Levy Flight-improved Particle Swarm Optimization (LF-PSO) algorithm. By employing the LF-PSO algorithm to optimize the hyperparameters of LSTM and integrating time series decomposition techniques (STL) for feature engineering, an efficient load forecasting model is constructed. The results demonstrate that this method significantly improves forecasting accuracy compared to traditional models, providing reliable technical support for smart energy management in parks.

title: Visual-Affective Transformers for Emotion-Rich Captioning
authors: Kukutla Jithandra, Macherla Dhana Lakshmi, Mandla Ramachandrudu, Inagandla Raja Narasimha Rao, Lalam Keshava, Kattubadi Sameer
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Modern image captioning models can describe scenes and objects accurately. However, they often fail to capture the emotional context. This makes the descriptions lack human-like empathy and nuance. As a result, their effectiveness is limited in applications that need deeper understanding. Examples include assistive technologies and affective computing. To solve this problem, we propose a new hybrid dual-stream architecture. It generates captions with emotional content by analyzing both visual features and affective cues at the same time. The visual stream uses a Vision Transformer to encode scene-level information. A parallel affective stream employs a CNN-based classifier to detect human emotions from faces. These streams are combined using a transformer-based fusion module. This creates a unified representation that understands both context and emotion. We tested the system on a custom multimodal dataset of 10,000 images. These images come from MS-COCO and are annotated with emotion labels from a classifier trained on AffectNet. The system shows strong performance. It achieves 96.2% accuracy for matching emotions with captions. It also scores well on standard captioning metrics such as CIDEr and BLEU. This study shows that combining visual data with affective analysis improves caption quality and relevance. It offers a strong and practical method for building AI systems that are more perceptive and empathetic.

title: From Motion to Meaning

title: Longitudinal Laboratory Drift Modeling for Multi-Class Chemotherapy Toxicity Prediction Using Tree-Based Ensemble Learning
authors: Md. Arifur Rahaman, Md Jabed Hasan Jabir, Md Amzad Sadik Abid, Md Sabbir Hossain Polak, Md Raj Kabir, Sudoy Kumer Ghosh
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Toxicity due to chemotherapy has been a major issue in cancer treatment and the existing risk measurements are mostly based on immune homeostasis parameters at baseline that are not capable of assessing changing physiological dynamics throughout the treatment. This paper presents a new machine learning model to predict the severity of toxicity by using longitudinal laboratory drift modeling on early chemotherapy cycles. The classification of multi-class toxicity using hematological, hepatic, and renal markers is obtained by identifying the similarities in the time-dependent dynamics of the markers. Preprocessing includes unit harmonization, effective outlier detection, and interval interpolation to standardized intervals. Rate of change measures, cumulative baseline deviations, and inter-laboratory coupling measures are focused on in feature engineering to encode dynamic organ system interactions. The XGBoost and Gradient Boosting models are determined by the stratified patient-level crossvalidation, which gives a macro-F1 score of 0.9039 and 0.8945, respectively, and a balanced balanced-class-wise-recall among the toxicity grades. Analysis of decision-curve shows that there is a positive clinical net benefit in proactive intervention strategies. This model allows the early stratification of risks and treatment-individual adjustment, and longitudinal drift analysis is quantified to form the basis of adaptive chemotherapy management and deploying targeted supportive care.

title: Longitudinal Analysis of Daily Heart Rate Signals and Stress Self-Report Using Wearable Technology: A One-Month Single-Subject Case Study
authors: Alvin Sahroni, Pramudya Rakhmadyansyah Sofyan, Dimas Maulana Arbi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: Stress and chronic stress are associated with the development of various emotions and manifestations. Previously, objective stress measurements were already conducted and provided higher accuracy; however, most were established in a laboratory environment with stress stimulation. This study proposed a pre-study to compare smartwatch stress assessment as a wearable technology and subjective evaluation (self-report) during a month of daily activities from a single subject's perspective, based on 24 days of observations. We extracted the time domain heart rate variability properties as major physiological signals from the smartwatch. We assessed the self-reports during morning (8:00 a.m. - 12:00 p.m.), afternoon (12:00-5:00 p.m.), and evening (5:00 - 9:00 p.m.), respectively. The study showed that the average time between heartbeats was associated with increased stress levels <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(r=-0.81)$</tex>; surprisingly, the objective stress measurement did not show a high correlation with the smartwatch stress assessment score during 24 days of daily activities. However, we found that the combination of afternoon and evening can provide a better correlation. We concluded that evaluating daily stress requires analysis and detection across time, rather than focusing on specific time events.

title: Brushids detection with Terahertz time domain imaging
authors: Jean-Paul Guillet, Jean-François Berthoumieu, François Demontoux
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: We report a terahertz time-domain imaging method for non-destructive detection of bruchid beetle infestation inside lentil seeds. Bruchid beetles create internal galleries in lentil seeds that depress market value and complicate quality control because routine inspection remains destructive, slow, and sample limited. This feasibility study evaluates whether terahertz time domain imaging in transmission can provide a non-destructive contrast for bruchid damage using a simple time of flight metric. Raster scans of single layer lentil arrangements were acquired with a femtosecond laser-based terahertz system. At each pixel the temporal waveform was recorded and the delay of the first transmitted pulse relative to air was computed. Two-dimensional maps of the peak delay were then rendered and a single threshold was applied to segment seeds. Across two independent acquisitions, the time-of-flight maps consistently delineated individual lentils and separated intact from damaged seeds: intact seeds exhibited larger delays than infested ones, yielding high visual contrast without complex modeling. Post hoc destructive inspection confirmed that seeds classified with larger delays were healthy in the present dataset. The physical mechanism is consistent with an effective index reduction and shortened optical path in damaged seeds due to larval galleries and local porosity, while intact seeds maintain higher bulk density and longer propagation time. The approach requires no spectroscopic features or multilayer fitting and uses only the position of the main transmitted pulse, which favors speed and robustness for prospective automation. These preliminary results establish physical feasibility and motivate development of an industrial non-destructive screening tool for lentil quality control.

title: Institute of Hyper-Consumption: Traces of Excess
authors: Fernando Carvalho, Ana Mariz, Kim da Motta, Diogo Aguiar, Patrícia Gouveia

title: AI that Understands Us: LLM-Based Emotion and Stress Insights from Online Communication
authors: Akash Rajak, Siddheshwari Dutt Mishra, Vidushi, Shashank Bhardwaj, Amit Kumar, Sunil Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The large language models (LLMs) show more accurate interpretation of human emotion and psychology from text which is available on the social media chats. This study analyses emotion recognition and stress detection capabilities of fine-tuned LLMs (GPT-2, FLAN-T5 and LLaMA-7B) using text data from social media and conversation logs. The models were tested for performance using the publicly available emotion language datasets, DailyDialog, EmotionX and GoEmotions. The models were evaluated for performance and efficiency using classification accuracy, macro-F1 score, and inference time. The results identify the performance spectrum of the models and the large models' enhanced ability to recognize detailed emotional states. These results offer real world applicability of LLM methodologies to stress detecting and decision-support automated systems in mental healthcare.

title: A System for Blockchain Auditability in Clinical Federated Learning to Ensure Trust and Transparency in Multi-Institutional Healthcare
authors: Kingsley C. Nwosu, Musbah Abdulgader, Yen-Hung Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables multiinstitutional model training without sharing raw patient data, but it leaves gaps in provenance, accountability, and regulatory auditability. We present a blockchain-based auditing layer for FL that uses Proof-of-Authority (PoA) consensus and smart contracts to validate, record, and immutably trace every client update. Contracts enforce format/size constraints and anomaly gates, yielding transparent and tamper-resistant training logs. We evaluate on expanded settings, 20-50 rounds, 5-8 simulated hospitals, fractional participation, and explicit non-IID label skew via Dirichlet partitions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha \in\{1.0,0.5,0.1\}$</tex>). Across these regimes, the auditable FL system maintains competitive Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUROC) while adding minimal latency and storage overhead, and it improves traceability by design. These results demonstrate that regulatorfriendly provenance can be integrated into FL without materially degrading predictive performance, providing a practical path toward trustworthy, compliant, multiinstitution healthcare ML.
