title: Design of a Fractal-Based Multi-Resonant Metamaterial Absorber with Gielis-Generated Resonators
authors: Samuel Rivero-Ballesta, Esteban Ortega-Bracamonte, Natalia Lara-Davila, Sebastian Montoya-Villada, Erick Reyes-Vera
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: A novel perfect metamaterial absorber, including double Gielis-generated resonators into an E-shaped fractal structure, has been numerically investigated. The absorber performance was evaluated over a frequency range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-7 \text{GHz}$</tex> and compared to a similar structure without the fractal structure. The optimization approach produced a compact resonator with dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \times 30 ~\text{mm}$</tex>. The results reveal that this structure has absorption efficiencies of 84.6, 78.97, and 96.4 % in the first, second, and third frequency bands, respectively. Also, other characteristic parameters were evaluated. This design is very successful at absorbing electromagnetic energy, making it a viable choice for energy harvesting.

title: Machine Learning–Driven CRM System for SMEs in the Digital Commerce Domain
authors: Laxmi Rai, Deepali Singh, Sonali Sharma, Santosh Bali, Vandana Madaan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The growth of 'online' players has meant that small and medium companies (SMEs) now face greater competition. To continue to grow, businesses need to bring in and retain customers. Cliche application of CRM. SMBs often use traditional CRM systems, which are human-driven and require rules to make decisions. For this reason, their principal task, which is to collect meaningful information from large and ever-changing customer databases, is challenging. The 4.0 CRM system is the app to address the problems SMEs face in digital commerce domains via machine learning methods. AI analyses customer interactions, behaviour patterns, and transaction histories. This provides demand predictions, customer churn estimation, personalised marketing communications, and effective consumer segmentation. The marketing initiative, consumer engagement, and platform retention are powered by data-driven decision-making with an automated system. Empirical experiments with real-world e-commerce data show that machine learning based CRM models outperform the traditional models on both predictive accuracy and sensitivity. The results show that SMEs can benefit from scalable, inexpensive machine learning solutions to improve customer relations, operational efficiency, and competitiveness in fast-changing digital markets.

title: Transient Stability Assessment Method Using Feature Injection Mechanism for Renewable Energy Power Systems
authors: Xiaoyu Han, Zihe Liu, Yan Li, Jinfu Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The increasing use of renewable energy poses challenges to traditional transient stability assessment (TSA) methods, which are based on complex modelling and have limited real-time capability. This paper proposes a method based on feature injection for renewable energy power systems. Firstly, a probabilistic model analyzed the output fluctuations of wind and solar power generation in order to construct disturbance scenarios via electrical and topological modifications. Secondly, the feature set encompassed the output characteristics of the new energy units and the electrical quantity of the grid-connected points, while the acquisition process of the complex state parameters inside the units was omitted. Finally, a global feature injection mechanism was introduced into the new energy system, and a convolutional neural network (CNN)-TSA model was constructed from both global and local feature dimensions. Validation on the modified IEEE 39-bus system confirms the effectiveness of the method.

title: Evaluation of Machine Learning Models' Performance in Plant Disease Diagnostics and Crop Prediction
authors: Tanishk Tyagi, Tanmoy Das, Ravi Verma, Shreyansh Omrey
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Agriculture in India is not merely an occupation but serves as the backbone of the nation's economy and livelihood. With nearly 60% of the population dependent on farming for sustenance and employment, the sector plays a crucial role in contributing to the Gross Domestic Product (GDP). yet, Indian farmers often encounter persistent challenges in deciding what crops to cultivate, when to sow, and how to optimize yield under changing climatic and economic conditions. Traditionally, these decisions have relied heavily on intuition, ancestral knowledge, and regional practices. While valuable, such approaches often fail to adapt to modern agricultural challenges, including unpredictable weather patterns, fluctuating market demands, soil degradation, and the pressing impacts of climate change. To address these issues, the present work proposes AgriNurture an intelligent, data-driven ML–based crop recommendation system designed to guide farmers in making scientifically informed cultivation decisions. this suggested model utilizes a rich combination of agro-climatic and environmental parameters, such as rainfall, temperature, soil nutrients, fertilizer usage, and historical yield records, to recommend the most suitable crops for a specific location and season. datasets were obtained from authentic government agricultural databases and open-access platforms, ensuring data credibility and real-world applicability.

title: Evaluation of Differential Breast Microwave Imaging on MRI-Derived Breast Phantom Pairs
authors: Tyler Weselowski, Loïc Lambert, Stephen Pistorius
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: Breast Microwave Imaging (BMI) has the potential to be used as an early breast cancer diagnostic tool, but its diagnostic performance is hindered by the strong reflections from the skin and anatomical differences between breasts. Differential imaging techniques can suppress common features such as the skin response and underlying breast tissue, displaying only differences between the left/right breasts such as a tumour. In this study, MRI-derived left and right breast phantoms were fabricated to examine the potential of differential imaging techniques on realistic breast exams. The delay-and-sum (DAS) and optimisation-based radar reconstruction (ORR) algorithms were used. Tumour responses were not identifiable after applying differential techniques to the realistic breast phantom pair. The influence of both breast translational misalignment and bilateral asymmetry was examined for differential BMI. When the contralateral breast was translationally shifted, tumour responses disappeared once the shift exceeded approximately 2 mm, yielding an AUC of 34.4%. Applying a simple temporal skin-alignment procedure restored strong tumour visibility and increased the AUC to 81.9%. For bilateral asymmetry, even slight differences in breast geometry—within the population’s normal volume asymmetry—produced an immediate reduction in the ability to detect tumour responses.

title: Ensemble Vision Transformers for Robust Diabetic Retinopathy Detection and Classification
authors: Manav Mittal, Meenakshi, Amardeep Gupta, Pradeep Kumar Kushwaha, Bhanu Prakash Lohani, Deepshikha Bhargava
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is the major cause of visual impairment in the world and millions of diabetic patients become victims each year. The timely diagnosis and the right categorization of the level of the severity of DR are important factors that prevent the permanent loss of vision. The paper introduces a new ensemble-based method with Vision Transformers (ViTs) which make use of automated DR and multi-class classification. Our ensemble design comprises three versions of ViTs, which include DeiT-III, Swin Transformer V2, and BEiT-3, that are united together by a weighted voting system with uncertainty measurement. Our algorithm also has state-of-the-art preprocessing steps such as adaptive histogram equalization, extraction of green channel and data augmentation schemes that are specific to fundus imaging. The experiments on the APTOS 2019, Messidor-2 and EyePACS datasets reveal that our ensemble model attains higher results based on the quadratic weighted kappa score of 0.923, accuracy of 91.7, sensitivity of 93.2 and specificity of 94.8 on the test set. The ensemble method is better in terms of kappa score by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 2 - 4. 7 \%}$</tex> than individual models and demonstrates a high degree of generalization in various datasets. This research ensemble Vision Transformers will be a promising solution to a high-quality and automated screening of the DRs in clinical practices.

title: Analyzing Interdependencies Between IoT Data Integration Capabilities and Real-Time Operational Decision-Making: A DEMATEL Approach
authors: Kuldeep Sharma, Abhishek Bhushan Singhal, Venkata Ramachandra karthik Chundi, Saraschandra Arveti, Aakash
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The growing use of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies has helped organizations to gather huge amounts of real-time information that has disrupted the operational decision-making systems. Yet, the success of the latter decisions largely depends on the integration capabilities on data level that is interdependent per se. The current literature adopts either approach of linear or hierarchical modelling solutions, which do not reflect the complex causal associations between these capabilities and decision results. To overcome this shortcoming, the research paper utilizes Decision-Making Trial and Evaluation Laboratory (DEMATEL) method to test the causal framework that relates the capability of the IoT data integration with the real-time operational decision-making aspect. Based on the professional assessments, ten factors were revealed and examined. The findings demonstrate that interoperability, real-time data processing, and system scalability are all dominant causal and decision accuracy, responsiveness, and operational flexibility are all effect-oriented outcomes. This study adds to a systematic causal paradigm of learning about the IoT-enabled decision ecosystem and provides practical suggestions to managers who would be willing to focus on strategic digital investments.

title: Intelligent Substation Main Wiring Automatic Generation Based on Deep Learning IED Attributes
authors: Yinni Zeng, Renyuan Cai, Guoju Wang, Shihao Wang, Chongpei Wu, Jiajia Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: In engineering applications, SCD files often lack direct descriptions of primary equipment and their topological relationships, making it difficult to generate the main single-line diagram of a substation. This paper presents an intelligent approach based on deep learning for automatically generating the main single-line diagram of a substation. By utilizing the descriptive features of IEDs such as descriptions, names, models, types, and virtual connections, a knowledge graph database is constructed. A deep learning model is trained to learn the attributes of IEDs, incorporating recurrent neural network techniques to analyze and infer properties such as voltage levels, connection methods, interval types, insulation levels, and associated busbars. The FR algorithm is employed to calculate reasonable distances for interval allocation at different voltage levels, enabling the generation of busbars for each voltage level. The intervals are sequentially determined, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm is applied to compute the optimal path for connecting high, medium, or low-side intervals of the main transformer, adjusting the interval positions accordingly. This method efficiently visualizes the implicit topological relationships of primary equipment and the associations between primary and secondary equipment. Instance verification results show that this method can accurately and automatically draw the primary main connection diagram, helping substation on-site engineering personnel intuitively and quickly understand the overall situation of the SCD, and significantly improving the work efficiency of on-site engineering configuration and verification.

title: Hyperlocal E-Commerce Platform for Seller-Customer Integration and Order Fulfillment
authors: Aditya Narayan Singh, Kanta Prasad Sharma, Amar Deep Gupta
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The hyperlocal e-commerce has become a disruptive paradigm that will decrease reliance on centralized warehouses by dispatching customer demand directly to the local stores. The presented paper is a proximity-based hyperlocalized marketplace that is designed to facilitate grocery and daily-need purchasing with a radius of 10 km. The platform allows the local shop owners to post the actual product availability in real-time which could be browsed by the customers remotely and could be ordered to be delivered to the home address or to be picked by the store. Scheduling of the backend is supported by Strapi CMS with REST APIs and organized data storage is secured with the help of PostgreSQL with indexed location fields allowing to assign sellers to the low-latency group. Customer interface and administration dashboard built on ReactJS offers operational management, inventory and transparency of order lifecycle. An evaluation of 25 local sellers performed through simulation demonstrates that the system latency can be predicted (API 320 ms; DB 140 ms; allocation 1.4 sec) and the rate of order fulfillment is high (96.2%). Findings confirm that geometrical-based matching of sellers enhances the involvement of retailers and facilitates quicker procurement and also scalable hyperlocal order pipelines that meets the requirements of academic full-stack engineering. The publication underlines the idea that decentralized marketplaces that can be facilitated by CMS may be capable of fulfilling last-mile grocery procurement in addition to enabling small retailers with organized administrator control and indexed relational warehousing.

title: TR-Gaussians: High-fidelity Real-time Rendering of Planar Transmission and Reflection with 3D Gaussian Splatting_supp2-3675416.mp4
authors: Tianjia Shao
abstract: We propose Transmission-Reflection Gaussians (TRGaussians), a novel 3D-Gaussian-based representation for highfidelity rendering of planar transmission and reflection, which are ubiquitous in indoor scenes. Our method combines 3D Gaussians with learnable reflection planes that explicitly model the glass planes with view-dependent reflectance strengths. Real scenes and transmission components are modeled by 3D Gaussians and the reflection components are modeled by the mirrored Gaussians with respect to the reflection plane. The transmission and reflection components are blended according to a Fresnelbased, view-dependent weighting scheme, allowing for faithful synthesis of complex appearance effects under varying viewpoints. To effectively optimize TR-Gaussians, we develop a multistage optimization framework incorporating color and geometry constraints and an opacity perturbation mechanism. Experiments on different datasets demonstrate that TR-Gaussians achieve real-time, high-fidelity novel view synthesis in scenes with planar transmission and reflection, and outperform state-of-the-art approaches both quantitatively and qualitatively.

title: StyleDe-CD: Dual-Level Style Disentanglement for Semi-Supervised Change Detection
authors: Ze Zhang, Yue Zhou, Linqing Huang, Zhentao Zou, Xue Jiang, Jianxin Xing, Xingzhao Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Semi-supervised change detection (SSCD) has emerged as a promising approach to reduce the burden of pixel-level annotation in bi-temporal remote sensing imagery (RSI). However, learning robust and reliable representations under style variations remains a key challenge, as appearance discrepancies across temporal acquisitions often account for a large portion of the visual differences between bi-temporal images. These challenges can be attributed to two levels of style–semantic entanglement: (1) cross-temporal style discrepancies caused by acquisition variations (e.g., illumination shifts and seasonal changes), which may confuse the model and increase sensitivity to superficial appearance differences; and (2) intra-temporal style heterogeneity introduced by diverse background characteristics (e.g., land-cover diversity and vegetation density differences), which can bias feature learning and obscure meaningful semantic changes. To address these challenges, we propose StyleDe-CD, a dual-level style disentanglement framework for SSCD. At the input level, we introduce stochastic spectral–photometric style augmentation (SSPSA), which integrates spectral style alignment (SSA) and photometric transformations (PT) through a stochastic switching mechanism. SSA alleviates cross-temporal style discrepancies by smoothly blending the low-frequency amplitude spectra of bi-temporal images using Gaussian-weighted soft masks, while PT introduces diverse appearance perturbations to improve robustness to style variations. At the feature level, we develop intra-temporal style mixing (ISM), which stochastically mixes channel-wise feature statistics across corresponding temporal components of shuffled image pairs within the same minibatch, encouraging the model to reduce sensitivity to instance-specific background styles and to emphasize semantic change cues. Extensive experiments on the LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and CDD benchmarks demonstrate that StyleDe-CD consistently achieves strong and competitive performance compared to existing semi-supervised baselines, particularly under low-label regimes, indicating its effectiveness in SSCD.

title: Modified Fixed-Time Extended State Observer Based Fixed-Time Sliding Mode Control for PMSM Position Servo Systems
authors: Wenhui Dou, Yi Mao, Shihong Ding, Qiankang Hou
abstract: Aiming to optimize the tracking and anti-disturbance capacities of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) position control systems, a modified fixed-time extended state observer (MFESO) based fixed-time sliding mode control (FSMC) algorithm is proposed in this paper. Firstly, the FSMC controller is constructed to enable the motor to accurately track the time-varying reference position signal. Secondly, the MFESO with a recursive structure is applied to estimate the total disturbance of the PMSM position servo system to reduce its adverse effects. By compensating the estimation value of the disturbance to the FSMC scheme, a smaller control gain can be selected to obtain satisfactory anti-disturbance performance, thereby reducing the chattering. Furthermore, rigorous Lyapunov stability analysis demonstrates that the motor position and speed tracking error can converge to zero in fixed time. Finally, experimental results under different types of reference signals and disturbance verify that the proposed position control scheme delivers outstanding performance.

title: FlexSpec: Frozen Drafts Meet Evolving Targets in Edge-Cloud Collaborative LLM Speculative Decoding
authors: Yuchen Li, Rui Kong, Zhonghao Lyu, Qiyang Li, Xinran Chen, Hengyi Cai, Lingyong Yan, Shuaiqiang Wang, Jiashu Zhao, Guangxu Zhu, Linghe Kong, Guihai Chen, Haoyi Xiong, Dawei Yin
abstract: Deploying large language models (LLMs) in mobile and edge computing environments is constrained by limited on-device resources, scarce wireless bandwidth, and frequent model evolution. Although edge-cloud collaborative inference with speculative decoding (SD) can reduce end-to-end latency by executing a lightweight draft model at the edge and verifying it with a cloud-side target model, existing frameworks fundamentally rely on tight coupling between the two models. Consequently, repeated model synchronization introduces excessive communication overhead, increasing end-to-end latency, and ultimately limiting the scalability of SD in edge environments. To address these limitations, we propose FlexSpec, a communication-efficient collaborative inference framework tailored for evolving edge-cloud systems. The core design of FlexSpec is a shared-backbone architecture that allows a single and static edge-side draft model to remain compatible with a large family of evolving cloud-side target models. By decoupling edge deployment from cloud-side model updates, FlexSpec eliminates the need for edge-side retraining or repeated model downloads, substantially reducing communication and maintenance costs. Furthermore, to accommodate time-varying wireless conditions and heterogeneous device constraints, we develop a channel-aware adaptive speculation mechanism that dynamically adjusts the speculative draft length based on real-time channel state information and device energy budgets. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FlexSpec achieves superior performance compared to conventional SD approaches in terms of inference efficiency.

title: FedOTO: towards Low-Latency Drowsiness Detection Service in IoV via Structured Federated Pruning
authors: Yadi He, Lei Wang, Jia Xu, Ping Wang, Linfeng Liu
abstract: Drowsy driving continues to be a leading cause of motor vehicle accidents (MVAs), accounting for nearly 30% of all incidents according to recent studies. While numerous drowsiness detection methods have been developed, existing solutions often struggle with practical deployment due to limitations in detection accuracy, processing latency, or computational efficiency. To overcome these challenges, we propose federated only-train-once (FedOTO), a low-latency drowsiness detection framework for the internet of vehicles (IoV). FedOTO integrates edge computing with a novel two-tier structured federated pruning framework to deliver personalized, accurate, and real-time drowsiness detection services. By leveraging individual user data, local models on vehicular edge nodes are dynamically pruned and optimized to capture the individual drowsiness patterns. To reduce the processing latency, we employ zero-invariant groups (ZIGs), which identify computation-redundant parameter groups that can be safely pruned, and a hybrid efficient structured sparse optimizer (HESSO), which automatically trains local models and performs pruning, thereby minimizing local training time, communication delay, and response time. Additionally, our co-adaptive strategy dynamically adjusts both the number of redundant groups and learning rate across iterations, thus significantly accelerating the model convergence. Extensive experiments demonstrate Fe dOTO's superior performance, achieving an average accuracy of 0.9997 and an average F1 score of 0.9989. Moreover, compared to conventional federated learning, FedOTO reduces local training time by 30.7%, communication delay by 28.9%, and response time by 34.3% to achieve superior overall performance over state-of-the-art baselines.

title: A Planar Wide-Angle 16×32 MIMO Antenna Array for K-Band Radar
authors: Yury M. Meleshin, Ruslan N. Kolesnikov, Evgeniy M. Syedin, Vadim K. Tsvetkov, Vadim N. Karpov, Alexey L. Pereverzev
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents the design of a wide-angle antenna array for single-board K-band radars, in which metallic shielding structures limit the scanning angles. A stacked circularly polarized patch antenna element is proposed and integrated into a 16×32 MIMO radar operating in the 24.05–24.25 GHz band. Measurement results demonstrate that the antenna provides a half-power beamwidth of at least 120° in both principal planes while maintaining an axial ratio within the required limits over the entire scanning sector. The complete MIMO system exhibits inter-channel interference suppression better than 50 dB. These results demonstrate the feasibility of single-board architectures for high-performance wide-angle MIMO radars.

title: Integral Passivity-Based Control of Underactuated Mechanical Systems with Hysteretic Elasticity
authors: Enrico Franco, Fahim Shakib, Kaiwen Chen
abstract: This work investigates the passivity-based control of underactuated mechanical systems subject to matched disturbances described by the Bouc-Wen model. This model is widely used to characterize the nonlinear hysteretic elasticity observed in mechanical systems, and it does so by employing an internal state that is not measured. A new interconnection and damping-assignment passivity-based control design is presented for this specific scenario, and simulations on a ball-on-beam system demonstrate its effectiveness.

title: A Posteriori Second-Order Guarantees for Bolza Problems via Collocation
authors: Dongzhe Zheng, Wenjie Mei
abstract: Direct collocation for Bolza optimal control yields discrete Karush–Kuhn–Tucker (KKT) points, while practical solvers expose only discrete quantities such as primal-dual iterates, reduced Hessians, and Jacobians. This creates a gap between continuous second-order optimality theory and what can be certified from solver output. We develop an a posteriori certification framework that bridges this gap. Starting from a discrete KKT solution, we reconstruct piecewise polynomial state, control, and costate trajectories, evaluate residuals of the dynamics, boundary, and stationarity conditions, and derive a computable lower bound for the continuous second variation. The bound is expressed as the discrete reduced curvature minus explicit residual-dependent correction terms. A positive bound yields a sufficient certificate for continuous second-order sufficiency and provides quantitative information relevant to local growth and trust-region sizing. The constants entering the certification inequality are conservatively estimable from reconstructed discrete data. The resulting test is operationally verifiable from collocation outputs and naturally supports adaptive mesh refinement through residual decomposition. We also outline an extension to path inequalities with isolated transversal switches.

title: DIJIT: A Robotic Head for an Active Observer
authors: Mostafa Kamali Tabrizi, Mingshi Chi, Bir Bikram Dey, Kelly Yuan, Markus D. Solbach, Yiqian Liu, Michael Jenkin, John K. Tsotsos
abstract: We present DIJIT, a novel binocular robotic head expressly designed for mobile agents that behave as active observers. DIJIT's unique breadth of functionality enables active vision research and the study of human-like eye and head-neck motions, their interrelationships, and how each contributes to visual ability. DIJIT is also being used to explore the differences between how human vision employs eye/head movements to solve visual tasks and current computer vision methods. DIJIT's design features nine mechanical degrees of freedom, while the cameras and lenses provide an additional four optical degrees of freedom. The ranges and speeds of the mechanical design are comparable to human performance. DIJIT attains 85% of the peak human saccade speed. Our design includes the ranges of motion required for convergent stereo, namely, vergence, version, and cyclotorsion. Here, we present DIJIT and some aspects of its performance. We also present a novel method for saccadic camera movements, using a direct relationship between camera orientation and motor values. The resulting saccadic camera movements are close to human movements in terms of their accuracy, with 1.17<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> and 1.14<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> mean error for the left and right cameras, respectively.

title: Distributed Formation Control for Second-Order Nonlinear Multiagent Systems Using Predictor-Based Accelerated Fuzzy Learning
authors: Jinzhao Miao, Jinliang Liu, Lijuan Zha, Engang Tian, Chen Peng
abstract: This paper investigates the distributed formation control problem of second-order multiagent systems (MASs) subject to unknown nonlinear dynamics. A predictor-based fuzzy learning framework is developed, in which fuzzy logic systems (FLSs) approximate the unknown nonlinear dynamics and a two-layer learning mechanism accelerates weight adaptation by utilizing the velocity prediction error. A distributed observer is designed for jointly connected switching directed topologies to handle limited leader accessibility, allowing agents to estimate the global reference trajectory using solely local information. Furthermore, an actor–critic reinforcement learning architecture is integrated to realize distributed optimal formation control, in which the value function is reformulated with a compensatory term that exploits available system structural information, thereby enhancing policy optimization efficiency and accelerating convergence. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach exhibits improved robustness and accuracy in both dynamics approximation and position tracking when compared with conventional learning-based methods.

title: Differential Transparency and Excitability Functions for Cooperative Systems
authors: Yu Kawano, Arijit Sarkar, Jacquelien M.A. Scherpen
abstract: In this paper, we introduce novel concepts of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L\_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> differential transparency and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L\_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> differential excitability functions and analyze their properties in cooperative nonlinear systems. Specifically, we establish connections among the positive definiteness of the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L\_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> differential transparency (resp. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L\_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> differential excitability) function, transparency (resp. excitability), and stability of the associated variational systems, where transparency and excitability are weaker observability and reachability concepts compared to local observability and local accessibility, respectively. These <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> differential transparency and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L\_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> differential excitability functions can be computed numerically by simulation of cooperative systems and their variational system. We also provide convex approaches to estimating their upper or lower bound. Furthermore, we define the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> incremental transparency and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L\_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> incremental excitability functions, which are studied in terms of the differential energy functions. Finally, we apply the proposed differential energy functions to model reduction of cooperative systems by truncation and singular perturbation.

title: A Direct Ink Written LC-Type Vibration Sensor and Measurement Platform for Tremor Monitoring
authors: V N Kavyashree, J Sathya Sree, G. Idayachandran, E Manikandan, Nagaraj Sivasankaran
abstract: In this work, we proposed a LC-type vibration sensor on a flexible substrate for tremor detection. The sensor is developed using the Direct Ink Writer (DIW) technique. The fabricated sensor is characterized to analyze its performance. The proposed sensor achieves a resonant frequency of 2.02 GHz, with the corresponding loaded quality factor of 101, a reflection coefficient of −27 dB, and a transmission coefficient of −5 dB. We also proposed a measurement platform where the sensor is experimentally evaluated for tremor frequencies (2 Hz to 10 Hz). The time-domain gain and phase measurements are obtained, and it is used to derive the peak and RMS voltage values at various tremor frequencies. The sensor demonstrates a linear response over the desired frequency range, achieving a sensitivity of 0.0135 V/Hz with a maximum nonlinearity error of 0.121%. The Fast Fourier transform (FFT) analysis of the phase signal further confirmed the peak detection at tremor frequencies. Multiple-trial repeatability analysis yielded a low error of 0.31%, confirming stable and predictable sensor behavior ideal for tremor monitoring.

title: A Recursive-Total-Least-Squares Adaptive Filter-Based High-Precision Harmonic Suppression Method for High-Speed MLTMP Motor
authors: Gengkui Wei, Shiqiang Zheng, Zhuang Liu, Kun Mao, Haifeng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Current harmonics can increase motor losses in high-speed magnetic levitation turbo molecular pumps (MLTMPs) and also impair both the motor’s control performance and the vacuum performance of MLTMPs. Therefore, this paper proposes a high-precision harmonic decoupling suppression method applicable to high-speed motors. First, an adaptive filter based on the Recursive-Total-Least-Squares algorithm is designed to precisely extract harmonic characteristics. This algorithm accounts for input noise induced by position estimation errors and exhibits stronger robustness under low signal-to-noise ratio conditions. Then, the voltage harmonics generated by the current controller are accurately modeled and analyzed, and compensation for digital control delays is implemented. Additionally, a current harmonic regulator based on PI control and harmonic decoupling is designed to suppress current harmonics. Finally, the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method are verified on a high-speed MLTMP platform.

title: AI-Driven Inventory Forecasting POS System
authors: Aman Kumar, Shivendera Pratap Singh, G. Sakthi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Most small and medium-sized retailers struggle with overstocks and stockouts, and most rely on rigid rules and/or gut feeling for inventory decisions. This paper introduces BillWise, an AI-based point-of-sale (POS) system that incorporates demand forecasting into business operations. Within a microservices framework (Spring Boot for the POS backend and FastAPI the for ML service), BillWise creates a random forest regressor for each product based on historical sales and lagged feature sales. Forecasts are evaluated for each product and compared to the historical sales in the POS database using mean absolute error (MAE) and root mean squared error (RMSE), and reorder suggestions are automatically generated to the POS when predicted demand is greater than available inventory. BillWise uniquely integrates predictive analytics with operational analytics in inventory systems by storing demand forecasts in the operational system and automatically triggering actions based on the forecast. Results from deployments in SME sales showed that BillWise successfully issued the reorder alerts in a timely manner to prevent stockouts. The modular architecture offers the possibility for incremental systems integration with legacy POS systems, to illustrate an end-to-end POS system that is fully operational with AI forecasting to drive inventory decisions.

title: Understanding Untrained Deep Models for Inverse Problems [From the Guest Editors]: Algorithms and theory
authors: Ismail Alkhouri, Evan Bell, Avrajit Ghosh, Shijun Liang, Rongrong Wang, Saiprasad Ravishankar
abstract: In recent years, deep learning (DL) methods have been extensively developed for inverse imaging problems (IIPs), encompassing supervised, self-supervised, and generative approaches. Most of these methods require large numbers of labeled or unlabeled training data to learn effective models. However, in many practical applications, such as medical image reconstruction, extensive training datasets are often unavailable or limited. A significant milestone in addressing this challenge came in 2018 with the work of Ulyanov et al. <xref ref-type="bibr" rid="ref1" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</xref>, which introduced the deep image prior (DIP)—the first training-data-free convolutional neural network (CNN) method for IIPs. Unlike conventional DL approaches, DIP requires only a CNN, the noisy measurements, and a forward operator. By leveraging the implicit regularization of deep networks initialized with random noise, DIP can learn and restore image structures without relying on external datasets. However, a well-known limitation of DIP is its susceptibility to overfitting, primarily due to overparameterization of the network. In this tutorial article, we provide a comprehensive review of DIP, including a theoretical analysis of its training dynamics. We also categorize and discuss recent advancements in DIP-based methods that are aimed at mitigating overfitting, including techniques such as regularization, network reparameterization, and early stopping. Furthermore, we discuss approaches that combine DIP with pretrained neural networks (NNs), present empirical comparison results against data-centric methods, and highlight open research questions and future directions.

title: Simulating Emergency Teamwork Behavior with Multi-Agent LLMs: Reproducing Human Training Patterns Using Persona-Based Agents
authors: Atsushi Shimoda, Rei Kobori, Keito Takahashi, Zhang Yatong
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Emergency response teams such as Computer Security Incident Response Teams (CSIRTs) must operate under intense time pressure, rapidly evolving threats, and incomplete information. Team effectiveness in these environments depends strongly on the interaction among individual skill proficiency, leadership style, and collective coordination. Traditional human emergency training exercises are costly, difficult to repeat under controlled conditions, and limited in the diversity of team configurations that can be systematically examined. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) and multi-agent conversational frameworks raise a new question: Can LLM-driven multi-agent simulations reproduce human teamwork behavior observed in real emergency training? This study investigates the extent to which persona-based LLM agents can replicate behavioral patterns documented in a publicly available emergency response training dataset. We construct triad teams of LLM agents, assign personas reflecting human skill and leadership profiles, and expose them to an incident scenario parallel to that used in the human exercises. Each conversation log is evaluated using the same 11-item teamwork behavior rubric applied in the human study. Experimental results show that approximately 60% of the behavioral tendencies observed in human teams-such as skillbalanced coordination or follower-dominant communication— are reproduced by the LLM multi-agent simulations. These findings provide the first empirical evidence that LLM-based multi-agent simulations can partially approximate human teamwork behavior in emergency contexts. The proposed approach offers a low-cost and reproducible framework for analyzing team dynamics, exploring hypothetical team structures, and supporting digital-twin-based training tools for CSIRT and other emergency response organizations.

title: Experimental Demonstration of a High-Realized-Gain Electrically Small Antenna
authors: Alessio Tornese, Antonio Clemente, Christophe Delaveaud
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper discusses the design, optimization and experimental characterization of an electrically small antenna (ESA) with a realized gain of 6.3 dBi. The proposed solution is based on a parasitic array comprising two cosine-line dipoles. One dipole is excited differentially, while the other is loaded with a reactive impedance resulting from an ad-hoc optimization process based on spherical harmonics theory. Simulations and measurements show a very good agreement, confirming the accuracy of the numerical model and the effectiveness of the design methodology.

title: Visual Analysis of Artificial Intelligence, and Sustainable Investing: ESG Funds
authors: Durgesh Bahuguna, Rajesh Tiwari, Rakesh Kumar, Vidushi Negi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Even though a number of studies have been conducted on the subjects of Artificial intelligence (AI), sustainability, and ESG funds, especially over the years, more research is still needed to determine the advantages and interconnections between these fields. Through a bibliometric and systematic literature approach using the “R Language biblioshiny package,” “VOS-Viewer 1.6.20,” and the Scopus database, this study aims to determine the relationship and integration between artificial intelligence (AI), sustainability, and ESG funds. This research study disclosed various of findings. In terms of keywords, top five keywords having strongest links, are Artificial intelligence, ESG, Investment, Sustainability and Decision making. The top-quality research papers on the searching keywords were created by United Kingdom, UAE, and USA. The top-ranked universities that have published the highest number of literatures on selected keywords are the University of Symbiosis International Deemed University, Christ University, Xiamen University, and Bucharest University of Economic Studies. Top cited authors are Anita Gehlot, Neeraj Priyadarshi, Rajesh Singh, and Bhekisipho Twala, in the search of Artificial intelligence, sustainable, and ESG fund. The research article concluded by highlighting future research directions, constraints, and challenges.

title: Microplastic and Particulate Surveillance in Aquatic and Medical Fluids Using Portable Electrical Impedance Spectroscopy and Edge-AI
authors: Georgia Chris Selwyna P, Pooja M, Priyadharshini P, Tamil Selvan P, Vishnu Priyanga A R
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: The growing presence of microplastics in water systems and medical fluids is threatening ecosystems and public health. Current laboratory detection methods of microplastics and other types of micro particles have many limitations: cost, long turnaround time, and limited ability for onsite rapid sampling. In response to this, a new, inexpensive, portable field-based microplastic and particulate surveillance system has been designed with the following capabilities: electrical impedance spectroscopy (EIS), dielectric constant measurements, and an edge-AI classifier. A Raspberry Pi (RPi) based prototype has achieved up to 80% classification accuracy for distinguishing clean water from polyethylene (PE), polypropylene (PP), and sand particles, all in the size range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-100 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. The proposed system minimizes preparation completion time, and provides real-time inference, wireless connectivity between the RPi and a smartphone or tablet through Bluetooth, and has a Micro SD card for long-term data storage. The proposed system enables fast field deployment, will allow for environmental monitoring of water, will allow for water quality assurance during dialysis treatment, provide validation of pharmaceutical-grade water, and provide point-of-use testing in a clinic and will be a viable and affordable alternative to laboratory-based methods.

title: SFIWMark: Secure Fundus Image Watermarking Scheme for Smart Healthcare Applications
authors: Anurag Tiwari, Vijay Birchha, Vinay Kumar Srivastava
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The increasing volume of medical data shows significant challenges for different healthcare information management (HIM), particularly in the form of image security, its accessibility, authentication, reliability and its protections against different security threats. In the proposed work, a Fundus medical image watermarking (FMIW) scheme based on Redundant Discrete Wavelet Transform (RDWT) and Multiple Matrix Decomposition (MMD) is suggested in which additionally chaotic encryption of watermark (Wmark) image is done to further enhance the security. RDWT enhances the robustness against various types of attacks, while multiple matrix decomposition (MMD) improves the invisibility of Wmark along with the robustness against affine attacks. In this approach, a FUNDUS image of patient used as cover image, and the patient ID i.e. 'Aadhar' image is used as Wmark image. The 'Aadhar' image is divided into two halves, each of which is separately encrypted using a Henon map chaotic encryption scheme and used as a Wmark, thereby enhancing the overall security of the system. The proposed method achieves a Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) exceeding 40 dB, indicating high imperceptibility of the Wmark. The Wmark is later extracted and reconstructed by merging the two halves. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed technique outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, offering approximately 20% improvement in robustness against various attacks.

title: Predicting High Research Impact Universities Using Bibliometric Indicators: A Machine Learning Study
authors: Prasad Mali, Gurinder Singh, Shikha Kapoor, Vinita Sharma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: University research impact is commonly measured using bibliometric indicators based on citations; however most existing studies focus on using these indicators to analyse the present or past research outcomes, leaving a gap in the predictive use of these indicators. In this study we have addressed this limitation by employing a temporal prediction framework which predicts high research impact status in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{T}+\mathbf{1}$</tex> period based on bibliometric indicators from the period T. The CWTS Leiden Ranking Open Edition 2025 data of 2,831 universities was used for this purpose. This prediction task was formulated as a binary classification problem, and the performance of logistic regression was compared with ensemble-based machine learning models, including Random Forest and XGBoost. Results show that ensemble models achieve superior discrimination performance, with XGBoost attaining an ROC-AUC of approximately 0.87 and also achieving a better F1-score. Feature importance analysis shows that publication volume and international collaboration are the strongest predictors of future high research impact status. Overall, this study demonstrates the potential of ensemble-based machine learning methods for prediction of future university research impact using transparent bibliometric indicators.

title: Millimeter Wave MIMO of Dielectric Patch Antenna Elements
authors: Haohan Ding, Rawad Asfour, Muntadher Alsabah, Salam K Khamas
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of designing a compact multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) system for millimeter wave (mmWave) applications with a reduced mutual coupling, which is in line with the increasing demands for efficient miniaturized systems. A 4-dielectric patch antenna MIMO has been designed, focusing on optimizing the performance. Simulation results demonstrate ta reduced mutual coupling of circa -35 dB at 36 GHz in a 2-elemnts MIMO. This is in addition to meeting the requirement of key MIOM performance parameters such as envelope correlation coefficient, diversity gain, and channel capacity loss. This confirms the validity of the proposed design strategy for future high-density wireless systems.

title: Spatiotemporal Distribution Characteristics and Dispatchable Capacity Analysis of Electric Vehicles Based on Real-World Travel Data
authors: Yuxuan Huang, Weitao Wang, Guoshun Li, Shan Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The large-scale adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) helps reduce transportation-sector emissions, while the spatiotemporal variability of EV loads introduces new challenges to power system operations. This paper analyzes the spatiotemporal characteristics and dispatchable capacity of EVs. Information entropy quantifies the regularity of vehicle status and location. A SOM algorithm clusters parking probabilities over time to derive controllability indicators. Weighted K-means clusters parking locations and defines service regions to extract spatial features. The dispatchable capacity is estimated, and power boundaries for scheduling are derived. Results verify the method's effectiveness in capturing EV behavior and reducing complexity in group-level spatiotemporal modeling.

title: Explainable and Robust AI-Enhanced Secure Verification Using Adaptive Federated Learning and Theoretical Analysis
authors: Sivakumar Depuru, Nagooru Chandana, P. Alekhya, S. Madhu, Seela Venkata Chandana Devi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Modern secure verification systems are becoming more dependent on artificial intelligence to enhance both detection accuracy and automation, although the majority of systems today are unchanged and opaque and do not provide formal performance guarantees. To overcome the shortcomings, the research introduced a flexible and interpretable AIgrounded secure design that merges federated intelligence, trust-aware learning and theoretical authentication of privacypreserving verification settings. The suggested framework uses an adaptive learning process which dynamically is used to revise the model parameters amongst distributed nodes without breaching data confidentiality. To increase the transparency, an explainability module is also added to decode the rationale of decision, trust scores allowing the accountable and verifiable verification. Moreover, there are formal convergence and complexity analysis that introduces theoretical performance guarantees. A large scale of experiments on various benchmark data reveals that the proposed framework offers better verification performance with up to 96-98 percent accuracy and better robustness in adversarial environments and faster convergence than state of art approaches. The effectiveness and cross-dataset evaluations through ablation, scalability, and cross-dataset evaluations are also used to prove the effectiveness and generalization ability of the proposed system. Such findings confirm the framework as a trusted, scalable, and explicable platform of next-generation secure verification applications.

title: An AI-Driven Dual-Stage CyberShield Framework for Intelligent Threat Detection in Telecommunication Systems
authors: Prashant Roy, Kalyani Kasar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: As the telecommunication and data-driven network infrastructure has expanded rapidly, the quantity, nature, and complexity of cyber threats have grown significantly, making the rule-based and signature-based security measures ineffective in detecting threats in a timely and effective manner. In an attempt to deal with these difficulties, the present research introduces CyberShield, a smart two-step AIbased model of cybersecurity that involves anomaly identification, threat identification, and automatic response to cyberattacks in network traffic settings. The framework starts with an extensive preprocessing and feature engineering pipeline that transforms raw packetlevel traffic into refined representations with normalized numerical attributes, statistical descriptors, entropy, and temporal flow statistics. Stage 1 represents a hybrid HyFlow Sentinel model that combines statistical deviation checking, boundary-based learning and deep reconstruction to determine an anomalous network behavior with minimum false positives. The ThreatMap Classifier used in Stage 2 utilizes a dual-learning approach that involves ensembles of decision pathways and gradient-boosted modeling to classify identified anomalies into various types of cyberattacks. In order to achieve transparency and trust, a Temporal Multi-Path Integrated Gradients (TM-IG) explainability module is integrated to display feature-level contributions and how they vary over time within network flows. The framework also incorporates automated incident response and threat intelligence analysis in order to produce risk score and activate real-time mitigation responses. The proposed CyberShield model attains high accuracy, precision, recall, f1-score and Cohens kappa of 96.7%, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.1 \%, 96.9 \%, 97.0 \%$</tex> and 96.2% in threat classification.

title: Multimodal Learning Predicting Customer Engagement Across Social Platforms
authors: Deepali Singh, Anamika Chaturvedi, Jyothi B. Bhat, Lavanya S.V., Divya Verma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Customer engagement across social media platforms has become a critical indicator of brand visibility, customer loyalty, and digital marketing effectiveness. Social platforms generate highly diverse data streams, including text, images, videos, audio, and interaction metrics, which traditional singlemodal analytics struggle to interpret holistically. In response to this challenge, multimodal learning has emerged as a powerful machine learning paradigm capable of integrating heterogeneous data sources to produce more accurate and context-aware predictions. This study explores the application of multimodal learning models to predict customer engagement across social platforms by jointly analyzing textual content, visual features, and behavioral interaction signals. By combining natural language processing, computer vision, and engagement metadata, multimodal models capture richer representations of user responses than unimodal approaches. The research highlights how cross-modal feature fusion improves prediction of engagement outcomes such as likes, shares, comments, and viewing duration. Additionally, the study discusses practical considerations including platform heterogeneity, data synchronization, and model interpretability. The findings suggest that multimodal learning significantly enhances engagement prediction accuracy and provides actionable insights for content optimization and platform-specific strategy development. This research contributes to the growing field of social media analytics by demonstrating how integrated machine learning frameworks can better reflect the complexity of user behavior in contemporary digital ecosystems.

title: Cleaning and Imputing Multi-Source Renewable Data with Quartile-DBSCAN and Multi-Neighbor BPNN
authors: Hongchun Yao, Ming Tang, Fang Qin, Huang Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: This paper tackles challenges in multi-source renewable energy data processing, such as asynchrony, redundancy, anomalies, and missing values, hindering resource analysis and power prediction in power systems. We introduce an AI-integrated framework: cubic spline interpolation for synchronization, Quartile-DBSCAN for anomaly detection via statistical and density-based methods, and Multi-Neighbor BPNN for imputation using spatio-temporal correlations. Case studies on photovoltaic and wind datasets confirm efficacy, with low errors and improved quality, aiding forecasting and grid management.

title: Research on Quantitative Regulation Capability and Active Regulation Strategies of Source-Load-Storage Flexible Interactive Regulation Technology for Integrated Energy Systems in Agricultural Park Scenarios: Construction of Regulation Capability and Revenue Models
authors: Zhi Li, Yong Wang, Shilin Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: To address the issues of multi-energy interweaving, large load fluctuations, and low energy efficiency in traditional modes within the integrated energy system of agricultural parks, this paper focuses on the source-load-storage flexible interactive regulation technology and constructs a regulation capability and revenue model suitable for this scenario. Firstly, the core connotation is clarified by integrating distributed energy sources, adjustable loads, and energy storage devices to form a multienergy collaborative regulation system. Secondly, a quantitative index system including adjustable capacity and regulation rate is established, and specific calculation methods for each component are provided. Then, an “electricity-heat-cooling” bus-type topology structure and 11 types of key component models are constructed. Finally, an active regulation revenue model based on a decision tree is proposed to maximize revenue through hierarchical decision-making. The second part of this subsequent series of studies will focus on the active regulation and optimization strategies of the integrated energy system and verify the economic feasibility of the strategies through practical cases. This research provides theoretical support for the efficient operation and economic optimization of the integrated energy system in agricultural parks.

title: A Parasitically Coupled Antenna Array Design for GNSS Applications in CubeSats
authors: Ezequiel Marranghelli, Ramón López La Valle, Francisco Núñez
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This work presents a novel four-element microstrip antenna array for small CubeSats, operating in the GNSS L1/E1 bands. The design achieves high radiation efficiency and good polarization purity while remaining structurally simple. Its key innovation lies in the use of parasitically coupled elements extended from the ground plane, which are designed to fold during launch and deploy in orbit.

title: A Cost-Aware Stacking Ensemble Framework for Early Heart Disease Detection
authors: Jhansi B, T. Kamalakannan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Heart disease remains a leading cause of mortality worldwide, emphasizing the need for reliable and early screening mechanisms. Although machine learning techniques have been widely explored for heart disease prediction, many existing approaches primarily optimize overall accuracy and provide limited attention to misclassification cost asymmetry and interpretability, which are critical for clinical screening applications. This paper proposes a cost-aware stacking ensemble framework for early heart disease detection that integrates heterogeneous base classifiers, optimized decision thresholding, and explainable artificial intelligence techniques within a unified pipeline. Logistic regression, support vector machine, and random forest models are employed as base learners, and their outputs are combined using a logistic regression-based meta-learner. Cost-sensitive learning is incorporated to prioritize reducing false negatives, while the final classification threshold is optimized using receiver operating characteristic analysis. Model transparency is enhanced through Shapley value-based local explanations and permutation-based global feature importance analysis. Experimental evaluation on the UCI Heart Disease dataset using stratified cross-validation demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves an accuracy of 87.17%, a sensitivity of 0.92, a specificity of 0.84, and an area under the ROC curve of 0.87. The results indicate that the proposed approach provides a robust and interpretable decision-support system suitable for early heart disease screening.

title: Augmented Reality Training Systems for Law Enforcement: Design, Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Manisha Rai, Bobbinpreet Kaur, Raximov Rustamjon Xamidovich, Mirzaev Fayzulla, Fakhritdin Turdialiev, Ametova Aysulu Mnajatdinovna
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The paper describes an augmented reality (AR)based training model of law enforcement agencies that aims at boosting the operational readiness, quality of decisions and situational awareness using immersive and adaptive problems solving environments; the system is designed through the use of real time scenario simulation, the ability to dynamically adjust the level of difficulty, performance analytics and ethical protection, and experimental research indicates significantly better results compared to traditional training design in terms of accuracy, decision time, cognitive efficiency, and the engagement of the trainees, making <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A R$</tex> a principled and efficient solution to the contemporary limitations in policingrelated issues. In this research paper, the concept of the Augmented Reality Training Systems to the Law Enforcement is studied in terms of their design principles, and issues of implementation, and future possibilities. The study will attempt to give a comprehensive background to the researcher, system designers, and policymakers who want to take advantage of AR as a disruptive means to enhance law enforcement training, functional performance, and citizen confidence.

title: Deep Learning–Empowered Predictive and Diagnostic Analysis of Diabetic Retinopathy Using Fundus Imaging Biomarkers
authors: Julias Caeser Joy Bose, Md Rabiul Awal Shuvo, Md Ariful Islam, Abdul Hady Akash, Fabiha Faiz Mahi, Kamrun Naher Irin
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is one of the leading causes of preventable vision loss in the world and automated screening based on color fundus images can greatly decrease clinician workloads as well as enhance diagnostic coverage. Recent developments in deep learning have shown good potential of precise detection and grading of DR using fundus images but, sensitivity to changes in image quality and inability to provide interpretable results remain limiting factors to clinical acceptance and widespread implementation. This paper provides a multi-class fundus image classification deep learning-based framework that combines biomarker-conserving preprocessing and transfer learning. The suggested preprocessing pipeline consists of image resizing, contrastlimited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE) of the luminance channel and gamma correction of images to increase the number of diagnostically useful retinal structures. It also assessed three convolutional neural network structures, which were pretrained: DenseNet201, InceptionV3 and Mobile Net. A 12,000-balance dataset of fundus images of four classes (normal, diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma and cataract) was used in carrying out the experiments. The data was divided into training, validation and testing groups in the ratio of 80: 10: 10. Experimental findings prove that DenseNet201 performed the best with a test accuracy of 93.75 percent and a strong sensitivity in the detection of diabetic retinopathy, and then Mobile Net and InceptionV3 in terms of higher accuracy of 92 percent and 87 percent respectively. Moreover, a Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) explain ability pathway is described to visualize model focus on the retina of clinically significant regions, which helps in biomarker-based interpretability. The suggested framework explains the opportunities of precise and explainable deep learning systems in terms of automatic retinal disease screening and clinical decision support.

title: Frequency-Band Identification for Microwave NDT of CFRP-Foam-CFRP Panels Using S11/S21 Characterization
authors: David O. Briceño González, Daniel A. Cruz Mejía, Diego A. Tibaduiza Burgos
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This paper presents an experimental electromagnetic characterization of a CFRP-foam-CFRP sandwich panel representative of UAV structural components, as a preliminary step toward microwave nondestructive testing (NDT). Free-space <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{21}$</tex> measurements from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.8-8 \text{GHz}$</tex> were obtained under multiple antenna-panel configurations using broadband horn antennas and a vector network analyzer. The results show strong attenuation above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-4 \text{GHz}$</tex> due to the conductive CFRP skins, whereas the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.8-4 \text{GHz}$</tex> band preserves measurable transmission and stable phase information suitable for probing the dielectric core. By comparing free-space and through-panel responses, the frequency-dependent phase behavior and excess delay were extracted. The analysis identifies the low-GHz region as the operational band for microwave NDT and supports the feasibility of future imaging approaches in multilayer composite structures used in UAV and aerospace applications.

title: Personalized Drug Recommendation and E-Verification Using Machine Learning
authors: Polepalli Bhargavi, Kamireddy Snehalatha Reddy, Golla Ganesh, Jana Srividhya, Kota Venkateswara Reddy
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Accurate and safe prescription recommendation is essential for assisting physicians in complex clinical decisionmaking, yet existing systems like COGNET-AMO primarily focus on patient complaints and similarity-based matching, often neglecting critical safety information. To overcome this limitation, we present COGNET-AMO++, a context-aware prescription recommendation framework that incorporates comprehensive clinical data such as laboratory results, allergy histories, comorbidities, and medication profiles to build a holistic patient representation. The system integrates a drug-drug interaction prediction module to proactively identify and prevent harmful combinations, while an explanation layer clarifies the rationale behind each recommended drug, improving transparency and physician trust. Evaluated on the MIMIC-III dataset, COGNETAMO++ outperformed existing models in accuracy and generated prescriptions that were both safer and more clinically interpretable. These results highlight the potential of COGNET-AMO++ to become a reliable clinical decision support tool, enhancing personalized healthcare by making treatment recommendations more precise, comprehensible, and safety-aware. Experimental results on the MIMIC-III dataset demonstrate that COGNET-AMO++ improves F1-score and Jaccard similarity while significantly reducing the drug-drug interaction rate compared to existing methods.

title: Influence of Artificial Intelligence on Managerial Decision Systems and Trust Formation: A Mixed-Methods Study
authors: Jofia Jose Prakash, Dilpal Singh Chhabra, Aakash, Rohitha Gajam, Abhishek Bhushan Singhal
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The fast embedding of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into the organizational decisionmaking process has changed the essence of managerial thinking, power, and responsibility. Although AI-based decision support systems are associated with increased efficiency, predictive accuracy, and real-time analytics, they also create serious issues in terms of transparency, explainability, and the establishment of trust between managerial stakeholders. This research experimentally examines how AI-based managerial decision systems affect intensification of trust, perceived trustworthiness and intentions to adopt the systems, through a mixed methods research design. Structured instruments to measure system explainability, decision confidence, and algorithmic trust were used to gather quantitative data regarding 327 mid-level and senior managers in technology, finance, healthcare, and manufacturing sectors. The relationships were tested using structural equation modelling (SEM). The qualitative data of 24 semi-structured interviews formed the basis of qualitative information to put into perspective the cognitive and emotional processes of trust calibration through AI mediated settings. It is shown that the perceived explainability, the perceived procedural transparency, and the human-in-the-loop mechanisms are strong predictors of trust in the AI systems, which, in turn, mediate their effects on the decision acceptance and continuity in its use. The paper is relevant to the growing debate on reliable AI by providing an integrative model that brings together technical qualities and psychological and organizational aspects of the trust. The practical implications of the design of ethical, interpretable and manager-centric AI systems are addressed.

title: Dynamic EV Energy Exchange using On-Board Converters
authors: Monala Shreya, K Praveen, Jatoth Rahul, A Ananda Kumar, Karuppiah Natarajan, Hariharan Shanmugasundaram
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) charging has become one of the strategies to be considered as an efficient energy sharing of the electric vehicles (EVs) to ensure the reduction of range anxiety and the lack of strong dependence on the fixed charging stations. Nevertheless, the current V2V charging models are prone to several power conversion steps, higher power losses and large exterior interfaces. Traditional methods of V2V charging that are based on off-board units of power transfer tend to make systems more costly and bulky, and the on-board type-2 chargers are inefficient because of the multi/repetitive phases of power conversion. In order to address these constraints, this paper introduces a new V2V energy transfer method where direct battery-to-battery connection has been proposed, using type-2 AC charging input ports by making use of controlled switching arrangements. The proposed technique will have better power transfer efficiency by removing multiple conversion processes. The on-board converter is based on Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistors (IGBTs) to provide an efficient switching, less power waste, and additional energy flow control between vehicles. Moreover, there is a fuzzy logic control scheme that is used to dynamically control power transfer depending on the battery conditions of the involved EVs to achieve a fair and optimal energy transfer. The proposed system is analyzed on the basis of the MATLAB/Simulink simulations and evaluated in different V2V charging conditions, the feasibility of which is tested on the basis of a scaled experimental prototype. The findings indicate that IGBT based converters, optimization of power transfer topology and smart control are beneficial in enhancing V2V charging as these combined methods are promising and sustainable in future electric mobility implementation.
